title: P0: tour+reposition graph collapsed to ORIGIN-ONLY at the 01:41Z boundary — systems=[origin] reachable=false on every launch; fleet earner dark 70+ min
description: Verbatim evidence: tour-run-TORWIND-2C-99bf0dcf at X1-PD21 02:48Z: 'Pre-positioning verdict ... reachable=false systems=[X1-PD21]' + 'Reposition: no jump-reachable candidate system with cached market data from X1-PD21 - exiting honestly' + 'margins died (3 consecutive) after 0 productive tour(s)'. Same origin-only pattern from KA42, DP51, YP16, ZC66, PD21 since ~01:41Z (the kk61/ggk2/mtvg/ed4i deploy boundary) — before it, multi-system tours ran all evening. Treasury flatlined at that exact minute (Admiral's chart). Non-tour engines unaffected. SUSPECTS: mtvg dropped-candidates counter merge (touched candidate assembly + discovery-horizon logging) or ed4i look-back (same solver area), possibly an era-scoped gate-graph read regression (1ki5's DP51 finding = the same rot's reposition face, pre-dating by ~1h — maybe the rot arrived with the earlier optimizer-review merge instead). REQUEST: revert the suspect merge(s) or fix-forward at MAX priority; acceptance = one tour log showing systems=[origin, neighbor+] and a productive multi-system plan.
notes:
HARBORMASTER (00:15 local): P0 seen late — captain filed it at the exact minute of the 23:50 deploy; owned. STATE SPLIT: (1) ORIGIN-ONLY COLLAPSE = FIXED at 23:50 — post-deploy logs show systems=[6 systems] + reposition ranking with 3 preranked candidates (your acceptance line, live in daemon.log 00:03:32 TORWIND-2E). Collapse window was 01:41Z->02:50Z; onset boundary carried kk61/ggk2/mtvg/ed4i; restoration = 1ki5 durable-first discovery in the 02:50Z boundary. (2) REMAINING: heavies still earn ZERO — every plan since restart returns no_profitable_tour (all candidates solver-infeasible, ping-pong 2-waypoint best-plans look degenerate), coordinator relaunch-loops on 3m cooldowns. Suspects: TOUR_SOLVER_OBJECTIVE=rate flip (02:50Z — revert lever is unset env + kickstart), tranche-decay guards at 672k treasury (~400k allowance), or mtvg/ed4i solver-area merges. Fable diagnosis lane dispatched at MAX.
session died
LANE SPLIT (Admiral-ordered parallelization, 00:3x): leg A (tour-graph seam durable-first fix) = fable lane in worktree sp-4hl5; leg B (rate-vs-capital verdict) = separate opus VERDICT-ONLY lane running the 1wp8 replay harness both objectives x both capital levels (143k/250k tranches) — deliverable includes the minimum-feasible-treasury number for the captain. Parallel: sp-1pli relaunch-backoff (sonnet) to stop the dark-fleet API burn. Revert lever for the objective flip remains staged (unset env + kickstart), pending leg B.
FABLE DIAGNOSIS (sp-4hl5 remaining P0, 00:25 local): VERDICT = (a) REGRESSION, single defect, FIXED ON MAIN (2063c05, gated+merged, NOT deployed — harbormaster's).

ROOT CAUSE: planForState (run_tour_coordinator.go:1383) forwards the RAW ABSOLUTE working_capital_reserve into TourConstraints; the Python solver's money guard is spend_cap = max(0, max_spend − reserve) (tour_solver.py score_sequence). Under the dynamic budget path (max_spend 0 → 25% of live treasury) that subtracts the capital guard TWICE: spend_cap = max(0, 25%×T − 1,000,000) = 0 for any treasury < 4M. afford=0 kills every buy pairing → zero allocations → profit 0 → 'no profitable allocation under tranche decay/guards' on every candidate, every hull, every relaunch. All 9 tour containers carry working_capital_reserve=1,000,000 (containers table, verified).

WHY TONIGHT: ggk2 (9d1680a, deployed 01:41Z — the exact flatline minute) fixed the intValue int64 coercion so the [trade_fleet] 1M reserve REACHED live launches for the first time; before it the reserve silently collapsed to the 50k default, whose subtraction was harmless (spend_cap = 25%×T − 50k). The origin-only graph collapse (separate defect, fixed 02:50Z) masked it for 69 min by failing tours earlier in the pipeline. yqx4 (9b6ca24) fixed the same category error at BUY time (proportional floor via ctx) but never touched the planner constraint.

SUSPECTS CLEARED: (1) TOUR_SOLVER_OBJECTIVE=rate is EXONERATED for feasibility — mathematically selection-only (beam + scoring never see the objective; any allocation clears min_margin>=1 so profit>0 ⇒ cph>0 sorts first); keep the flip. (2) mtvg/ed4i: no money-path contact. (3) Market state: HEALTHY — live-snapshot solver runs (diagnose_sp4hl5.py, real solver + fresh PG rows) show 28–44k profit/tour (27–71k cph) at TORWIND-39/2E/2D positions with a 50k reserve; with 1M reserve: infeasible, exact live signature reproduced under BOTH objectives. Also proved: forwarding the yqx4 proportional floor (182k at T=456k) would STILL zero the planner (182k > 114k budget) — the fix has to decouple, not soften. Absorption ledger clean (0 PLANNED rows).

FIX (TDD, worktree sp-4hl5 → main 2063c05): dynamic path (cmd.MaxSpend==0) hands the planner reserve 0 (the 25% sizing + per-buy reserveHeadroom floor ARE the capital guard); explicit --max-spend keeps the original sp-1ek0 cash contract (reserve subtracted). Covers reposition preflight too (same planForState seam). Tests: run_tour_coordinator_planner_reserve_test.go (field values: T=456,270 → budget 114,067, reserve 1M → planner must see 0) + explicit-path contract pin. Full suite 2142 pass / 89 pkgs; captain-gate GatePassed+Merged, diffstat verified.

DEPLOY NOTE (harbormaster): Go daemon rebuild+restart only; routing service untouched; TOUR_SOLVER_OBJECTIVE stays. Acceptance: first post-deploy tour logs a feasible multi-leg plan (~28–51k projected at current prices, e.g. UU57-E21Z→ZC66-F12F) and treasury curve turns up. RESIDUAL: TORWIND-2C is stranded at gate-inaccessible X1-PD21 (genuine no-adjacency, honest exit) — fix does not recover that ONE hull, needs manual reposition/separate bead. Treasury bled 1.42M→456k in 24min post-restart (factories spending, tours dark) — expect recovery once heavies fly.
=== LEG B VERDICT (Fable diagnosis, read-only) — VERDICT (b): honest infeasibility, but NOT from the objective flip and NOT from the treasury level. ROOT CAUSE = a launch-config reserve regression. ===

MECHANISM. Tour containers restarted carrying working_capital_reserve=1,000,000 with max_spend=0 (->25% of live treasury ~132k at treasury 528,773). The solver computes spend_cap = max(0, max_spend - working_capital_reserve) = max(0, 132k - 1,000,000) = 0. A fresh empty-hold heavy can afford ZERO buy units (afford=(spend_cap-spend)//buy_price=0), so no arb pairing allocates; only held-cargo liquidation could contribute and the hull launches empty -> profit=0 -> no_profitable_tour. The raw reserve reaches the solver UNCLAMPED: run_tour_coordinator.go:1383 sets cons.WorkingCapitalReserve=reserve directly; the sp-yqx4 counter-cyclical floor only clamps buy-time reserveHeadroom and only when pct>0 (line 541), and the live config carries pct=0. (Note: container_ops_tour.go:88 comment claims pct=0 resolves to a 40% floor, but coordinator code only stamps the pct when pct>0 — comment/code mismatch; and even at 40% the floor never reaches the solver reserve.)

OBJECTIVE FLIP IS NOT THE CAUSE (rules out (a)). Feasibility is objective-invariant by construction: _sort_scored (tour_solver.py:145-160) is a final-sort only; the gate scored[0].profit<=0 (line 801) returns the same feasible/infeasible answer under both objectives because cph and profit share sign when seconds>0 (the replay harness asserts this at replay_objective.py:228). Empirically confirmed in EVERY matrix cell below: profit and rate give identical feasibility and identical/near-identical winning profit. UNSETTING TOUR_SOLVER_OBJECTIVE WILL NOT RESTORE EARNERS. The staged env revert is the wrong lever.

2x2 MATRIX (current snapshot 03:19Z, home X1-ZC66, hold 225, min_margin 1; profit-obj / rate-obj):
  reserve 1,000,000  max_spend 143k  (spend_cap 0)     -> INFEASIBLE / INFEASIBLE
  reserve 1,000,000  max_spend 250k  (spend_cap 0)     -> INFEASIBLE / INFEASIBLE
  reserve    50,000  max_spend 143k  (spend_cap 93k)   -> FEASIBLE +41,756 / +41,756
  reserve    50,000  max_spend 250k  (spend_cap 200k)  -> FEASIBLE +91,104 / +91,104
Same pattern for homes UU57, PA3 (+187,973 @143k / +360,843 @250k — richest), UQ87 (+17,367 / +37,215). The disambiguating variable the requested tranche-only 2x2 omitted is the RESERVE: at the live 1M reserve BOTH tranche levels are infeasible under BOTH objectives; drop reserve to 50k and BOTH become feasible and profitable — including at the CURRENT 143k tranche. So "profit-obj at 143k also finds nothing" = TRUE at reserve 1M (=> not objective), FALSE at reserve 50k (=> feasible).

"PING-PONG 2-WAYPOINT DEGENERATE BEST-PLAN" IS NORMAL, not a rate artifact. The genuine feasible winner IS a 2-waypoint arb (X1-UU57-E21Z -> X1-ZC66-F12F) under MAX_TOUR_SYSTEMS=2. The zero-profit ping-pong shells (F12F->D28E->F12F->D28E) are what the beam reports as top_rejected when spend_cap=0 forces every candidate to profit<=0 — objective-agnostic beam output, a symptom of zero budget.

FEASIBILITY THRESHOLDS (capital bisection, ZC66 & UU57, identical both objectives):
  - BROKEN reserve 1,000,000: feasible only when max_spend >= ~1,003,417 -> treasury >= ~4.0M. Absurd; fleet cannot climb there while dark. This is the deadlock.
  - CORRECT reserve 50,000: feasible when max_spend >= ~54,198 -> treasury >= ~217k. Current treasury is 528,773 (> 217k), so EARNERS RESTART IMMEDIATELY on a reserve fix — no waiting for treasury recovery.

TIMELINE (containers table, UTC). Reserve flipped 0 -> 1,000,000 at 2026-07-11 00:47Z and has persisted through every restart since (incl. the 02:50Z restart that fixed the origin-only collapse — that fix was durable-discovery, not the reserve). Profitable era (2026-07-10) ran reserve=0 (->50k default) with explicit max_spend 100k-600k. Transactions corroborate: PURCHASE_CARGO 729k @01:00Z then ZERO after ~01:41Z; residual liquidation sells only at 02:00Z; nothing at 03:00Z. So earner-dark = the reserve regression, independent of and outliving the discovery fix.

CORRECT ACTION (harbormaster executes; I did not). Restore the trade-fleet working-capital reserve to the 50k default: set the trade_fleet spec reserve (config key trade_fleet_reserve -> spec.WorkingCapitalReserve -> container working_capital_reserve) back to 0 (->50k) or an explicit 50000, and/or restore an explicit max_spend, then kickstart/relaunch the trade fleet so containers rebuild with the corrected config. Do NOT unset TOUR_SOLVER_OBJECTIVE for this — it is orthogonal (though reverting it is harmless if desired for other reasons). Earners resume at the current 528,773 treasury once the reserve is fixed.

LATENT CODE GAP (file a follow-up bead; do NOT build now). The solver reserve is passed raw into spend_cap=max(0, max_spend-reserve) with no clamp against max_spend and no distinct infeasible_reason — a reserve >= max_spend silently zeroes the budget and reports the generic "no_profitable_tour / no profitable allocation under tranche decay/guards", which is exactly why this masqueraded as market/objective death for 70+ min. Defensive fix: (a) clamp the solver's effective reserve below max_spend (or apply the sp-yqx4 counter-cyclical floor to the SOLVER reserve, not just buy-time); and/or (b) emit infeasible_reason="reserve_exceeds_budget (spend_cap=0)" when max_spend-reserve<=0 so the next occurrence is one-line diagnosable. Also fix the container_ops_tour.go:88 comment/code mismatch on the pct=0 -> 40% claim.

METHOD: reused replay_objective.py reconstruction (same 75-min staleness, CRUISE travel, GoodListing orientation) driving solve_tour directly across objective x max_spend x reserve. Routing service confirmed healthy (routing.log live, model 2@torwind-2026-07-05 matches artifact, no version mismatch). No files edited, nothing deployed/restarted.
LEG A DEPLOYED 00:27 local (2063c05: dynamic-budget tours hand planner reserve 0 — the buy-time reserveHeadroom floor remains THE capital guard, RULINGS #4 enforcement seam untouched; explicit --max-spend keeps the original contract). Daemon rebuilt+kickstarted; routing untouched (rate objective stays — EXONERATED by leg A evidence: selection-only, market healthy 28-44k/tour with sane reserve on live snapshot). Watcher armed for first feasible plan + treasury upturn. Root-cause chain for the record: ggk2 int64 fix (01:41Z boundary) made the 1M reserve REACH the planner for the first time -> spend_cap=max(0, 25%%xT - 1M)=0 below 4M treasury -> deterministic fleet-wide infeasibility, masked until the origin-only graph fix landed.
LIVE ACCEPTANCE MET (00:27-00:30): zero no-profitable lines since restart; 5 Tour planned lines — 42:+19,533(2 legs), 2E:+17,365(2), 2D:+35,682(3), all model-stamped. Captain flagged (st-wisp-wicdb) with the sells-resume + treasury-up read; their close validates. Leg-B verdict lane wound down — superseded by leg-A root cause + live confirmation (rate objective exonerated, flip stays). Residuals: stranded 2C@PD21 (ops extraction ask in flag + engineering detector bead), sp-1pli backoff in build.
LEG-B VERDICT (independent, converged with leg A): same mechanism (reserve reaches solver unclamped -> spend_cap=0 below 4M treasury), objective EXONERATED with a 2x2 (feasibility objective-invariant; identical profits both objectives); ping-pong shapes = normal beam output under spend_cap=0, not a rate artifact. Timeline reconciliation: containers table shows reserve flipped 0->1,000,000 at 00:47Z (captain re-man), became EFFECTIVE at the 01:41Z ggk2 deploy — clock-independent, compatible with leg A. Thresholds (superseded by the code fix but banked): broken shape needed treasury >=4M; correct shape >=217k. Leg-B recommended a config revert to 50k reserve — NOT taken: 2063c05 (deployed) decouples the planner from the reserve while KEEPING the 1M buy-time enforcement, which is the captains intended standing guard. Follow-ups filed from leg-B findings: pct-stamp gap verify + solver infeasible_reason/clamp. POST-FIX FULL-CHAIN ACCEPTANCE: 03:31Z tour PURCHASE_CARGO 149,328 executed + first realized telemetry leg + sells landing (+68.8k/+69.5k) — plans, buys, legs, revenue all live.

title: HANDOFF (command change, Admiral-ordered demotion 01:45Z): fleet secured self-running — 9 heavies + 11 cheap chains + coordinators all autonomous; floors pending ggk2/kk61; full failure record inside
description: == WHY THIS HANDOFF == The Admiral demoted the sitting captain after three treasury near-stalls in one night (53k / 82k / 186k troughs). The failure record, owned in full, is on sp-59xl notes (postmortem + tonight's chain): (1) accepted the trade-coordinator deploy by observing behavior instead of reading guard parameters — 7 tours flew 2h on 50k floors; (2) acknowledged an unsafe propagation window with no tripwire armed — the Admiral was the balance monitor twice; (3) blanket-unfroze all 16 chains on treasury-recovery pressure INCLUDING the known-expensive-tranche chains (ADV_CIRC buys 1M+ microprocessor tranches; LAB/EQUIPMENT 250-450k electronics tranches) against a 50k floor — three more sub-200k troughs. The class errors: verify-at-enforcement-point, tripwire-the-known-gap, never blanket-revert a surgical posture. == STATE AS SECURED == Treasury ~186k recovering on sells (fleet inventory ~4-5M liquidating continuously; NO money was ever lost — every trough was working capital in legal buys). RUNNING autonomous: 9 heavy tours (trade coordinator self-relaunching, dynamic caps, reserve config 1M but see sp-j4yv/ggk2 — enforcement gap under hot fix), 11 cheap-input chains (FOOD/CLOTHING/FABRICS/SHIP_PARTS/MIL_EQ/MEDICINE — tranches 100-450k max), contract fleet + T1 frigate, stocker portfolio mode, 305-slot dual warehouse, frontier + scout coordinators, 34 systems priced. STOPPED pending kk61 floor: ADV_CIRC (1M tranches), LAB x2, EQUIPMENT x2 — relaunch list on sp-59xl. == PENDING GATES (relaunch/verify on each flag) == ggk2 (tour floor enforcement — HOT), kk61 (factory floor config — HOT; unfreezes the 5 stopped chains), f5pr (worker ferry), ed4i (deadhead loading), 1wp8 ($/hr objective, fable), mepj (long-haul exotic capture — ADMIRAL RULING pending, counter sizing the leak), 5q2c/zhii-follow-ons. Era-close LAST-BUY cutoff armed T-2.5h (sentinel watch). Wake tripwire credits-below 200k armed. == VERIFICATION DOCTRINE FOR MY SUCCESSOR == read sp-59xl notes end to end + memories captain-money-deploy-acceptance, captain-worker-abundance, captain-fleet-hands-runbook-protocol. Money-touching deploys: verify the ENFORCEMENT read, not the config row.
notes:
WATCH LOG (01:50Z): kk61+ggk2 BOTH LIVE — all sub-1M spend surfaces now closed ENGINE-SIDE (factory floor via [manufacturing] config key; tour floor via the int64 fix; the 01:32-01:37 drain was factory pipelines RESPAWNING past my freeze — the engine floor closes that hole too, my container-stops never could). Runbook item executed per the pre-declared list: LAB x2 + EQUIPMENT x2 relaunched, VERIFIED carrying reserve=1000000 in running config (the enforcement-point read this time). ADV_CIRC held: its 1M+ input tranches vs the 1M floor = permanent skip until treasury >2M — relaunching it now would just spin; rides treasury recovery. D39 microprocessor ask-ladder economics (each buy +26k/u) flagged to analyst as advisory question. Fleet: 15 chains + 9 heavies + all coordinators running, everything floored, tripwire 150k armed. Treasury recovering on sells.
TERMINATED by Admiral order 01:55Z — command ended. Fleet at termination: fully autonomous and engine-floored (9 heavies/trade-coordinator, 15 chains/1M floor, contracts+frigate, stocker+305-slot warehouse, frontier+scout coordinators, tripwire 150k, era-close cutoff armed T-2.5h). Successor: this bead + sp-59xl notes are the complete record. No session actions after this note.
REHIRED — command resumed by Admiral order (02:00Z). Succession bead stays as the failure record; operational log returns to sp-59xl. Debt acknowledged: prove-out via treasury recovery + the 5M/hr program under verified floors.

title: P0: hull TORWIND-24 CORRUPTED to 0/0 fuel + 0/0 cargo (hauler→probe-stats, xxhn-class) AND coordinator spawn-loops workers on it with NO backoff (4 spawns/9s, contract chain fully stalled 30+min — income.stalled fired correctly)
description: 08:58Z: contract coordinator persisted 4 worker containers for TORWIND-24 within 9 seconds, each dying instantly ('deliveries not complete' — a 0-cargo hull can't deliver). 24's server/daemon state: Fuel 0/0, Cargo 0/0 — a LIGHT_HAULER bought at 06:0xZ degraded to probe-stats (the xxhn conversion-corruption family; note ship-list showed it as SATELLITE role). Chain was DOWN 30+ min (0 fulfillments; income.stalled detector caught it — good). Captain mitigation: unpinned 24 → coordinator selects healthy hulls. TWO FIXES: (a) worker-spawn needs backoff + a poison-hull quarantine (N instant-deaths → skip hull + event); (b) the 0/0 corruption root — what converts a hauler's stats? (check 24's history: purchased ~06:00, pinned contract, worked normally ~2h, then 0/0). Check 25 same-batch.
acceptance_criteria: Spawn storms impossible (backoff+quarantine); 24 restored or written off with cause named; no recurrence on same-batch hulls.
notes: DEPLOYED 3cd585a (pid 7470, verified 6 files +803/-12; roster-diff clean 14/0/5). ROOT CAUSE confirmed: general pool already excluded 0-cargo; the DEDICATED pool's deliberate 'dedication is authorization' exemption was the hole the mispinned probes fell through. Fix A: CargoCapacityPolicy on FindIdleShipsByFleet — coordinator opts in RequireCargoCapacity, idle-arb calls byte-identical, exclusions logged+counted. Fix B: spawnGovernor (instant-death=30s signature; backoff 5/15/45s; quarantine at 3 in a 10m window, edge-triggered interrupt-class coordinator.error_loop event naming the hull; per-run in-memory by documented design). RULINGS #1: defer-never-decline — healthy hull selected immediately; all-held → park+re-check, income.stalled backstops. Pin-time warn deliberately not built (fleet-agnostic handler; scouts legitimately 0-cargo; selection-side neutralizes mispins) — not filing, captain census discipline is the process fix. HOLD for captain acceptance: spawn storms impossible + no recurrence on same-batch hulls.
close_reason: Captain WRITTEN ACCEPT (st-wisp-o733): spawn guard live — no storms since deploy, 24/25 back in the scout pool, no same-batch recurrence.

title: P1: 1z2h cross-gate sourcing selects cross-system sources the worker CANNOT reach — 'waypoint X1-PA3-FB6A not found in cache for system X1-KA42' — 6 contract.failed + 7 container.crashed in one hour
description: 01:35Z coordinator: 'Worker for TORWIND-3 failed: failed to navigate to market: waypoint X1-PA3-FB6A not found in cache for system X1-KA42. Please sync waypoints from API'. The sourcing optimizer (1z2h, now wired) selects the cheapest source ACROSS systems (PA3 fresh from 18's sweep), but the contract worker's navigate is in-system-only + the waypoint cache lookup is scoped to the ship's current system. Result: repeated worker crashes (6 contract.failed/7 container.crashed this hour), throughput tax on any contract whose best source is cross-system. No money lost (pre-buy failure). FIX options: (a) sourcing optimizer constrained to REACHABLE sources (same-system until workers use the BFS travel()), or (b) contract workers adopt shared travel() for cross-system sourcing legs (the full feature). (a) is the stopgap, (b) the feature — ship (a) now.
acceptance_criteria: Zero 'not found in cache' worker failures across 20 cycles; cross-system sources either flown correctly or excluded from selection.
notes: DEPLOYED d950c05 (daemon pid 54053, 2026-07-09 22:54): SystemReachability predicate on the sourcing optimizer — nil = in-system-only (fail closed), both contract call sites pass nil permanently per RULINGS #14. Verified numstat 4 files +204/-45 incl 117 test lines. Smoke: contract-work-TORWIND-11 re-adopted instantly, route planned+flying, zero 'waypoint not found in cache'. Cross-system feature CANCELLED per Admiral (single-system is the final design). HOLD for captain acceptance: zero unreachable-source failures over a full contract cycle.
close_reason: ACCEPTED formal (03:00Z): 140-failure-pair spam → flatline within one minute of d950c05; multiple clean contract cycles since, zero waypoint-not-found. Ruling recorded: contracts single-system permanently, pre-positioning is the P2 path.

title: P1: travel() plans single-jump only — no multi-hop gate pathing (KA42 gate has NO JP61 edge; route needed KA42→GQ92→JP61; frigate crashed laden at the home gate)
description: 23:24Z, arb-run-TORWIND-1-cc8a9136 (lab C37→JP61-XZ7D): bought at C37, flew to home gate, then 'jump TORWIND-1 to X1-JP61 failed: no jump gate connection from origin gate' ×4 → crashed honestly (good), ship released laden. KNOWN TOPOLOGY (empirical tonight): KA42↔GQ92 (proven many times), NK36↔GQ92 (proven), KA42↔PA3 (18's jump), GQ92↔JP61 (13 toured JP61; verify). travel() must build a JUMP PATH over the gate graph (BFS over gate edges) with cooldown waits between hops, not assume origin→dest is one edge. Where IS the gate adjacency stored? (my psql sweep found no edges table — system_graphs is intra-system only; the daemon validates connections so it has the data somewhere — expose it, and consider a 'route preview' in arb-run pre-flight so an unroutable dest refuses BEFORE buying (the pre-flight bought first, then discovered no route — inverse order).) Folds under sp-7yej invariant #1 (never exit laden) + a new pre-flight rule: ROUTABILITY CHECK BEFORE SPEND.
acceptance_criteria: arb-run home→JP61 completes via a 2-jump path with cooldown waits; an unroutable --sell-at refuses pre-buy with a clear message.
notes: FRONTIER CENSUS for the sweep chain (captain ask st-wisp-yjan): 10 OPEN (built-gate) unswept systems, by hub — via GQ92: HU21, PD21. via JP61: YP16, XT71, FQ55, CK44. via NK36: KN67. via PA3: ZC66. via ZC66: UU57. via KM70: XX56. (Under-construction excluded: AF2, QJ93, BG81, YZ87, UQ87, GU51.) DP51 trio chains naturally onward via KM70→{PD21, XX56} or GQ92→{HU21, PD21}.

title: P0: factory MAKE-stage sells output at the BUY market (guard planned sink A1@5,248; worker dumped 80u MEDICINE at D39 ~1,600-1,800 then re-bought) — guard-vs-execution sink divergence
description: 21:28-21:33Z, goods_factory-MEDICINE-9244bfa1, worker TORWIND-12. Guard-1 cleared the chain with 'make MEDICINE bid5248-ask3000' — sink A1. EXECUTION: 12 accumulated MEDICINE at D39 across cycles (buys −59,160/−63,580/−73,760/−62,460/−65,480), then SOLD 4×~20u AT D39 ITSELF (+36,880/+34,600/+32,740/+31,240 — laddering the bid DOWN from ~1,844 to ~1,562/u vs ~3,100-3,300 cost) and immediately re-bought. Net medicine cash flow −258,160; 12 left holding 40 MEDICINE + 40 POLY. Pattern reads as make-room-dump (hold-full → sell locally to buy again) or sell-stage using current-market instead of the guard's chosen sink. FEED legs were all profitable (+~130k fabrics/poly) — the defect is ONLY the output sell-leg routing. Captain: factory stopped, hand-flying 12→A1 to sell the held 40 (+~210k recovers the position to ~breakeven-or-better). FIX: (a) the sell step must target the guard's sink waypoint (A1), never the current market; (b) NEVER sell output at a waypoint whose bid < basis (a one-line sanity floor: sell only if bid ≥ ask-paid × margin-min); (c) if hold-full forces a decision, FLY THE SELL LEG, don't dump. This is guard-vs-execution divergence — the guard's plan must BIND execution, or the guard is decorative.
acceptance_criteria: A MEDICINE factory cycle sells ONLY at the guard's chosen sink; zero SELL_CARGO rows at the lift's buy market; realized sell price within 20% of the guard's quoted sink bid.
notes:
POSITION RECOVERED (21:52Z): 40 held MEDICINE sold at A1 +209,060 (5,226/u — the guard's quoted sink, hand-flown). Episode net ≈ +80k (feeds +130k, recovery +209k, churn −258k) — the guard-era failure mode ends PROFITABLE. 12 retains 40 POLY as feed stock for the relaunch. Factories stay OFF until your flag; MEDICINE remains #1 relaunch candidate (chain economics proven, only the sell-leg routing was broken).
MERGED-VERIFIED 6fd308a + DEPLOYED pid 59048 (restart carried rqwm+1z2h-P1+ku8e together; tgp5 5-for-5 instant resume). title: P0: arb-run RETRY LOOP RE-BUYS — 3× purchases blew through --max-spend (118,872 vs 40k cap), then false success=true with 36 units stranded (source-side waypoint→gate hop missing = vzxu fix is dest-side only)

title: P0: goods-factory coordinator nil-pointer PANICS THE DAEMON when claiming ships - fleet-wide outage, 3 panics

Trigger: goods_factory-FAB_MATS-06e30318 (vmrj build) starting parallel workers 'BUY IRON using TORWIND-3' + 'BUY QUARTZ_SAND using TORWIND-6' - ships likely coordinator-owned/nil-assignment at claim -> nil deref. The factory constructor was the LONE coordinator missing the 'if clock==nil { clock=NewRealClock() }' guard that every sibling has (parallel_manufacturing:123, scouting, scout_markets); main.go:449 passes nil expecting substitution -> h.clock stayed nil -> clock.Now() at ship.go:444 SIGSEGV'd. title: REGRESSION (sk68 D2): past next_wake_at is level-triggered — supervisor fires a wake EVERY TICK until captain re-declares; burned the 8/h cap
description: Live incident 2026-07-07 06:43-06:47 (Admiral-observed: 'heartbeat every few seconds'). After sk68 deploy (c4b90dc), a declared next_wake_at that passes without being re-declared becomes a STANDING wake condition: every 30s tick evaluates now>=next_wake_at -> wake, again and again — 8 wakes in ~4 min until max_sessions_per_hour capped it, then 'session cap reached' spam every tick. Root cause: D2 removed the suppression of overdue alarms but nothing CONSUMES the alarm after it fires. FIX (one-shot semantics): in evaluateWakeGate, the declared next_wake_at triggers only while UNSERVICED — fire iff next_wake_at > last_session (a wake after the alarm services it); once last_session >= next_wake_at, fall back to heartbeat cadence (last_session + HeartbeatMinutes). Test matrix: alarm future -> no wake; alarm past + last_session < alarm -> wake ONCE; after that wake (last_session >= alarm) -> NO further alarm wakes, heartbeat resumes; overdue-heartbeat protection (D2's point) still holds when NO alarm is declared. Also: rate-limit the 'session cap reached' log line (once per cap window, not per tick). MITIGATED live at 06:5x by emergency re-declare (+45m, thresholds restored to 3.4M/500k — captain had briefly had below=800k/no-above; inform captain). This is MY regression from tonight's sk68 merge; same Admiral order covers the repair.
notes: session died
close_reason: merged a758735, DEPLOYED: captain rebuilt, supervisor kickstarted pid 9662, VERIFIED QUIET (0 log lines across 2+ ticks; no spurious wakes). Root fix: next_wake_at is a ONE-SHOT alarm — governs the gate only while unserviced (*NextWakeAt > LastSession); once a session runs at/after the alarm it is spent and heartbeat cadence resumes (D2 anchor semantics preserved: anchor = later(LastSession, DeclaredAt); no-alarm overdue heartbeat still wakes). TestWakeGateNextWakeAtIsOneShot RED-proven against the regression; cap log rate-limited (TestCapReachedLogIsRateLimitedAcrossConsecutiveTicks). Incident timeline: detected 06:47 (Admiral report), mitigated 06:50 (emergency re-declare), root-fixed+deployed+verified by 07:0x. Damage: 8 max-effort captain turns (hourly cap contained it). Post-deploy: captain re-declared its own policy (next wake 07:20) — full loop healthy.

title: Consult (Admiral-directed): FULL review of the factory operation — design + architecture. What can be made better? Too complex or too simple? Deliverable: a ranked redesign brief
description: ADMIRAL ORDER (13:00Z): review the ENTIRE factory operation design and architecture, not just sourcing. SCOPE FOR THE ANALYST: (1) ARCHITECTURE AS-BUILT vs AS-DESIGNED — original docs (gobot/docs/GOODS_FACTORY_GAP_ANALYSIS.md 2025-11-22, GOODS_FACTORY_DYNAMIC_SHIP_DISCOVERY.md, supply_chain_resolver.go, run_factory_coordinator.go, the parallel task coordinator) vs what actually runs: per-good coordinators + shared worker pool + one-work-unit rotation + demand-gated harvest + tour-realized margins. (2) THE ERA'S EVIDENCE FILE, all on beads: sourcing blowups all from SCARCE sources (wedx ruling — supply-first restoration already spec'd, a5j7); iv65 price-ceiling failed on poisoned baseline; worker P&L thin (~72k/hr best) while tour-side realization ~6.6M/hr gross flowed through factory goods (8w40); export-ask-subsidy signal (tours paying premium at OWN markets = rest signal); chain rotation works engine-side; era-1 lesson: capacity-without-workers. (3) THE QUESTIONS: is the design too complex (16 chains, recursive dependency trees, dual coordinator designs, fabricate ladders) or too simple (no chain-level P&L accounting, no supply awareness at buy time, no per-chain kill-switch on realized margin, value realization unmeasured because it flows through tours)? What single change most improves fleet $/hr? Should chains be FEWER and deeper (top-3 goods only) or broader? Should factory output feed TOURS explicitly (planner-visible stock) instead of passive export markets? (4) DELIVERABLE: ranked redesign brief — keep/kill/change per mechanism, sized by expected $/hr impact and build cost; becomes the era-3 factory doctrine and the shipwright's build list. Era context: T-24h, treasury cycling low, factories currently 14 chains running (2 furnaces dead pending a5j7).
notes:
ARCHAEOLOGY DATUM for your review (from shipwright, file:line): the original supply-first ranking is ALIVE at market_locator.go:254 (FindExportMarketBySupplyPriority — ABUNDANT>HIGH, skips SCARCE/LIMITED, doc comment cites 43cr vs 6,863cr cost data) — buyGood at production_executor.go:286 simply calls the price-first sibling FindExportMarket instead, while LOGGING the supply data it ignores. The class was fixed once (b469484, 2025-12-02) scoped to RAW materials only; fabricated inputs kept price-first — the ADV_CIRC path. So the architecture question sharpens: the design was right, implemented, and then bypassed in one call site for half the input space — a review finding about DRIFT CONTROL as much as design (how does a one-line selector swap in a money path escape notice for 7 months? the dangerous function keeps its innocent name). a5j7 now carries the restore + poison-proof baseline; furnaces dead until it deploys.
TRADE-ANALYST: FULL FACTORY OPERATION REVIEW — RANKED REDESIGN BRIEF (sp-hzz5, Admiral-directed) — 2026-07-11 ~13:10Z
Evidence spine: sp-wedx (sourcing incidents), sp-8w40 (erosion + attribution), sp-v8ha (tour-feed values), sp-naw6 (raw-input economics), sp-spen (warehouse/stock model), the trade audits, and the design docs (GOODS_FACTORY_GAP_ANALYSIS.md verified).

## THE VERDICT ON "TOO COMPLEX OR TOO SIMPLE": BOTH — in exactly opposite places.
**The ACQUISITION side is over-built; the REALIZATION side is under-built.** The design invests its complexity in recursive input trees and dual coordinators — where the era's ledger says almost no money lives (raw inputs = 0.29% of spend, market-buy permanently correct per naw6). Meanwhile the side where ALL the money lives — output realization, which flows through tours at ~6.6M/hr gross — has NO stock model, NO chain-level P&L, NO kill-switch, and is discovered by tours only via market-cache side-effects. Workers show ~72k/hr while their chains' goods gross 6.6M/hr through tours: the value is real but UNMEASURED AND UNMANAGED at the chain level. The redesign moves complexity from input trees to output realization.

## KEEP (proven, cheap, load-bearing):
K1. **In-system chain rule + make-led operation** — simple, correct, guard-verifiable (Guard-1 caught biocomposites).
K2. **Shared worker pool + rotation** — THE anti-erosion mechanism (chains sustain ~1 visit/1.5h recovery; a camper grinds its chain AND its own P&L to zero — 8w40, measured). Engine-side rotation works; keep it.
K3. **Demand-gated harvest** — only lift what has a sink; prevents inventory rot.
K4. **Supply-first sourcing** (a5j7 spec, already ruled at sp-wedx) — the leading-indicator fix for every blowup this era.
K5. **Launch-time chain-margin guard (2dv4)** — refused candidates cost zero; keep the boldness-with-verification pattern.

## KILL (complexity without money):
X1. **Recursive fabricate ladders beyond depth-1.** Raw inputs are 0.29% of buys; naw6 ruled market-buy permanently correct with named flip conditions that have never fired across 397 markets. Depth-1 (buy inputs at market, lift output) captures everything; fabricate-the-inputs-of-inputs is dead weight in code, API calls, and failure surface. The furnace class lived here.
X2. **One of the two coordinator designs.** One job, one design — whichever the shipwright designates legacy dies. Dual designs are a maintenance tax with zero $/hr.
X3. **Standing tail chains under ~50k/hr feed value** (micro/machinery/jewelry/plastics class). The tour book says 5 goods carry 80%+ of factory-good revenue. Tail goods stay as opportunistic arb legs, not staffed chains.
X4. **iv65 trailing-SELF-median ceiling as primary protection** — demote AND fix: it failed on a poisoned baseline (a ladder drags its own trailing median up behind it, so the ceiling chases the ladder). Backstop becomes 1.5x the median ask of ELIGIBLE (healthy-supply) sources cross-market — a baseline the ladder cannot poison.

## CHANGE (ranked by expected $/hr x build cost):
C1. **PLANNER-VISIBLE STOCK — the headline redesign (+300-600k/hr, build: medium).** Unify factory output with the warehouse/pre-positioning model: a factory IS a stocker whose acquisition method is 'lift cheap local production' instead of 'haul cheap cross-system goods.' Factory lifts DEPOSIT into warehouse stock with cost basis recorded; the tour solver sees stock as a reservable zero-ask source at basis (the dchv chain already proved deposit -> zero-ask withdrawal end-to-end); contracts already withdraw. This closes the leak where tours discover factory output only through the market cache and BUY IT AT THE LADDERED ASK — the export-ask-subsidy inversion (tours paying a premium at our own markets, the 8w40 signal) becomes structurally impossible: stock withdraws at basis. One model replaces two (factory-output handling + warehouse) — this is the change that REDUCES architecture while adding the most money.
C2. **CHAIN P&L LEDGER + KILL-SWITCH (+100-200k/hr, build: small).** Per-chain realized = stock withdrawals at market value − input costs − lift costs (my psql attribution is the working prototype). Auto-pause any chain under 30k/hr realized over a 6h window. The portfolio becomes self-pruning; today's hidden losers survive precisely because value flows through tours unmeasured.
C3. **CHAIN COUNT RE-SIZE: 14-16 -> 8-10, tour-book-aligned, rotation-sized (+50-150k/hr, build: config).** Correct sizing = workers x 3-4 rotation slots, concentrated on the tour book's top goods per system (clothing/adv_circ/equipment/medicine/lab). Fewer, deeper, aligned — not broader. Kill-list per X3.
C4. **Mechanize the REST signal (build: small).** The 8w40 export-ask-subsidy signal becomes an automated trigger: own-market lift ask > cross-source eligible median => chain rests one recovery window. Humans stop having to notice it.
C5. **iv65 baseline fix per X4 (build: small).**

## THE SINGLE CHANGE THAT MOST IMPROVES FLEET $/HR: C1.
It is simultaneously the biggest money (+300-600k/hr at current 6.6M/hr factory-good flow, from tours realizing at basis instead of laddered ask) and a net REDUCTION in architecture (two models become one). If era-3 builds exactly one thing from this brief, build C1.

## FEWER-OR-BROADER, answered: FEWER AND ALIGNED. 8-10 chains max at current worker count, all in the tour book's top goods, rotation-sized. Breadth is the tours' job (they arbitrage 39 systems); the factories' job is to make the 5 goods the tours sell most of cost less.

## ERA-3 DOCTRINE (one paragraph, portable):
"A factory is a stocker with a local acquisition method. Its output is planner-visible stock at cost basis, never passive market supply. Chains are few, tour-aligned, and rotation-sized to the worker pool. Sourcing is supply-first with cross-source price backstops. Every chain carries its own P&L and dies automatically when it stops paying. Input recursion beyond depth-1 is forbidden — raw inputs are bought, not made."

**Confidence:** HIGH on the economics of every keep/kill/change (each traces to a measured ruling on a bead); MEDIUM on the C1 build-cost estimate (shipwright owns that); the $/hr ranges use the same rested-lift honesty clause as v8ha — plan on the low ends. T-24h note: C2-C5 are small enough to land this era; C1 is era-3's first build.

VALIDATION ADDENDUM (Admiral challenge, ~13:25Z) — epistemic status of the C1 cycling claim, link by link: MEASURED: feeding lowers asks/grows supply (1.17M dump: D39 4,681->3,919 overnight; export vols 6->15-22 over era); double-draw ladders asks (D40 3,444->6,901 under factory+tour draw; +21%/11 tranches); tours pay premium at own markets (home medicine inversion); rest recovers (fitted per-tier half-lives). PROVEN n=1: stock->zero-ask withdrawal (dchv 19:44->19:55Z). DERIVED not observed: the +300-600k/hr (tour book paid-ask vs rested-lift deltas x measured volumes — real numbers, unbuilt mechanism). ASSUMED, not isolated: that lifting sustains the export market's production (correlates only: fed markets go GROWING, unfed RESTRICTED — no controlled separation of feed vs lift effects). TWO VALIDATION TESTS ATTACHED TO THE DOCTRINE: (T1, runnable THIS ERA, free) pick two comparable fed chains, lift one on rotation, leave one unlifted 6h — supply-state divergence isolates the assumed link; (T2, C1 acceptance metric) tours' avg acquisition cost for factory goods must track the RESTED ask series, not the ladder — single telemetry series, pass/fail at burn-in. Era-3 doctrine inherits C1 CONDITIONAL on T2; T1's result updates the lift-cadence parameter either way.
T1 ANSWERED FROM HISTORICAL DATA (pandas analysis over 32,669 history rows / 14,815 export observations, full era span — Admiral-directed): **THE ASSUMED LINK IS FALSE IN THE SAFE DIRECTION: export production does NOT require our purchase-demand.** Q1, treatment-group comparison of 317 export series (>=4 obs, >=12h span): UNTOUCHED exports (n=115, probe-observed only, zero fleet transactions) held supply perfectly (median drift 0.0 [0,+1]) with asks SOFTENING -2.5% — production continues and supply accumulates with no buyer at all. FED-ONLY (n=63): softest asks of any group (-4.1%) — feeding builds output supply even unlifted. FED+LIFTED (n=128): the ONLY group with tightening (supply -0.5, asks +4.8%) — OUR LIFTING is what tightens markets, not what sustains them. Q2 event study (n=97 series with pre/post coverage around our first lift): ask +6.5%, supply 2.3->1.9 — lifting consumes, full stop. IMPLICATION FOR C1: routing tours to stock-at-basis CANNOT starve market production — feeding alone keeps export markets healthy and soft; C1 is BETTER than projected. T2 (burn-in acquisition-cost telemetry) remains the deploy gate. Q3 BONUS — independent recovery re-fit: 31 spike-decay events, half-life median 194min IQR [149,270] — VALIDATES the model artifact's tagged tiers (180-414min) from our own era's data; the rotation doctrine's foundation is confirmed twice over. HONEST CAVEATS: observational, not randomized — untouched series skew toward penny goods (we touch what's valuable); and one correction to my own earlier claim: trade_volume is essentially STATIC per market (median growth 1.00 in ALL groups) — the D39/D40 vol 6->15-22 growth was a rare threshold event, not the normal feed benefit; the feed benefit is PRICE and SUPPLY-STATE, not volume. Era-3 doctrine updated accordingly.

title: Consult (Admiral-directed): review the ORIGINAL factory design (gobot/docs/GOODS_FACTORY_GAP_ANALYSIS.md, 2025-11-22) vs current input-buying strategy — the original SupplyChainResolver ranked sources by SUPPLY (STRONG activity, HIGH supply preferred, price as tiebreaker); rule the correct sourcing policy
description: ADMIRAL ORDER (12:30Z): check the original factory design and rule whether current strategy matches it. GIT FINDING (captain): the original design doc specifies SupplyChainResolver 'Market Ranking: Prefers STRONG activity, HIGH supply' + 'Price Tracking: Returns cheapest market' — i.e., SUPPLY-FIRST selection, price as tiebreaker within healthy-supply candidates. The 07-11 furnace incident (ADV_CIRC chasing a SCARCE laddered market to -6.6M) could not have happened under the original ranking — it would have re-sourced. Current state: iv65 price ceiling live (lagging backstop), sp-a5j7 filed for supply-gate + buy-side absorption. ANALYST ASKS: (1) confirm/refute that supply-first ranking (the original design) is the correct primary sourcing policy vs the current cheapest/nearest+ceiling approach; (2) rank the three mechanisms (supply-state ranking, trade_volume tranche caps, price ceiling) as primary/secondary/backstop; (3) any case where price-first beats supply-first (e.g., era-end liquidation buys)? DELIVERABLE: a ruled sourcing policy the shipwright implements as sp-a5j7's final spec — restoration of the original design's intent, not an invention.
notes:
TRADE-ANALYST RULING (sp-wedx, Admiral-directed, ~12:40Z) — verified the doc first: GOODS_FACTORY_GAP_ANALYSIS.md lines 71-72 say exactly what the captain found ('Market Ranking: Prefers STRONG activity, HIGH supply' / 'Price Tracking: Returns cheapest market'). The original design made RANKING govern selection with price as information/tiebreak; the current implementation inverted it to price-first-with-ceiling. **(1) CONFIRMED: SUPPLY-FIRST IS THE CORRECT PRIMARY POLICY** — and not on the doc's authority alone: the era's own incident ledger proves it. EVERY input blowup began with a buy from a SCARCE/LIMITED source (D39 parts -220k, the 19-26k/u micro chase, the -891k electronics contract, the -6.6M furnace); ZERO blowups originated from ABUNDANT/HIGH sources. Mechanism: supply state is the LEADING indicator (my fitted tier model: SCARCE/RESTRICTED regenerates at 413min half-life — it ladders IMMEDIATELY under draw; ABUNDANT absorbs draw without repricing). Price is the LAGGING signal — by the time the ask ladders, the money is spent. **(2) MECHANISM RANKING: PRIMARY = supply-state eligibility+ranking; SECONDARY = trade_volume tranche caps; BACKSTOP = iv65 price ceiling.** The spec for sp-a5j7: (a) ELIGIBILITY: supply in {ABUNDANT, HIGH, MODERATE}; SCARCE/LIMITED ineligible EXCEPT (no eligible source exists) AND (chain blocked) AND (ask <= 1.2x trailing median) — the rescue clause. (b) RANKING among eligible: supply state desc > activity (STRONG > GROWING > WEAK > RESTRICTED) > price asc > distance. (c) TRANCHE SIZING: <=2 tranches/visit at MODERATE, <=3 at HIGH/ABUNDANT (measured ladder ~+2%/tranche keeps basis drift <6%). (d) RE-SOURCE TRIGGER (the furnace fix): if the selected source's supply state degrades below MODERATE mid-chain, re-run the locator instead of continuing — the original design would have re-sourced; the incident is the proof of what happens without it. (e) BACKSTOP: iv65 ceiling at 1.5x trailing-6h median stays, catching stale supply states and simultaneous multi-source depletion. **(3) WHERE PRICE-FIRST LEGITIMATELY WINS — three narrow cases:** (i) ERA-END MODE (< T-6h): mean-reversion has no time to work; a cheap-but-SCARCE ask that clears margin NOW beats waiting — flip to price-first-with-margin-check at the same T-6h boundary as the stocker rundown; (ii) SINGLE-TRANCHE MICRO-BUYS (<=1 tranche cannot move supply — acceptance tests, fillers); (iii) EXCHANGE-type markets (narrow fixed spreads, supply semantics weak — rank by price there). Everywhere else: supply-first, no exceptions. RULING SUMMARY FOR THE SHIPWRIGHT: restore the original design's intent — ranking governs, price tiebreaks, ceiling backstops; this is a RESTORATION, not an invention, and the era's P&L says the original designers were right.
RULED + CLOSING (12:45Z): supply-first CONFIRMED as the primary sourcing policy — the original design doc verified at lines 71-72, and the era's own ledger is the proof: every input blowup (parts -220k, micro chase, electronics -891k, furnace -6.6M) originated at a SCARCE/LIMITED source; zero from ABUNDANT/HIGH. Mechanism order ruled: (1) supply eligibility+ranking PRIMARY, (2) trade_volume tranche caps SECONDARY, (3) iv65 price ceiling BACKSTOP. Plus the furnace-specific fix: a RE-SOURCE trigger on mid-chain supply degradation (don't park forever — move to the next healthy source). Price-first survives only: era-end T-6h mode, single-tranche micro-buys, EXCHANGE markets. The Admiral's original design vindicated by the era's own data. a5j7 spec finalized on that bead; shipwright builds the restoration.

title: P1 MONEY: factory input buyer has NO price ceiling — ADV_CIRC chain bought inputs at 4x market (19k/u ELECTRONICS+MICROPROCESSORS ladder) to fabricate a 7k/u output: -6.6M in 3h (-2.2M/hr). The analyst's ladder-chase ruling (abort at 1.5x trailing median) was ruled but NEVER implemented engine-side
description: Evidence 3h window (05:40-08:40Z): workers 57 (-4.1M) + 5A (-2.6M) bought 6x43u ELECTRONICS at ~18.8k/u (4.86M) + 4x43u MICROPROCESSORS at ~18.7k/u (3.2M) at KA42 F45/A3 — the D39-ladder class repricing +each buy — as ADVANCED_CIRCUITRY inputs; the output sells ~7-8k/u. Chain-level P&L: every fabrication cycle destroys ~60-70% of input capital. This soaked up the ENTIRE margin of 5 green tour hours (the +200-580k closes should have been +1-2M). Third recurrence of this chain burning money (823k-1.12M single tranches on 07-10, stopped twice, relaunched by captain in blanket unfreezes — captain error logged separately). ENGINE FIX (the analyst's sp ruling, verbatim from their answer, never landed as code): input buys abort at >1.5x trailing median ask, defer one rest window, check alt-sources first — never chase. PLUS the structural gate: refuse fabrication when sum(input asks x ratio) > output bid (chain-level negative-margin abort, RULINGS #4 fail-closed). ACCEPTANCE: a ladder-priced input logs an abort line and the chain parks instead of buying.
notes:
MERGED to local main: 944c384 (rebased onto 50b5793 after sp-avt4 landed — clean, no file overlap). numstat: 11 files, +945/-0.

Files: input_price_ceiling.go (new, both guards + reader port + ctx config + medianInt); production_executor.go (+33: priceHistory field, ceiling call in buyGood pre-nav, chain-gate call in fabricateGood); run_factory_coordinator.go (+14: SetPriceHistoryReader passthrough + WithInputPriceCeiling ctx stamp); factory_types.go (+9: 2 cmd fields); command_factory_registry.go (+6: OptionalFloat/OptionalBool reads); container_ops_manufacturing.go (+13: 2 keys in clear-list + inject); config/manufacturing.go (+14: 2 mapstructure fields); main.go (+4: SetPriceHistoryReader(priceHistoryRepo)). Tests: input_ceiling_test.go (8: ADV_CIRC 19k-vs-4750 park, under-ceiling proceed, fail-closed no-median, fail-closed read-error, fail-open unwired, disable flag, custom multiplier, single-sample stable market); chain_margin_gate_test.go (6: park/proceed/resume/fail-open-no-sink + fabricateGood wiring + inputs-only bypass); command_factory_input_ceiling_live_test.go (5 config round-trip pins).

go build ./... + go test ./... green (gate ran full suite twice). GatePassed:true Merged:true. NOT deployed (ships HOT on gate = harbormaster's move), NOT closed. Both furnace chains can restart once this deploys — the ceiling parks a ladder-priced input per-tranche, the chain gate parks a structurally-underwater fabrication.
MERGED 944c384 (+945, 11 files, harbormaster-verified), pushed, DEPLOYED HOT 09:13 local (daemon rebuild+kickstart; routing kickstarted same boundary for avt4 — rate objective boot line re-verified). Root cause banked: ChainMarginGuard projects once at launch; the ladder climbs DURING the per-tranche buy round — no per-buy ceiling existed. Design calls accepted on review: minSamples=1 (on-change history = stable market has ONE row; >1 would fail-closed-park every stable input — guard-rejects-a-class applied), ceiling INFO not WARN, sell_price like-for-like median. ACCEPT WHEN (captain): restart the furnace chains; ADV_CIRC either buys at sane asks or logs the ceiling park INFO (good/ask/median/ceiling) with ZERO buys above 1.5x median in the ledger.
ACCEPTANCE FAILED (captain, 12:32Z): post-relaunch ELECTRONICS ladder 8973->10767->12976/u, 1.4M/10min, ZERO parks (rows 12:18:32/12:21:55/12:25:17 @ KA42, worker 5A). ROOT CAUSE: waypoint-scoped 24h median is SELF-POISONED — yesterdays own 19k ladder at KA42 sits in the baseline, so 1.5x(inflated median) never fires on 9-13k asks. Fleet-wide 24h avg 4803/u would have parked all three. The ceiling failed exactly where the fleet had already laddered — the lagging indicator lagging its own damage. Fix vehicle = sp-a5j7 supply-first (SCARCE/LIMITED gate parks regardless of price baselines) + a5j7 should harden the ceiling baseline (multi-market median and/or exclude own-fleet buys from the series). Furnaces DEAD (3rd stop) until a5j7 deploys. Bead stays OPEN pending the a5j7-combined acceptance.

title: Tour jump leg fires from a non-gate waypoint: 'cannot jump: not at a jump gate' hard-crash (37 @ DP51-X11A planning a jump to NK36) — the planner assumes jump-from-anywhere or the executor skips the gate-transit leg
description: Evidence 05:16Z: tour-run TORWIND-37 crashed 'travel to leg 1 (X1-NK36-FE8A) failed: jump TORWIND-37 to X1-NK36 (hop 1 of 1) failed: cannot jump: no jump drive module and not at a jump gate' — the hull sat at X1-DP51-X11A (a market, NOT the gate B26F). Either the multi-system plan omitted the navigate-to-gate leg or the executor didn't inject it (28n2 fixed dock-state at gates; this is the position-state sibling: NOT-AT-GATE). Frequency: first occurrence post-4hl5 multi-system tours — likely a live-scan-era plan shape the fixed graph now produces. Watchdog+coordinator re-man cover recovery (37 already released); the crash class needs the auto-navigate-to-gate guard the manual jump verb already has ('will automatically navigate to the nearest jump gate' per CLI help — the tour executor lacks the same).
notes:
GATED + MERGED to LOCAL main. Merge SHA 3fa8cc2 (squash). numstat: 6 files, +260/-3 — production fix run_trade_route_coordinator_travel.go (+27, the departure-hop re-confirmation) + 5 test/harness files. No .beads staged. captain-gate ran full 'go build ./... && go test ./...' GREEN (GatePassed=true, Stale=false, Merged=true, EmptyMerge=false; incl. the new sp-39oi liquidation package + internal/captain). Did NOT deploy, did NOT close.

COST NOTE: the fix adds ONE authoritative SyncShipFromAPI read per cross-system departure hop (market-origin legs only; gate-origin lanes skip the departure hop entirely). Acceptable vs a fleet-killing crash, and it mirrors dock's sp-ynuf resync.

FOLLOW-UP (not in scope, worth a bead): the deeper root is arrival_wait.go:229 — WaitForShipArrival's safety resync treats 'NavStatus != IN_TRANSIT' as 'arrived' even when the arrival is NOT yet due (dueIn>0), so a stale pre-departure snapshot reads as a false-positive arrival. My fix hardens the departure-hop->jump seam (the one that hard-crashes) defensively; other navigate-then-act flows (navigate-then-dock, etc.) could still see a premature 'arrived' from the same false-positive, though most tolerate it via their own resync/dock. Hardening arrival_wait.go (e.g. don't conclude arrival on a not-due not-in-transit read without an authoritative re-check) would fix the class at the source.
REAL FIX MERGED 3fa8cc2 (+260/-3; production diff +27 in travel departure hop), pushed, DEPLOYED 03:29 boundary (with 39oi). Root cause: arrival_wait safety-resync false positive (stale not-in-transit snapshot 30s into a 2m33s flight) completed the gate hop with the hull still at the market; jump fired from X11A. Fix: authoritative SyncShipFromAPI + IsJumpGate() confirmation before jump; resume-safe error otherwise. Both pins reproduce the verbatim live crash with fix disabled. Deeper class filed as its own bead (arrival_wait false-positive). ACCEPT WHEN: zero "cannot jump: not at a jump gate" crashes across 12h of multi-system touring (grep daemon.log).

title: Trade Flows drilldown v2 (Admiral-directed): clicking a system draws its waypoints TO SCALE (real x/y) with actual trade routes between them
description: Admiral design direction 2026-07-11 (live, on seeing the shipped tab): the system drilldown must render REAL geometry, not abstract positioning. SPEC: (1) on system click, fetch that systems waypoints with their true coordinates (existing endpoint GET /api/systems/:systemSymbol/waypoints — PG waypoints.x/y, range ±~800) and lay them out TO SCALE (fit-to-viewport transform preserving aspect, like the main system map); (2) draw the ACTUAL trade routes between waypoints: realized legs from /api/flows/lanes filtered to intra-system pairs (from/to are waypoint symbols already) as profit-colored arrows waypoint->waypoint (reuse the TradeRouteLayer arrow/label idiom + noir tokens + the flows profit scale); cross-system legs render as an exit vector toward the gate waypoint; (3) resident hulls positioned at their actual waypoint (ships.location or nav data via the /live join); (4) when the daemon feed is live, overlay the intent hops (dashed) for resident flows at waypoint granularity — degrade silently when feedLost. Tests per the tabs established split: pure geometry helpers unit-tested (fit transform, lane filtering), HTML panel tests, screenshot read for the visual half (S1 rule — verify the drilldown at a system with real lanes, e.g. X1-UQ16). Visualizer-only lane. Builds on sp-7nyt (merged fef8615).

SOURCE: players table has no headquarters column — derive server-side from the game API GET /my/agent (server already holds the token via players.token + SpaceTradersClient) -> headquarters waypoint -> home system = first two symbol segments; stamp homeSystem onto the /api/flows/topology response (cached, so zero extra polling). title: Contract pool jam: parked-with-cargo hulls need an auto-liquidation leg — 11/11 hulls hold 6-77u leftovers, spawn-filter rejects all, fulfillments 7-10/hr -> 0
description: Captain evidence (04:43Z mail): contract history 00h +283k/7, 01h +541k/10, 02h +100k/2, 03h +556k/9, 04h 0/0. All 11 contract hulls hold small unrelated-cargo leftovers; the spawn filter ("No spawnable ships - N hold unrelated cargo" — the exact line visible in tonights burst log at 10/11) rejects every candidate. Suspected origin: mid-crisis floor-aborts stranded tranches across the pool. TWO QUESTIONS to answer in-code: (1) why do idle-arb/tour exits NOT fully liquidate holds (find the exit paths that can strand cargo — floor-abort, margin-abort, max-spend abort); (2) design + build the unjam: parked-with-cargo hull gets an AUTO-LIQUIDATION leg (sell leftovers at current waypoint if bid exists, else nearest in-system bid; respect floors/guards — RULINGS #4 fail-closed) OR the contract spawn path sells leftovers before adopting. Prefer the standing mechanism (auto-liquidation) over spawn-path special-casing — the same jam class hits tour re-adoption. Captain hand-clears the worst 3 meanwhile; ~300-550k/hr foregone until fixed.

title: Contract pool self-jams: stranded cargo on ALL 11 dual-duty hulls + parked-candidates filter = zero eligible workers, zero fulfillments (04:00 hour) — pool needs a self-clearing liquidation path
description: P1 evidence (04:45Z): every contract-pool hull held 6-77u of leftover cargo (T1 77 IRON, 3: 67 PLASTICS, 7: 66 QUARTZ_SAND, 6: 56 ALUMINUM, 10: 56 SILICON_CRYSTALS, 11: 54 FABRICS, plus 5 small lots) — the goods match CONTRACT-BOUGHT lots orphaned when crisis-era workflows died mid-delivery (e.g. the 66u SILICON_CRYSTALS contract at 20:18). The parked-candidates filter (correctly) rejects laden hulls -> coordinator logged 'Parked candidates holding unrelated cargo' every cycle -> fulfillments 7-10/hr -> ZERO for 45+ min. Same filter+stranded-cargo class as the T1-ballast incident (sp-pof6 note) and 38's FABRICS strand — third occurrence, now fleet-scale. WANTED: parked-with-cargo hulls get an AUTO-LIQUIDATION leg (sell at best in-system bid; jettison below a value floor) instead of sitting filtered — mirrors lbbm's held-tranche honest-exit doctrine. Also worth checking: why did contract workflow deaths NOT liquidate their sourced goods on exit? CAPTAIN INTERIM (done): 8 hulls jettison-cleared (low-value), 3 valuable holds ferrying to importers; pool re-eligible as of this wake.
acceptance_criteria: a parked-with-cargo contract hull self-clears (sell or floor-jettison) and re-enters candidacy without captain hands
notes:
MERGED-IN from harbormaster duplicate sp-hrzd (mails crossed; captain bead survives — it carries the hold inventory + orphan mechanism + acceptance). Engineering framing folded in: (1) enumerate ALL exit paths that can strand holds (floor-abort, margin-abort, max-spend abort — the crisis fired all three classes; the same jam class threatens tour re-adoption, not just contracts); (2) fix = STANDING auto-liquidation mechanism (sell at current waypoint if bid, else nearest in-system bid; RULINGS #4 floors/guards intact, fail-closed), not spawn-path special-casing. Acceptance stands as captain wrote: parked-with-cargo hull self-clears and re-enters candidacy, zero hands. Queue: next freed lane (cap full: 13tl/y0f6/x8i5).

comments.author: Andres Camacho
comments.text:
MILESTONE 1 — Reconnaissance complete: strand-capable exit-path table + seam choice (build agent, sp-39oi)

## Deliverable #1 — Strand-capable exit paths (trading + contract commands)

Legend: STRAND = terminates with cargo aboard, no recovery; SELL = liquidates before exit; PARK = keeps cargo with a re-adoptable worker (contract-only); PRE-BUY = aborts before any cargo is bought (no strand possible).

### TRADING — run_arb_coordinator.go
| Path | line | class | cargo on exit |
|---|---|---|---|
| routability / location / margin / max-spend / spend-floor / buy-ceiling aborts | 487-659, 761-787 | PRE-BUY (floor/margin/max-spend) | none — abort before buy |
| reload/travel/dock/sell dispatch errors post-buy | 354-401 | error-exit | **STRAND** |
| sell-floor abort (sp-lbbm) | 423-437 | **floor-abort** | **STRAND** ("held aboard for later liquidation") |
| destination cannot absorb | 438-447 | starvation | **STRAND** (action arb_stranded_cargo) |
Arb has NO liquidation loop; comment defers held cargo to "the m5kv/next-leg liquidation path" (an external future actor that, for a parked idle hull, never comes).

### TRADING — run_trade_route_coordinator*.go (circuit)
| Path | line | class | cargo on exit |
|---|---|---|---|
| max_visits / no_lanes / margin_exhausted / unroutable | 502-590 | orderly / margin | none (epilogue liquidates or pre-buy) |
| cargo_blocked pre-flight (starts laden) | 599-607 | pre-existing strand | **STRAND** (parks, minFreeCargoForCircuit gate — the trading-side twin of FilterUnrelatedCargo) |
| runCircuit epilogue liquidateHeld SUCCESS | circuit.go 745-753 | SELL | none — the one real trading liquidation |
| runCircuit epilogue liquidateHeld gives up (3 fails) | circuit.go 759-765 | **floor/error** | **STRAND** (CargoStranded=true) |
liquidateHeld (circuit.go:37) = bounded 3-attempt deliver-to-lane-dest+sell. Lane-specific (sells only at the lane's known importer), not a generic best-in-system-bid liquidation.

### TRADING — run_tour_coordinator*.go
| Path | line | class | cargo on exit |
|---|---|---|---|
| buy caps / floor / ceiling in executeBuy | 1034-1152 | max-spend/floor | none — caps just buy less |
| CargoStranded veto (FINAL exit) | 764-769 | **floor/margin** | **STRAND** — no liquidation |
| re-plan budget exhausted / infeasible | 860-886 | starvation | **may STRAND** |
| reposition "honest-exit" (maybeReposition) | reposition.go | margin-death | defers (rides the jump, m5kv), never liquidates |
Tour has NO bounded liquidation engine.

### TRADING — run_stocker_coordinator.go
| CargoStranded veto (FINAL) 292-297; warehouse-full haulAndDeposit/depositGood 678-719 | starvation/capacity | **STRAND** (undeposited; no market-sell fallback) |

### CONTRACT — delivery_executor.go / run_contract_workflow.go
Contract "parks" (cargo stays with the SAME worker, later re-adopted to resume) — not lost, but a parked laden worker is filtered from NEW candidacy.
| insufficient-credits / ladder-cap / no-progress parks | delivery_executor 233-299; workflow 119-190 | margin/starvation | **PARK** (re-adoptable, but blocks candidacy) |
| JettisonWrongCargoIfNeeded | cargo_manager 81-170 | — | destructive jettison of WRONG cargo only (value-destroying, not a market sell) |

### THE JAM SEAM — ship_pool_manager.go FilterUnrelatedCargo (489-533)
Any hull whose hold HasItemsOtherThan(requiredCargo) → `parked`, excluded from `claimable`. In run_fleet_coordinator.go:566-582, when every candidate is parked/held, the coordinator logs "No spawnable ships - N hold unrelated cargo" and waits forever. This is where cargo stranded by ANY workflow above (arb/trade/tour/stocker/contract) converges into "cannot re-enter contract candidacy" = the fleet-scale jam.

### Existing liquidation subsystems
- ONLY real one: MANUFACTURING orphaned_cargo_handler.go + liquidate_executor.go (standing discovery-loop scan → sell at best in-system bid via marketRepo.FindBestMarketBuying; jettison below MinLiquidateCargoValue=10000). ZERO references from trading/contract — that is the gap.
- Trading: liquidateHeld (lane-specific, bounded) + sellWithFloor primitive. No standing pickup of an already-idle stranded hull.

## Seam choice — CHOSEN: contract candidacy-rejection seam (Seam A), NOT container-exit hook (Seam B)

Standing auto-liquidation dispatched from the contract fleet coordinator's parked-ship path (FilterUnrelatedCargo → parkedShips), mirroring the proven manufacturing OrphanedCargoHandler doctrine.

Why NOT Seam B (container_runner.go releaseShipAssignments, the 7yej hook):
1. DECISIVE: the acceptance targets hulls ALREADY parked (crisis-era strands) — their containers died long ago. An exit-time hook only fires at the moment of death, so it can NEVER pick up an already-idle laden hull. Only a standing scan on the discovery loop clears both already-parked hulls AND future strands.
2. Wrong layer: releaseShipAssignments runs in the gRPC adapter on teardown, context.Background() + short dbOperationTimeout, no market/nav/sell capability. Navigate+sell is a multi-minute workflow; it would have to spawn a worker anyway, coupling a generic adapter to one workflow. It also fires on normal "completed" exits (empty holds) — cost on the hot path.
3. Doctrine match: manufacturing already solved this identical jam class with a standing discovery-loop scan. Contract gets the same doctrine at its candidacy seam.
4. Whole-jam-class: the jam is "laden hull rejected from candidacy." Cargo from ANY workflow converges at FilterUnrelatedCargo, so clearing it there covers the class regardless of cargo origin.

## Planned mechanism
- New one-shot worker container `cargo_liquidation` (twin of worker_ferry): deps shipRepo + marketRepo + mediator; dispatches existing Navigate/Dock/Sell/Jettison mediator commands (reuse, no new I/O).
- Ladder: sell at current waypoint if it bids the good → else nearest in-system market with a bid (navigate+sell) → jettison ONLY as last resort below min-value threshold (default 0 = OFF). Money guards fail-closed: sale is revenue (floors don't block), movement/fuel respects guards; if unaffordable to move, sell-in-place-or-hold+log (never dump valuable cargo).
- Coordinator spawns it on each parked-with-cargo hull; on completion the hull releases empty and re-enters candidacy.
- Config (ae64f03 4-point seam): contract.auto_liquidation.disabled (default false = sell ON) + contract.auto_liquidation.min_jettison_value (default 0 = jettison OFF).

comments.text:
MILESTONE 2 — Built, gated, MERGED to local main (build agent, sp-39oi). Bead left OPEN; not deployed, per instructions.

Merge SHA: 50333948098d8064994e765ab2b7a894036b7eb7 (local main)
Gate: {"GatePassed":true,"Stale":false,"Merged":true,"Dirty":false,"EmptyMerge":false} — full `go build ./... && go test ./...` green inside the gate (rebased onto local main first — sp-y0f6/others had landed; clean rebase, no conflicts).

Numstat (17 files, +1371/-3):
  liquidate_cargo.go 270 | liquidate_cargo_test.go 343 | run_fleet_coordinator.go +129
  run_fleet_coordinator_liquidation_test.go 249 | container_ops_liquidation.go 100
  command_factory_auto_liquidation_live_test.go 80 | container_ops_contract.go +38
  command_factory_registry.go +33 | reclaim_interrupted_worker_ships_test.go +30
  config.yaml.example +24 | config/contract.go +19/-2 | contract_types.go +14 | main.go +11
  worker_lifecycle_manager.go +10/-1 | daemon_client_local.go +9 | ports.go +8 | container.go +4

SEAM CHOSEN: contract candidacy-rejection seam (FilterUnrelatedCargo -> parkedShips in run_fleet_coordinator), NOT the container-exit hook. Decisive reason: the acceptance targets hulls ALREADY parked (crisis-era strands whose containers died long ago); only a standing discovery-loop scan clears those, an exit-time hook fires only at the moment of death. Also mirrors the proven manufacturing OrphanedCargoHandler doctrine and reuses existing navigate/dock/sell/jettison commands. Full seam analysis + exit-path table in the prior comment.

WHAT LANDED:
- New package internal/application/liquidation: LiquidateCargoCommand/Handler — one-shot worker (twin of worker_ferry) that sells each leftover lot at the best in-system bid; jettison only as last resort below the value floor (default 0 = OFF); nav-fail/no-market => HOLD (never dumps value); sync-fail => fail honestly. Deps: shipRepo + marketRepo + mediator only.
- New container kind cargo_liquidation: domain/daemon/ports + container type + daemon_client_local Persist/Start cases + container_ops_liquidation.go (Persist/Start, coordinator-managed, one iteration) + registry build func + daemon main.go handler registration.
- Contract fleet coordinator: dispatchLiquidationForParked (standing, every pass, per-hull 5m cooldown to bound a stuck-hull storm) + spawnLiquidationWorker (atomic claim under operation "contract", start-fail rollback). Config fields on RunFleetCoordinatorCommand.
- RECOVERY-SAFETY (the enumerate-the-rejected-class lesson): ReclaimShipsFromInterruptedWorkers now reclaims CARGO_LIQUIDATION as well as CONTRACT_WORKFLOW — a restart marks an interrupted liquidation worker FAILED with its claim preserved, and FindAllByPlayer does NOT reconcile stale claims (only the external RefreshShip query does), so without this the hull would deadlock claimed to a dead container. The re-adoption path (FindInterruptedWorkerShipsWithCargo) is deliberately left CONTRACT_WORKFLOW-only (don't resume a liquidation as a contract delivery).
- Config (RULINGS #5, ae64f03 four-point live seam): config struct AutoLiquidationSettings{Disabled, MinJettisonValue} -> registry read -> container_ops resolve/inject + key-clear -> config.yaml.example. Live-resolved every build so a retune reaches a recovered coordinator. Defaults: sale ON, jettison OFF/0.

ACCEPTANCE TEST (verbatim pin): TestFleetCoordinator_ParkedHullSelfClearsAndReentersCandidacy — laden hull parked by FilterUnrelatedCargo (the jam) -> coordinator dispatches a cargo_liquidation worker on it -> the REAL liquidation worker sells the strand at the best in-system bid -> the now-empty hull is claimable by FilterUnrelatedCargo again. Zero captain hands end-to-end.

TDD total: 9 worker tests + 3 coordinator dispatch/cooldown/disabled + 1 reclaim + 3 live-config = 16 new tests.

title: Earner-dark alerting: machine detection for revenue-critical fleet stalls — tonight nobody was paged, a human caught the flatline
description: 2026-07-11 P0 lesson: the fleet earned zero for 2h50m and NO automated signal fired — the captain noticed the treasury chart. Detection was human, ~60 min after onset. Build the alert layer: (1) Prometheus alert rules on the existing metrics — zero heavy SELL transactions over a rolling 20-min window while daemon up + tours relaunching (the exact tonight signature); tour_exit_total{reason=starvation} rate spike; tour infeasibility streak (needs the infeasible_reason bead as input). (2) Route: alertmanager -> watchkeeper wake (the captain wake mechanism exists — wire alerts as a wake trigger) so the watch is PAGED, not polling. (3) Alert design per no-monitoring-between-wakes doctrine: event-driven pages, no LLM polling loops. Acceptance: replay tonights 01:41Z window against the rules -> alert fires within 20 min of onset.
notes:
FOLD IN (burst lesson, 00:56Z event): averages hide saturation — alert rules must be burst-aware. Add: p95 rate_limit_wait > 1s sustained 3+ min -> page (the exact tonight signature that 15m-average utilization missed at 53%). Also add utilization > 85%% sustained 10m as the approach-warning.
session died
=== sp-y0f6 progress: rules merged (8c9d90c), Go wiring held pending line-count sign-off ===

RULES (merged to main, pushed): 4 alert rules in
gobot/configs/prometheus/rules/fleet-health.yml — EarnerDark (critical,
zero SELL_CARGO rate over 20m while a TRADING container RUNNING, no
`for:` since the 20m window IS the detection budget), BurstSaturation
(critical, p95 rate_limit_wait>1s for 3m — the 00:56Z lesson),
ApproachCeiling (warning, request-rate>85% of 2req/s budget for 10m),
StarvationWave (warning, starvation-exit rate>30/hr for 5m, calibrated
down from tonight's 123/hr). All 4 metric/label names verified live
against localhost:9091 before writing. rule_files wired into
prometheus.yml; rules dir bind-mounted into both docker-compose.metrics.yml
files (gobot/ and repo-root copies). Did NOT restart/reload Prometheus —
harbormaster's call.

VALIDATION:
- `promtool check rules` via a throwaway `docker run --rm --entrypoint
  promtool -v .../rules:/rules:ro prom/prometheus:latest check rules
  /rules/fleet-health.yml` (does NOT touch the live spacetraders-prometheus
  container) -> "Checking /rules/fleet-health.yml / SUCCESS: 4 rules found".
- query_range replay, HONEST finding: the literal "2026-07-11T01:41Z,
  2h50m zero revenue" window does NOT show a matching signature in this
  Prometheus instance's actual retained data (verified raw increase()
  counts 00:30Z-05:00Z; retention/scrape history covers the full window,
  so this isn't a retention gap — the live telemetry simply doesn't
  reproduce that exact narrative timestamp/duration on this box).
  What IS real in the data: a genuine ~30min SELL_CARGO silence,
  player_id=2, TRADING container RUNNING throughout, last sale ~02:16Z,
  recovery resuming ~02:46Z. Replayed the actual EarnerDark expression
  (both clauses ANDed) via query_range at 30s resolution across this
  real window: first non-empty (fires) at 02:35:30Z, last non-empty
  (clears) at 02:44:30Z. That's ~19m15s from the last real sale to
  first-fire — inside the 20-minute acceptance target, using real
  production data rather than a fabricated match to the narrative
  timestamp. Recommend whoever owns the incident record double check
  whether sp-4hl5's "01:41Z" is this box's actual history or a
  different environment/approximation — flagging rather than papering
  over the mismatch.

GO WIRING — HELD BACK, NOT merged: detectPrometheusAlerts (watchkeeper
polls Prometheus's own /api/v1/alerts once per RunDetectors tick, no
Alertmanager in this stack; records prometheus.alert_firing captain
events, deduped via the existing HasSince cooldown idiom; wired as the
10th DefaultInterruptTypes() entry; last in RunDetectors' chain so a
Prometheus blip can't mask a sibling detector). Fully written, fully
tested (5 new tests, all passing; go build + go test ./... clean, 52
packages, 0 failures at commit time) — but measures 176 net lines of
production Go (detectors.go +123, wake.go +32, events.go +17,
supervisor.go +4) against the task's explicit "~100 lines of Go, else
STOP at rules-only + a documented decision note" ceiling. That's a
real 76-line overage, not a rounding difference, so per instructions
I split it into a separate commit and did NOT include it in the
captain-gate merge. It is preserved intact at commit 3a582ac on branch
sp-y0f6-wiring-held in the same worktree
(/Users/andres.dandrea/IdeaProjects/cities/captain-worktrees/sp-y0f6),
ready to cherry-pick/merge as a fast-follow.

My read: the overage is concentrated entirely in the one function that
has to do HTTP+JSON marshaling (no sibling detector talks to anything
but the same Postgres the app already depends on, so this is
inherently a bit heavier) plus this codebase's own dense-comment
convention (e.g. a 7-line comment on WHY the detector runs last) — zero
new packages, goroutines, or polling loops were introduced; it's 100%
reuse of the existing detector/event/wake-mail machinery applied to a
5th signal source. I think it's justified, but the instruction was an
unconditional stop-rule, not a suggestion, so leaving the actual
merge/no-merge call to the team lead rather than deciding it myself.

main, pushed to origin. Diffstat: 4 files, 115 insertions (0
deletions) — docker-compose.metrics.yml +1, gobot/docker-compose.metrics.yml
+1, gobot/configs/prometheus/prometheus.yml +7,
gobot/configs/prometheus/rules/fleet-health.yml +106 (new file).

Gated sp-y0f6-wiring-held after team-lead sign-off (overage accepted:
176 lines reusing the existing detector/event/wake pattern with 5 tests
is the intended shape, not a watchkeeper redesign). Rebased cleanly onto
the already-merged rules commit (git recognized 328884b's content was
already upstream via patch-id and auto-skipped it during rebase, leaving
just the wiring commit on top of main). go build + go test ./... clean
post-rebase, 52 packages, 0 failures. captain-gate: GatePassed=true,
Merged=true, Dirty=false, EmptyMerge=false. Merge commit 2b56d06 on
main, pushed to origin (8c9d90c..2b56d06). Diffstat: 6 files, 357
insertions/3 deletions — detectors.go +124/-1, wake.go +32/0,
events.go +17/0, supervisor.go +4/0, events_test.go +7/-2,
detectors_prometheus_alerts_test.go +173/0 (new).

DEPLOY: watchkeeper runs under launchd label `com.spacetraders.captain`
(~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.spacetraders.captain.plist) — despite the
"captain" label, ProgramArguments points directly at
gobot/bin/watchkeeper (WorkingDirectory gobot/, KeepAlive=true,
RunAtLoad=true, logs to gobot/captain-supervisor.log). Confirmed
currently loaded and running (PID 23066 at time of check). Rebuild +
`launchctl kickstart -k gui/<uid>/com.spacetraders.captain` is
team-lead's step per the handoff, not run by me — no restarts performed
this session.

=== Replay reconciliation (team-lead request) ===

Tonight's actual incident shape was HEAVIES-dark, not total-dark:
factory/contract SELL_CARGO activity on non-heavy goods continued
throughout, so the all-goods EarnerDark expression
(sum(rate(...SELL_CARGO...[20m])) == 0, no good-type filter) correctly
would NOT have fired for it — there was never a window where the
all-up SELL_CARGO rate hit exactly zero. That's *why* my earlier replay
had to substitute a genuine all-goods zero-revenue window from later in
the same dataset (~02:16Z-02:46Z) rather than the literal incident
window: EarnerDark as designed detects total silence, and tonight
wasn't total silence.

The rule that DOES catch tonight's actual partial-dark shape is
StarvationWave: tour-exit starvation hit 123/hr during the incident,
4x over the 30/hr threshold shipped in fleet-health.yml — that's the
leading indicator that should have paged before revenue ever looked
anomalous in aggregate.

Noting per team-lead's ask, not building now: a heavy/tour-specific
EarnerDark variant (scoped by goods-category or tour-type label,
matching only the earner class that actually went dark) would close
this gap precisely, but our SELL_CARGO metric doesn't yet carry a
heavy-vs-other or tour-vs-contract dimension — needs that label added
at the metrics layer first (tour-sell metrics "maturing", per
team-lead's phrasing) before a scoped variant is meaningful rather than
guesswork. Filing as a future refinement, not scope for this bead.
