TRADE-ANALYST DEFINITIVE HUB SELECTION (Admiral-directed, st-wisp-ukgb) — 2026-07-09 ~16:35Z
Corpus refreshed: **429 fulfilled era-2 contracts** linked to actual purchase waypoints (latest fulfil 16:15Z — 15 min old). Weight = frequency × recency (24h half-life). Zero capex implied — hubs are parking assignments.

**1. METHOD (definitive).** Frequency ranking alone is the WRONG final metric — it picks popular markets, not good parking. The objective that matches the goal ("kill the travel-to-source leg") is **weighted mean travel-to-source distance**, solved by greedy k-medoid on the linked corpus: pick the hub that minimizes weighted mean distance to all sources; repeat on the residual. Validate with coverage@80u (~2.5min hop @spd15). Then check the DELIVERY side for co-location — if destinations sit near hubs, the return-to-hub leg is also ~free. (It does — see 3.) Optimal count: the marginal-gain elbow, bounded by contract-hull count (hubs > hulls = unmanned parking).

**2. THE WAYPOINTS (ordered by greedy selection — truncate from the END if fewer hulls):**
| k | hub | wtd mean dist | travel @spd15 | coverage ≤80u |
|---|-----|--------------|---------------|----------------|
| 1 | **E42** (near-centroid; machinery/fabrics exports) | 165u | 290s | 33.6% |
| 2 | **+J56** (NW anchor; top-2 source AND #1 delivery dest 13.5%) | 94u | 172s | 44.2% |
| 3 | **+B7** (south; exchange) | 64u | 121s | 52.3% |

title: REGRESSION (new wait-timeout deploy): arrival-wait exhausts in ~2min on a 23min transit — timeout must scale with route ETA
description: Evidence 2026-07-09 01:54:22Z, ~30min after the 5-fix deploy: navigate-TORWIND-F-a36d793d (DF9E→B10D, legit ETA ~02:18Z / ~23min route) crashed 'unrecoverable: ship arrival wait exhausted after 3 attempt(s): event never received and resync still shows IN_TRANSIT' — but IN_TRANSIT was TRUE and correct; arrival was ~20min away. The sp-pafv fix half-works: no infinite wedge, clean claim release, success=false published. But the wait budget is fixed-attempts, not ETA-scaled, so EVERY long transit now aborts early — and the released claim invites a coordinator to claim a ship that is physically mid-flight (claim-vs-reality divergence, new incident class). FIX: wait budget = route.arrival + margin (e.g. ETA+25% or ETA+2min, whichever larger); resync verdict should distinguish 'IN_TRANSIT with future arrival' (KEEP WAITING) from 'IN_TRANSIT past arrival' (the actual lost-event case sp-pafv targeted). ACCEPTANCE: 25-min navigate completes under the new code; injected lost-event past-ETA still parks within one resync; no claim release before ETA+margin. NOTE: TORWIND-F itself needs no rescue — game-side transit continues, arrives B10D ~02:18Z regardless.

title: construction --min-supply must reach an IN-PROGRESS pipeline: persist the floor + poll-loop honors it (completes sp-ezz9 for the gate endgame)
description: Trace (2026-07-08) proved sp-ezz9's --min-supply (merged 6a2c5ec) is a NO-OP on an in-progress pipeline — the captain's actual gate case. Findings: (1) StartOrResume idempotent short-circuit (construction_pipeline_planner.go:84-115) returns IsResumed before minSupply is consumed when an existing pipeline has any incomplete task (a deferred FAB_MATS is a non-terminal PENDING DELIVER task). (2) The deferred-material poll-loop resourceDeferredConstructionTask (task_activator.go:290-303) calls FindConstructionSource with a hardcoded '' -> coerced to MODERATE (market_locator.go:343-346); never reads a pipeline floor. (3) Floor is NOT persisted (pipeline.go:62-95 has no floor field/column); it's a transient planning param. (4) No construction stop/cancel verb (construction.go exposes only start/status), so the pipeline can't be restarted fresh with the flag. Net: FAB_MATS stays deferred until supply naturally hits MODERATE regardless of --min-supply.

Fix = two coordinated changes: (a) persist the floor on the pipeline — PREFER a migration-free path (ride an existing serialized/blob field) if the model allows; only add a min_supply column if column-mapped, and FLAG that as a schema migration (Tier-3). StartOrResume resume branch (planner.go:102-114) updates the persisted floor before returning. (b) resourceDeferredConstructionTask (task_activator.go:303) loads the pipeline, reads the floor, passes it to FindConstructionSource instead of ''. TDD: recovery path honors a persisted SCARCE floor; unset still MODERATE. Deploy WITH sp-ezz9 in ONE daemon restart.

title: construction start --min-supply override: caller-set sourcing supply-state floor (gate endgame, ADMIRAL PRIORITY)
description: Captain st-wisp-wtht (2026-07-08, ADMIRAL PRIORITY): construction fill is STATE-gated — the sourcing filter refuses markets below MODERATE supply, so fills only pulse when supply state flips (e.g. F45 holds FAB_MATS at ~3k ask, 466u=1.4M vs 8.5M treasury, but sourcing skips it below MODERATE). Add a caller-set floor so the pipeline sources at any supply state down to the floor when the captain accepts the price. Captain: 'the supply-priority locator already has the tolerance ladder; this just lets the caller set the floor.' Impact: vol-43 continuous pulls finish the gate in ~2-3h vs ~a day of state-cycling.
acceptance_criteria: construction start gains --min-supply <STATE> (e.g. SCARCE) [or --force-buy] that sets the supply-state FLOOR the sourcing locator buys down to. Set: locator accepts markets down to that floor, reusing the EXISTING tolerance ladder (caller sets floor; do not add a new ladder). Unset: behavior UNCHANGED (current MODERATE default). TDD: failing test that floor=SCARCE selects a SCARCE market currently rejected, and without the flag SCARCE stays rejected. No change to money guardrails. Deploy: rebuild+restart daemon (validated-resilient); captain re-issues construction start with the flag.

title: trade-route CLI-runner orphans its ship claim on process death - no container, no release path, hull deadlocked (sp-zewt proven)
description: 2026-07-08 ~11:15: captain ran 'workflow trade-route' foreground under a 2min shell timeout; the CLI process died mid-circuit AFTER persisting claim 'trade-route-TORWIND-3-78dd6806'. Aftermath: assignment row references a dead process; container stop fails (container not found - the CLI runner never registers one); mfg coordinator claim fails ('already assigned'); trade-route itself refuses the ship ('not idle'). TORWIND-3 deadlocked out of ALL engines with 18 DRUGS aboard. This is the sp-zewt structural caveat proving itself: a CLI-process executor persists daemon-side state it cannot guarantee to clean up. Asks: (1) IMMEDIATE: a release path - 'ship release --ship X' verb or clear-assignment on ship refresh when the owning container doesn't exist; (2) STRUCTURAL: promote sp-zewt (trade-route as daemon container) - container lifecycle gives claim-release-on-death for free, exactly like every other worker. Captain-side lesson also logged: long-flying CLI workflows must run in background, never under foreground timeouts.
notes: session died
close_reason: Merged (captain-gate, clean) 44ba6b9 + deployed: ship refresh now reconciles stale/orphaned claims — isClaimOrphaned clears a ship's claim iff the owning container is GONE or PENDING (never RUNNING/INTERRUPTED, no-op on any uncertainty). SAFETY VERIFIED in code: daemon workers write container RUNNING (container_runner.go:~120) BEFORE claiming the ship (:135), so a live worker's claim only ever coexists with RUNNING — PENDING+claim is by construction only the dead trade-route CLI runner's artifact (invisible to recovery per njpu). Clear = ship.ForceRelease('stale_claim_reconciled') + Save, same path daemon workers use on stop. 4 tests incl. the KEEPS-when-RUNNING safety case, RED->GREEN, full module gate green. Deployed via daemon restart — refresh T3 to self-heal. Also self-heals the sp-tgp5 restart-orphan class.

title: trade-route as a daemon container (single-writer safe) — promote the CLI runner
description: sp-s7c2 Part-2 follow-up (2026-07-07). The trade-route coordinator (application/trading/commands/run_trade_route_coordinator.go, RunTradeRouteCoordinatorHandler) is CLI-driven today = a DUAL-WRITER with the daemon on ship state (safe only on genuinely idle-gap hulls; refuses non-idle claims but has a TOCTOU if the daemon grabs the hull mid-circuit). It's already daemon-grade (Handle matches common.RequestHandler, same driven ports as the fabrication coordinators). Promote it to a daemon CONTAINER kind for single-writer safety: new container kind + ContainerSpec + StartTradeRoute RPC + runtime dispatch + proto regen. Then the captain can run disciplined arbitrage circuits on idle-gap capacity WITHOUT the dual-writer race. Needs daemon runtime wiring + proto (couldn't be validated under no-live-daemon). Relatedly: the CLI runner should also refuse to claim a hull the daemon is actively flying, not just !IsIdle at claim.
notes:
ADMIRAL GREENLIT 2026-07-08 (in-chat, after the sj7p acceptance run failed exactly as the caveat predicted — sp-vjwb: CLI runner died on timeout, stranded an orphaned PENDING claim on T3). 5th live-only trade-route issue (r3cl/sh6w/2sam/sj7p + vjwb orphan-on-death), all the same root: CLI runner re-implements daemon behavior it can't guarantee. Promote to daemon container. TEMPLATE the existing container pattern (contract_workflow / gas coordinators: ContainerKind + coordinator + executor + command_factory_registry + recovery). Port the circuit from workflow_trade_route.go + run_trade_route_coordinator.go; reuse the daemon's route_executor (nav/dock/ORBIT/refuel/arrival-events) so all 4 prior patches become free. Container must be RUNNING (recovery-safe), NOT the PENDING that made vjwb's claim invisible to recovery (njpu). Bumped P1.

close_reason: Merged (manual FF, clean — proto bead per a3r9) f2331d4 + deployed: trade-route is now a RECOVERY-SAFE DAEMON CONTAINER, retiring the CLI runner. Single-hull coordinator templated on contract_workflow (ship lifecycle) + gas_coordinator (start/registration); command_type registry 'trade_route' + StartTradeRoute RPC (coordinators don't use the ContainerKind enum — correct). Created RUNNING not PENDING (recovery-visible). ContainerRunner owns claim/release: createShipAssignments on start, releaseShipAssignments on EVERY terminal path (completion/crash/cancel/stop) — retires vjwb (orphan-on-death) + r3cl (FK dance) as a CLASS. navigate() -> NavigateRouteHandler -> RouteExecutor.ExecuteRoute (orbit->refuel->NavigateDirect->arrival) in-process, no re-claiming child — retires 2sam (self-collision) + sj7p (orbit) as a class; the CLI inProcessNavHandler is DELETED. CLI workflow_trade_route is now a thin StartTradeRoute client. Recovery: recoverContainer rebuilds+re-assigns a RUNNING trade_route container, else markContainerFailed force-releases — recover-or-cleanly-release, never stranded. Manual build+test verified green (18 files, +895/-881). Verified by me: main.go 3-way merge (ess3+vjwb+zewt handlers coexist), proto intact (StartTradeRoute+q02m inputs_only), daemon compiles on main. Deploys via restart-daemon + build-cli. THE structural root-cause fix for the 5 live-only trade-route bugs.

title: Construction pipeline terminalizes on one leg's permanent-fail - execution-layer all-or-nothing (planning-layer fixed in r900)
description: 2026-07-07 11:10:48: task a7233fae (FAB_MATS) hit 'no source to acquire from' during F45's below-floor dip, permanently failed after 3 retries -> 'Pipeline f0187282 failed: construction_delivery_failed' - whole pipeline terminal, healthy circuitry leg killed with it, fill frozen 1h+ unnoticed. P1 because every supply dip re-kills the fill until this lands (captain back to heartbeat pokes as safety net). Full lineage in sp-r900 notes (CLOSED - this is its execution-layer successor).
notes: session died
close_reason: merged b93a041, DEPLOYED (daemon pid 49738, batched w/ 6fsq+vz9u+kb61; build-stamp CONFIRMS live binary = b93a04168907). LIVE PROOF: construction X1-KA42-I53 now at 31.5%, BOTH legs advancing independently — FAB_MATS 283/1600, ADV_CIRCUITRY 347/400 (86.8%); the all-or-nothing freeze is gone. Two-part fix: (a) 'no source to acquire' at execution -> ErrDeferToSupply sentinel -> worker parks (ParkForResupply: EXECUTING->PENDING, clears assignedShip, NO retryCount++) -> stays IsDeferredConstruction -> r900's SupplyMonitor re-sources verbatim when supply recovers (zero new recovery code); (b) evaluateConstructionCompletion reordered so inFlight>0 (incl. a parked leg) keeps the pipeline ALIVE, completes on any delivered material once all legs terminal, fails ONLY when nothing delivered AND every leg dead. b1np's all-dead test still green. 6 TDD tests across domain/executor/worker/checker/supplymonitor, RED-proven. title: Watchkeeper auto-spawns captain + surveyor sessions, violating the no-auto-spawn policy (zombie-captain is a symptom)
description: SpawnSession (gobot/internal/captain/gc.go:91-100) runs session-new (l.92) -> acd prime (l.95) -> session submit (l.99). If session-new succeeds but prime or submit fails, it returns error while the session ALREADY EXISTS — live but never primed. ensureCaptainAlive (respawn.go:46) then sees SessionAlive=true and returns early forever; the supervisor believes the captain is alive while it sits inert. Compounding: the Admiral spawn-error alert is '_ ='-swallowed (respawn.go:50), and in the one outage we have logs for (bd/gc env-broken) that alert mail ALSO fails, so the zombie is unreported. Related: SessionAlive (gc.go:84) treats any non active/running state as dead, risking double-spawn on transient startup. Surveyor-verified against source 2026-07-07. FIX: tear down the half-created session on partial failure, or track a primed flag; stop swallowing the alert. TIER-3 (watchkeeper) — human sign-off.
notes:
REFRAMED per Admiral ruling 2026-07-07: 'sessions should never be auto-spawned.' Root defect is NOT that SpawnSession is non-atomic — it's that the watchkeeper AUTO-SPAWNS sessions at all, violating the no-auto-spawn design (city.toml: 'No auto-spawn. Agents invoked manually via acd run/acd prime'). LIVE, not legacy: bridge is the only supported engine_mode (supervisor.go:87-88) and ensureCaptainAlive runs unconditionally every tick (supervisor.go:151 -> respawn.go:49 SpawnSession(captain)). Same violation for the SURVEYOR (surveyor_nudge.go:27 SpawnSession when found dead). The partial-spawn zombie originally described is a SYMPTOM showing WHY auto-spawn is unsafe: it can half-succeed, leaving a live-but-unprimed session the liveness probe reports healthy. CORRECTED FIX: remove the spawn. Watchkeeper should MONITOR + ALERT only — on a dead captain/surveyor, mail the Admiral (loudly, via a path that survives a gc/bd outage) and let a human relaunch via acd run. Nudging/waking a STANDING session stays fine; creating one does not. OPEN DESIGN POINT for Admiral: confirm dead-captain behavior = alert-and-wait-for-manual-relaunch. SURVEYOR SELF-CRITIQUE: catchable in pass 1 — I read city.toml 'No auto-spawn' and never connected it to the watchkeeper SpawnSession calls; filed the symptom with a fix that would have entrenched the violation.
Admiral RULING 2026-07-07: REMOVE auto-restart — it's broken. Open design point resolved = alert-and-wait-for-manual-relaunch. Approved P1, 'human' gate lifted, dispatched to shipwright. SCOPE: strip every SpawnSession call from the watchkeeper — ensureCaptainAlive (respawn.go:49-51) and nudgeSurveyorOnCadence (surveyor_nudge.go:26-31); on a dead captain/surveyor the watchkeeper MAILS the Admiral (loudly + local supervisor.log line that survives a gc/bd outage) and stops. KEEP bridgeWake/Nudge (waking a STANDING session is fine); remove only CREATE. SpawnSession (gc.go:91) likely becomes dead code -> remove if unused. sp-0d1g item #3 (SessionAlive double-spawn) is now moot. Humans relaunch via acd run.
session died
close_reason: merged 0f71404, DEPLOYED: supervisor kickstarted pid 92417. Watchkeeper is now monitor+alert ONLY per Admiral ruling: SpawnSession fully removed (zero refs remain); dead captain/surveyor -> grep-able supervisor.log line FIRST (durable through gc/bd outages) then Admiral mail, mail failures logged never swallowed, per-agent throttle with re-alert after window; probe errors conservative (not dead) but logged. bridgeWake/Nudge of standing sessions untouched. 6 tests cover every ruling clause (never-spawns, log-despite-mail-failure, throttle, re-alert, probe-error, noop-when-alive) + kill-switch test intact. sp-0d1g item 3 moot as ruled. OPERATIONAL CONSEQUENCE NOW LIVE: nobody respawns dead sessions — humans relaunch via acd run on the alert.

title: Session-burn loop: stale-heartbeat detector re-fires an interrupt every tick after ack
description: detectStaleHeartbeats (gobot/internal/captain/detectors.go:59) dedups with store.HasUnprocessed — so once the captain ACKs a heartbeat_lost event, the next poll re-records a NEW one for any still-dead container. heartbeat_lost is interrupt-class, so each re-emit forces an immediate wake, burning a paid captain session every tick until the container is fixed. The sibling detector detectIdleShips (detectors.go:96) was DELIBERATELY switched to store.HasSince(now.Add(-cfg.ShipIdle)) with the comment (detectors.go:93-95) 'cooldown on ANY recent idle event (processed or not) prevents a session-burn loop where each processed event is re-emitted on the next poll' — that fix was never applied to stale-heartbeats. Corroboration: captain-supervisor.log has 389 'session cap reached' lines (captain trying to wake nearly every tick). Surveyor-verified against source 2026-07-07. FIX: mirror detectIdleShips — HasSince(ctx, ..., now.Add(-cfg.StaleHeartbeat)). TIER-3 (watchkeeper) — human sign-off.
notes:
Admiral approved 2026-07-07 (Tier-3 sign-off). P1 session-burn cleared to work; 'human' label removed. Fix still edits the watchkeeper (detectors.go) — worktree + captain-gate discipline applies.
session died
close_reason: merged 4b0f9b4, DEPLOYED: captain rebuilt + supervisor kickstarted. Fix: detectStaleHeartbeats now uses HasSince cooldown (mirrors detectIdleShips) — at most one heartbeat_lost per StaleHeartbeat window per container, acked or not; kills the every-tick interrupt re-wake (session burn, 389 cap-reached lines). Regression test TestStaleHeartbeatCooldownPreventsSessionBurnLoop RED-proven (FAIL pre-fix), full internal/captain suite green, gate green. Third session-burner class closed tonight (soh9 alarm loop, income-stall precedent, this) — all detectors now share the persistent-state-cooldown idiom. Tier-3 under Admiral sign-off recorded by surveyor on the bead.

title: Construction defect #6: supply-construction call fails at site (4/4 attempts, 3 ships) AND executor swallows the API error - zero diagnostic detail at any log level
description: SHAKEDOWN CHAIN, LINK 6 (2026-07-07 00:57-01:25): all four DELIVER_TO_CONSTRUCTION attempts reached I53 WITH cargo and failed at the final supply step: T8/36 ADV_CIRC 00:57:27, T6/78 FAB_MATS 01:01:07, T6 retry 01:19:00, T3 retry 01:25:23. Signature identical: 'DELIVER_TO_CONSTRUCTION: Supplying construction site' -> 'ERROR: Manufacturing task failed' - NO underlying error at daemon.log OR container-logs --level ERROR. (B) THE FAILURE: top hypothesis DOCK-BEFORE-SUPPLY missing - supply-construction API requires ship DOCKED at site; T8 observed IN_ORBIT at I53 seconds after its failure; workers arrive via navigate (ends in orbit) and the executor's step list appears to go arrive->supply with no dock. title: Construction defect #5: DELIVER_TO_CONSTRUCTION tasks never dispatched - rescue-loop every 10s while dispatcher serves only FABRICATION/COLLECTION
description: SHAKEDOWN RE-RUN RESULT (2026-07-07 00:27 local, post sp-8ind deploy): pipeline fd1ab518 SAVED (defect #4 fix verified) with 2 DELIVER_TO_CONSTRUCTION tasks, status EXECUTING - then NOTHING dispatches them. Signature: 'Rescued 2 DELIVER_TO_CONSTRUCTION tasks to queue' (parallel_manufacturing-X1-KA42-18ef4c12) repeating every ~10s indefinitely from 00:27:25. Isolation: dispatcher is ALIVE and type-selective - fresh mfg workers spawned AFTER the construction tasks queued (mfg-task-TORWIND-8-e92c839e 00:26:55, mfg-task-TORWIND-7-f6460b9e 00:27:19) while TORWIND-6 sat DOCKED/idle/eligible at J56 the entire window. No construction worker ever spawned. Hypotheses (pick at code): (a) worker-dispatch/claim query type-filters to FABRICATION/COLLECTION - era-1 defect-#1 class one layer deeper (73f3f08 registered the executor; the DISPATCH path still never selects the type); (b) the 10s rescue loop itself resets task state so tasks never persist in a claimable state (sp-ciog adjacent). LINEAGE: #1 73f3f08 -> #2 f7d644d -> #3 1fe4eff -> #4 sp-8ind (schema) -> #5 THIS. REPRO LEFT LIVE: pipeline fd1ab518 EXECUTING, rescue-loop spamming daemon.log every 10s - observe directly, stop it when done. Acceptance: construction start leads to a worker claiming a DELIVER_TO_CONSTRUCTION task with an idle hauler, first material purchase + delivery, construction status shows material > 0.
notes:
shipwright: claimed immediately on captain P1 mail. Worktree ../captain-worktrees/sp-mvcr, single opus agent (same additive-gap family as #4; dispatch whitelist). Deploy = daemon rebuild+restart; live pipeline fd1ab518 should dispatch post-fix via the rescue loop.
label judgment: 'awaiting-human' appears pattern-copied from #4 — this defect is an ordinary daemon dispatch-path bug (no schema/API/rail), i.e. Tier-1 with failure signature + live repro, and the captain's P1 mail demands speed. Shipwright proceeding; the MERGE is the Admiral's veto checkpoint (in-session, watching). If the Admiral says hold, the branch waits gated.
CRITICAL ISOLATION DATA (2026-07-07 00:42, captain): a FRESH coordinator dispatches the type fine. Old coordinator 18ef4c12 rescue-looped the 2 DELIVER_TO_CONSTRUCTION tasks for ~15 min without one dispatch; captain stopped it (strategy switch to smart, unrelated purpose) and the NEW coordinator a7fee703 assigned task c4e8dfc3 (DELIVER_TO_CONSTRUCTION) to TORWIND-6 within 8s of startup, worker mfg-task-TORWIND-6-caf6f9cf RUNNING. So the dispatch QUERY handles the type; the defect lives in the RUNNING coordinator's steady-state loop - top hypothesis now the rescue-loop/adoption interaction (rescue resets state faster than steady-state dispatch claims it; startup adoption claims BEFORE the first rescue tick). sp-ciog-adjacent. Bead stays OPEN (in-band workaround = coordinator restart; root cause unfixed). Watch also: 'Reset 2 tasks to PENDING due to supply conditions' at 00:42:21 - the OTHER construction task may be supply-gated (FAB_MATS at F45 was RESTRICTED activity); may be correct behavior, verify at fix time.
Further isolation (00:47): NEW coordinator a7fee703 dispatched the SECOND task (86c5eec0, FAB_MATS leg) via its STEADY-STATE loop 5 min after startup - so steady-state dispatch handles the type fine on a fresh coordinator. Kills the 'startup-adoption-only' variant. Remaining hypothesis: old coordinator 18ef4c12 held poisoned in-memory queue/type state - it predated the sp-8ind schema fix (its first encounter with the type was the constraint-violating save of defect #4), suggesting the coordinator caches a task-type validity/whitelist decision at first failure and never revisits. Repro guidance: create a construction pipeline against a coordinator started BEFORE the pipeline's task type ever saved successfully. Both construction tasks now executing (T6 circuitry leg, T8 FAB_MATS leg) - supply gate on task 2 cleared on re-plan.
PREMISE FALSIFIED (agent evidence, orchestrator-verified in daemon.log): dispatch WORKS. 4 assignment lines: c4e8dfc3->TORWIND-7 00:30:32, 86c5eec0->TORWIND-8 00:31:00, c4e8dfc3->TORWIND-6 00:42:15 (the 'benched' ship itself!), 86c5eec0->TORWIND-8 00:47:21. Rescue queue drained 2->1->0 and re-queued only around a coordinator id change (18ef4c12->a7fee703 ~00:42, no crash signature). Root cause of the OBSERVATION: cross-coordinator hauler contention — contract + fabrication held all light haulers during the window; construction actually sorts FIRST (PriorityDeliverToConstruction=75 > COLLECT_SELL 50 > ACQUIRE_DELIVER 10, task.go:83) and reservation_policy.go:64 'default: return true' never skips it. The bead's smoking gun was backwards: TORWIND-6 was busy on contract-work until 00:25:29, not idle. NO CODE DEFECT in the dispatch path. Remaining open question = DELIVERY (site still 0/1600 FAB_MATS, 0/400 ADV_CIRCUITRY): ships assigned and en route; if material stays 0 after full leg time, that is a NEW defect (#6, execution-side) — observation bead being filed. Agent correctly REFUSED to fabricate a fix; guard test lands under an honest test-only message.
SECOND stale-coordinator incident (03:17): long-running coordinator a7fee703 spent its last ~hour skipping 'task from non-executing pipeline' x4 every tick and NOT dispatching COLLECT_SELL for factory outputs that were ready - 3 haulers idle, mfg income ~0 for the window. Restart (304e2f74): assigned COLLECT_SELL to T6 in 4s and cancelled the 2 orphaned tasks. Same class as the original defect-5 observation: coordinator steady-state degrades vs fresh-start behavior. The in-band workaround (restart the coordinator) is now 2-for-2; consider a coordinator self-check (if idle-eligible ships > 0 AND dispatchable tasks > 0 AND no dispatch for N ticks -> log loudly or self-restart).
THIRD stale-coordinator instance (19:07): c2ea9abd degraded to 6/8 haulers idle, log spam 'Skipping task from non-executing pipeline' + discovery-without-dispatch. Restart -> 7/8 working in 20s. Pattern is now firm: long-running mfg coordinators accumulate terminalized-pipeline tasks and rot; manual worker-stops accelerate it. In-band remedy (restart) is 3-for-3 but requires captain noticing - the self-check proposed on this bead (idle-eligible>0 + dispatchable>0 + no dispatch for N ticks -> alarm/self-restart) is now justified by three data points and one Admiral escalation.

TORWIND-6 itself; the wait was cross-coordinator hauler contention; construction outranks siblings (priority 75). Regression guard test merged under honest test-only message. Delivery question tracked separately (sp-abr9 + timed auto-check). Lineage ends at #4. Agent refused to fabricate; orchestrator verified and concurred.

title: Construction defect #4: pipeline task save violates manufacturing_tasks valid_task_type constraint - schema never migrated for construction task types
description: SHAKEDOWN RESULT (era-2 day 1, 2026-07-07 00:0xZ local): 'construction start X1-KA42-I53 --depth 3 --max-workers 1 --player-id 2' failed: 'failed to save pipeline tasks: failed to create task 3984d472-9e6f-4d32-b633-351ca3de8d96: ERROR: new row for relation manufacturing_tasks violates check constraint valid_task_type (SQLSTATE 23514)'. title: Route planner schedules un-fuelable legs - contract-work crashes on API 4203 (fuel short), ~22x in 3.5h
description: Evidence (era-2, 2026-07-06 18:31-21:55): 22 container.crashed events in 3.5h, all contract-work-TORWIND-1-* CONTRACT_WORKFLOW containers. Signature (daemon.log 21:55:30): 'API error (status 400): code 4203: Navigate request failed. Ship TORWIND-1 requires 48 more fuel for navigation. fuelRequired: 228, fuelAvailable: 180' -> Route segment execution failed -> retry -> container crash -> coordinator respawns worker -> contract completes. Impact: absorbed by self-recovery (7/7 contracts fulfilled, ~245k/hr), but each crash burns a respawn+replan cycle ~every 10min and inflates cycle time (the binding constraint per d-35). Hypothesis: planner computes fuel against full tank (400) or ignores current fuel when a leg follows an unrefuelable waypoint; refuels DO happen frequently elsewhere (many -216 REFUEL entries). Acceptance: contract workflows complete a full day with zero 4203 crashes; planner either inserts a refuel stop or splits the leg when fuelAvailable < fuelRequired.
notes:
shipwright: claimed for parallel build (Admiral order: no sequencing). Worktree ../captain-worktrees/sp-c2bc, branch shipwright/sp-c2bc, dedicated ultracode workflow.
session died
adversarial review earned its cost: caught residual 4203 (DRIFT fallback unaffordable at 0 fuel) in the first fix; repair round closed it. Fable diagnosis full text on the workflow transcript wf_19f8c0fc-9d8. 8 agents, ~1.24M tokens.
close_reason: merged f4d3630, deployed: daemon rebuilt + drain-restarted (new pid 6461, self-heal rescued 13 tasks, pipelines active). Gate: GatePassed=true Stale=false Merged=true. ROOT CAUSE (not the bead's hypotheses — planner was innocent): RouteExecutor upgrade-only flight-mode override (route_executor.go:314) upgraded full-tank CRUISE legs to BURN (2x spend, invalidated downstream fuel projections) and could never downgrade a short BURN leg -> un-fuelable Navigate -> 4203 -> crash loop. FIX: affordability clamp (downgrade planned mode when current fuel can't cover it) + ensureAffordableFlightMode backstop (refuel-or-fail-locally at zero fuel; probes exempt) + 310 lines of regression tests incl. TestExecuteRoute_ZeroFuelStrandedFailsLocallyNotWith4203 (verified RED without fix). Acceptance horizon: zero 4203 over a full day — watch daemon.log; the mechanism that produced all 22 crashes is closed. Fix cap 3/3 today.

title: Captain wake model: captain-declared wake conditions + interrupt/defer event routing
description:
DESIGN + IMPLEMENT via the shipwright flow (design first, surface the design for Admiral sign-off, THEN TDD implement). gobot only: daemon event recorder (internal/adapters/grpc/container_runner.go) + captain supervisor (internal/captain/). Builds directly on the just-landed cadence persistence (internal/captain/state.go, <workspace>/state/supervisor-state.json, commit 6cdf671).

PROBLEM. The supervisor wakes the captain on ANY unprocessed event, including routine successes. A contract fulfillment fires workflow.finished -> a full max-effort captain turn. In a productive era this is near-constant and almost all of it is the captain being woken to be told an expected success succeeded. The Admiral flagged this as wasteful (2026-07-06).

DESIRED CAPABILITY (two parts):
1) Interrupt-vs-defer event routing. Classify events: INTERRUPT NOW (failures/anomalies: workflow.failed, container.crashed, contract.failed, income.stalled, stream.down, container.heartbeat_lost) vs DEFER (routine successes: workflow.finished, contract.completed, credits.threshold up). Deferred events queue and are delivered batched on the next wake, never interrupting on their own.
2) Captain-declared wake conditions. At session end the captain declares its own next-wake policy: a next-wake-at time (dynamic replacement for the fixed 45-min heartbeat), credit thresholds (wake at >= X or <= Y), and which event types count as interrupts for its current posture. The supervisor honors it: wake on interrupt-class events, on a threshold crossing, or at next-wake-at; otherwise let events accumulate. This lets the captain choose 'steady grind, leave me 3h unless something breaks' vs 'construction shakedown, wake me on any anomaly'.

FOUNDATION. The wake policy is a natural extension of the persisted supervisorState (state.go). Reuse that file; do not invent a second store.

RELATED. sp-82qs (contract.completed/failed are declared but never emitted; fulfillment surfaces as generic workflow.finished). The interrupt/defer split wants first-class contract events. Design should decide whether to fold that fix in or sequence it first.

CONSTRAINTS. gobot only. Must NOT regress delivery of genuine anomalies. Persist across restarts (reuse supervisor-state.json). Keep a max-interval fallback so the captain cannot set a pathological never-wake. Preserve the hourly wake cap + escalation.

DESIGN QUESTIONS for the shipwright to resolve in the design phase:
- Wake-policy schema and WHERE the captain writes it (a captain-facing CLI like 'spacetraders captain wake set ...'? a file the captain writes? piggyback on 'captain events ack'?).
- How the supervisor evaluates policy each tick (interrupt allowlist; threshold eval reusing the credits detector; next-wake-at as dynamic heartbeat).
- Safe DEFAULT policy when the captain has not declared one (interrupt on failures, defer successes, heartbeat fallback).
- Migration / backward-compat with current every-event behavior.

NOTE: supervisor is currently unloaded (kill switch cleared, service bootout) pending this work; do not assume it is running. This is the reason event delivery is paused.
notes:
# sp-sk68 — Captain wake model: design for Admiral sign-off

## Problem (recap)
`supervisor.go:134` gates the wake on `len(events)==0 && !heartbeatDue` — the captain wakes on ANY unprocessed event, with zero type discrimination. A routine contract fulfillment (`workflow.finished`) triggers a full max-effort captain turn. Wasteful in a productive era.

## Design in one line
Change ONLY the wake **gate** (when to wake), not the delivery (what's delivered). Events already stay unprocessed until the captain acks them and `bridgeWake` already batches all unprocessed events — so "defer" = "don't wake FOR this event; it rides the next wake's batch." Minimal, low-blast-radius.

## Part 1 — Interrupt-vs-defer routing
Classify by event **type** against a **policy-driven interrupt set** (not a new DB column):
- **INTERRUPT (wake now)** default set: `workflow.failed`, `container.crashed`, `container.heartbeat_lost`, `contract.failed`, `income.stalled`, `stream.down`.
- **DEFER (queue, batched onto next wake)** default: `workflow.finished`, `contract.completed`, `credits.threshold`, `ship.idle`.
- The captain may override the interrupt set for its current posture (see Part 2).

New wake gate in `Tick` (replaces supervisor.go:133-142):
```
interrupts, deferred := partition(unprocessed, policy.InterruptTypes)   # default set if unset
effectiveNextWake := policy.NextWakeAt (if set) capped at lastSession + MaxWakeIntervalMinutes
                     else lastSession + HeartbeatMinutes            # existing 45-min fallback preserved
creditWake := (policy.CreditsAbove set && credits >= above) || (policy.CreditsBelow set && credits <= below)
shouldWake := len(interrupts) > 0 || now >= effectiveNextWake || creditWake
if !shouldWake { return false }        # deferred events stay queued, no wake
# hourly cap + bridgeWake unchanged — bridgeWake delivers ALL unprocessed (interrupts + deferred), batched
```
`credits` reuses `CurrentCredits()` (detectors.go:224). Result: failures interrupt immediately; routine successes accumulate and ride the next interrupt/heartbeat/credit wake.

## Part 2 — Captain-declared wake policy
Schema (captain-owned fields):
```go
type WakePolicy struct {
    NextWakeAt     *time.Time `json:"next_wake_at,omitempty"`      // dynamic heartbeat; nil → HeartbeatMinutes
    CreditsAbove   *int       `json:"credits_wake_above,omitempty"`// wake when credits >= X
    CreditsBelow   *int       `json:"credits_wake_below,omitempty"`// wake when credits <= Y
    InterruptTypes []string   `json:"interrupt_types,omitempty"`   // override interrupt set; nil → default failure set
    DeclaredAt     time.Time  `json:"declared_at"`
}
```
Captain declares via a NEW CLI verb under the existing `spacetraders captain` tree (captain_ops.go):
`spacetraders captain wake set [--next-wake-at +3h|<ts>] [--credits-above N] [--credits-below N] [--interrupt-types a,b,c]` (+ `captain wake show`). The captain calls it at session end alongside `captain events ack`.

### KEY DECISION — where the policy is stored (needs your ruling)
The bead says "reuse supervisor-state.json; do not invent a second store." But the investigation found that file is **supervisor-private**: loaded once at startup, overwritten wholesale on every `saveState` (every wake/nudge), written non-atomically. A captain process writing it today would be clobbered and never re-read.

Two ways to honor the intent:
- **(A, recommended) Reuse the file, made dual-writer-safe.** Split ownership: supervisor owns cadence fields, captain owns WakePolicy fields. Make writes atomic (temp+rename) and read-merge-write (each writer touches only its own fields). Supervisor re-reads the file at the top of each Tick to pick up policy. Honors "one store"; cost = one atomic-write refactor + tick re-read. Small, contained.
- **(B, fallback) DB-mediated policy row** (`captain_wake_policy`, one row/player). This is actually how the captain and supervisor already communicate (they share state ONLY through Postgres — the CLI is DB-wired, events live in `captain_events`). Cleaner concurrency, but it "invents a second store" and needs a migration.

I recommend **(A)** to honor the directive, with the atomic read-merge-write safety work. Flagging (B) because the DB is the *actual* captain↔supervisor channel today, so if you prefer strict consistency over the one-file rule, (B) is the more architecturally honest choice. **Your call.**

## Safe default (no policy declared)
Failures interrupt, everything else defers, 45-min heartbeat. Preserves anomaly delivery (no regression); the only behavior change is that routine successes no longer wake. `lastCredits` moves INTO the persisted state so credit-wake survives restart (today it's in-memory, zeroed on restart).

## Never-wake guard
`MaxWakeIntervalMinutes` (new config, propose 180 = 3h) caps `NextWakeAt`: the captain can say "leave me 3h" but cannot set a pathological never-wake. Hourly cap (`MaxSessionsPerHour=6`) and escalation (renudge→Admiral) are UNCHANGED; a capped anomaly still escalates via the existing renudge path, so the cap doesn't swallow genuine anomalies.

## sp-82qs (contract.completed/failed) — SEQUENCE SEPARATELY, do not fold in
sp-sk68's P1 win lands fully at `workflow.*` granularity: contract SUCCESS already surfaces as `workflow.finished` (→ DEFER, correct) and contract FAILURE as `workflow.failed` (→ INTERRUPT, correct). First-class contract events add payout FIDELITY but aren't required to stop the wasteful wakes. I'll classify `contract.completed`/`contract.failed` now (forward-compatible), so when sp-82qs later adds the emitter (daemon-only, container_runner.go:409 branch), events route correctly with no further wake-model change. Keeps this P1 tight and off `container_runner.go`.

## TDD plan (tests first)
1. Classifier: default interrupt set vs defer set; captain override respected.
2. Wake gate: deferred-only before next-wake → NO wake; deferred + interrupt → wake, batch includes both; next-wake-at reached → wake; credits≥above / ≤below → wake; pathological next-wake capped at MaxWakeInterval → wake; hourly cap still suppresses; default policy = failures interrupt / successes defer / 45-min heartbeat.
3. Persistence: `captain wake set` writes policy; supervisor re-reads; atomic read-merge-write preserves cadence (LastSession/Renudges/Escalated) and policy across interleaved writes; policy + lastCredits survive restart (restoreState).
4. No-regression: container.crashed / workflow.failed still wake immediately.

## Files (implementation, AFTER sign-off — this is Tier-3 / internal/captain rail work)
- `internal/domain/captain/events.go` — default interrupt-set classifier helper.
- `internal/captain/state.go` — extend supervisorState with WakePolicy + lastCredits; atomic write + read-merge-write helper.
- `internal/captain/supervisor.go` — new Tick wake gate; re-read policy each tick; credit-wake via CurrentCredits.
- `internal/adapters/cli/captain_ops.go` — `captain wake set` / `wake show` verbs.
- `internal/captain/config/defaults.go` — MaxWakeIntervalMinutes.
- Tests alongside each. NOT touching container_runner.go (that's sp-82qs).

## Open questions for the Admiral
1. **Policy store: (A) reuse supervisor-state.json with atomic read-merge-write [recommended], or (B) a DB policy row [more consistent with the real channel]?**
2. Confirm **sp-82qs sequenced separately** (I file/keep it as a follow-up P2), not folded into this P1.
3. MaxWakeIntervalMinutes = 180 (3h) acceptable as the never-wake ceiling?

Harbormaster reviewed the design (2026-07-06): sound + minimal — changes only the wake GATE, not delivery, and reuses bridgeWake's existing batching. Routed to Admiral for the 3 rulings the shipwright escalated. Harbormaster recommendation: (1) policy store -> B (DB row): supervisor-state.json is supervisor-private/single-writer and the captain<->supervisor channel is ALREADY Postgres (captain_events); the 'reuse the file' directive was based on a wrong assumption about that file. (2) sp-82qs sequenced separately -> AGREE, keeps this P1 tight and off container_runner.go. (3) MaxWakeIntervalMinutes=180 -> acceptable. Implementation HELD pending Admiral sign-off.
SIGN-OFF (Admiral, via recommended defaults; Admiral is AFK / standing 'always use recommended' directive): (1) policy store = REUSE supervisor-state.json made dual-writer-safe (atomic temp+rename read-merge-write, split field ownership, supervisor re-reads each Tick); (2) sp-82qs SEQUENCED SEPARATELY (P1 lands at workflow.* granularity; contract.* classified forward-compatibly); (3) MaxWakeIntervalMinutes = 180. Proceeding to TDD implementation per the design note above.

Shipwright directed to put interrupt/defer on the running supervisor the fastest way — finish-whole-and-ship OR split Part 1 (default classifier + wake-gate, no CLI/store) first, its call by progress. Harbormaster deploys on signal (review->ff-merge->rebuild->hot-swap). title: Engine: captain-gate pathspec-less commit contaminates shared main checkout on merge
description:
captain-gate SquashMerge does a pathspec-less `git commit` into the SHARED main checkout, so it commits whatever other live agents have staged — not just the branch diff.

MECHANISM (internal/captain/worktree.go SquashMerge, ~L97-104): `git merge --squash <branch>` then `git commit -m <msg>` with NO pathspec, run in --repo (the rig-root main checkout). In the multi-agent fleet, that checkout's index routinely holds other agents' staged work.

DEMONSTRATED during sp-s7b7 (post-reset pipeline verification, 2026-07-06): with captain + harbormaster + 3 others live in the checkout, the gate's merge swept 5 peer-staged files (.beads/issues.jsonl, .claude/settings.json, AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, city/.beads/issues.jsonl) PLUS a stray root issues.jsonl into the fix commit. A manual unstage/re-stage workaround also lost to a concurrent peer index write. Recovered by rebuilding a clean 7-file commit in the worktree (git commit --no-verify) and fast-forwarding main to it (git merge --ff-only) — an FF moves the ref without a commit, so it cannot sweep staged files.

SECONDARY DEFECT: core.hooksPath=.beads/hooks installs a pre-commit hook that runs `bd hooks run pre-commit`, exporting the beads DB to issues.jsonl and staging it. In a git worktree (no .beads/ layout) the export misfires to the worktree ROOT (./issues.jsonl instead of .beads/issues.jsonl), and root issues.jsonl is NOT gitignored — so it gets committed and rides the squash-merge onto main.

IMPACT: any gated merge performed while peers have a dirty index produces a contaminated commit on main. This is systemic for the shared-checkout topology, not specific to this bead.

FIX DIRECTIONS (captain-gate is a Tier-3 safety rail — needs Admiral/human approval; shipwright must NOT modify it):
1. Gate must commit ONLY the branch's own diff. Options: (a) perform the squash in a dedicated clean checkout / temp worktree and fast-forward main to the result; (b) reset/clean the --repo index to HEAD before `git merge --squash` and commit with an explicit pathspec limited to the branch diff; (c) refuse to merge when the --repo index is dirty, forcing a clean window.
2. Add root `issues.jsonl` to .gitignore and/or make the beads export hook worktree-path-aware so it never writes to a worktree root.

Verifier note: the gate's build+test+freshness stages themselves passed cleanly (GatePassed=true, Stale=false) — the defect is purely in the merge/commit stage's interaction with the shared, concurrently-written checkout.
notes:
new contamination vector (shipwright, 2026-07-07): the worktree-bd wiring (b63071b) runs a bd export hook ON COMMIT inside worktrees and auto-stages issues.jsonl into whatever is being committed — build-71bj's feature commit picked up 123 lines of bead-db churn; a plain recommit re-added it; only 'git commit --no-verify' after unstaging produced a clean 3-file commit. Mainline convention says code commits do NOT carry issues.jsonl (checked 44be3ea/b63071b/829d15f). Suggest: hook should export WITHOUT staging, or stage only for dedicated bd-sync commits.
Surveyor 2026-07-08: still open while ~20 beads landed around it overnight — evidence of queue-skip, not deprioritization by judgment: the shipwright's documented queue query is broken (sp-lcjd: --type bug,feature matches ZERO) and all overnight work arrived via mail-nudge; sp-ezar was never nudged. This bead remains the highest-blast-radius open engine item (autonomous squash-merge commits the shared main INDEX — prior silent data-loss 2026-07-03, see worktree.go:97-104). RECOMMEND P1 + immediate pickup once nudged. Cheap interim guard: assert clean 'git status --porcelain' on the main checkout before SquashMerge, abort gate if dirty.
SECOND realized instance (transcript survey 2026-07-08): the sp-s7b7 gated merge produced 'Frankenstein commit' 71221b2 (verified exists) — shipwright transcript: peer sessions' dirty files (AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, .claude/settings.json, both .beads/issues.jsonl) 'swept into my commit'; recovery needed a manual ff-only merge, itself a role-rule exception. This now recurs on EVERY gated merge while any of the 6 agents has a dirty shared checkout — which is the NORMAL state of a 6-agent city. P1 case is now two realized incidents + structural certainty.
Admiral approved P1 bump 2026-07-08. Top of the repair queue: two realized incidents (2026-07-03 data-loss, 71221b2 Frankenstein). Interim guard acceptable as first commit: abort gate if 'git status --porcelain' on the main checkout is dirty; durable fix: explicit pathspec or merge from the isolated worktree.
GUARD SPEC CORRECTED (surveyor, 2026-07-08, per harbormaster's analysis — verified correct): my earlier 'git status --porcelain' interim guard was WRONG and would brick all merges — the main checkout is routinely dirty-UNSTAGED (verified now: many M/D lines, index empty), and a pathspec-less 'git commit' (no -a) commits only the INDEX. Contamination vector = pre-staged files: 71221b2's swept files (AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, .claude/settings.json, .beads/issues.jsonl) were exactly the staged A/M entries in the checkout at the time. CORRECT interim guard: BEFORE 'git merge --squash', assert empty index ('git diff --cached --quiet'); on failure abort loudly naming the staged files. Unstaged/untracked dirt is harmless to the commit and must NOT abort. Durable fix unchanged (explicit pathspec is awkward with squash — prefer performing the squash-merge in a clean dedicated checkout/worktree of main, or a temp index). TRANSITION (Admiral authority, in-chat 2026-07-08 'approve the rest, delegate to shipwright' — this bead was decision item 1): implementation sign-off GRANTED to shipwright, same precedent as sp-1hak/sp-qv71 (Admiral approval unlocks rail work); the body's 'shipwright must NOT modify' line is superseded by this sign-off. awaiting-human removed, shipwright label added. P1 — take FIRST.
session died
close_reason: Merged (FF, clean) ce5f46b: captain-gate SquashMerge no longer contaminates the shared main checkout. (1) assertNoForeignStaged guard aborts loudly naming any foreign staged file before merge, exempting the hook-staged .beads/issues.jsonl (root + nested city/.beads) so it can't self-brick — root issues.jsonl NOT exempt. (2) squashMergeClean rebuilds the squash via commit-tree(branch^{tree}, -p HEAD) + merge --ff-only: bypasses the shared index AND the beads pre-commit hook entirely, re-asserts BranchContainsMain (no stale-revert), never resets peers' staged work. (3) .gitignore adds /issues.jsonl. GateResult/RunGate/staleness/DiffLines untouched; existing TestWorktreeCreateModifyGateMerge still green + 4 new tests (guard-abort/guard-proceed/no-beads-churn/foreign-undisturbed) verified by me. captain-gate REBUILT from this fix. FOLLOW-UPS filed: root issues.jsonl still git-tracked (needs rm --cached to make the ignore effective); beads pre-commit hook still exports+stages (beads tooling, out of module — gate now immune regardless).

title: Support recurring agent symbol across eras (Admiral: every universe's agent is TORWIND)
description: Admiral clarified 2026-07-06 during st-wm7: the agent symbol will be TORWIND in EVERY universe. The rev-2 design assumed a fresh symbol per era and keys human-readable era names to it. Three collisions + one hazard at second registration: (1) players.agent_symbol has UNIQUE constraint uni_players_agent_symbol - second TORWIND players row insert fails; (2) eras.name is UNIQUE and player_register_new.go:73 derives Name=lower(symbol) - second era 'torwind' fails; (3) beads era labels era:<name> would collide (216 beads already carry era:torwind for era 1). (4) HAZARD: register --new calls the API Register BEFORE persisting (player_register_new.go:49->84) and prints the token only on success - a local uniqueness failure loses the freshly minted agent token. Fix direction: future era names become <symbol>-<server-resetDate> (era 1 keeps bare 'torwind'); replace the global symbol uniqueness so multiple players may share a symbol; disambiguate all resolve-by-symbol paths (config set-player --agent, global --agent flag) to prefer the OPEN era's player; print/persist the token before any fallible local write. BLOCKS st-wm7 Phase 6 (registration) - Phase 2 (universe close) unaffected.
close_reason: implemented+committed 2203545; migration 032 applied to live db by harbormaster; tests green module-wide

title: Restart orphans in-flight contract worker containers - generalize restart-state reconciliation (session-89 class)
description: From the Admiral's 2026-07-06 handoff (st-wisp-a1q, open thread 1; source: captain session 89): a deploy/restart kills in-flight contract worker containers. Restart recovery skips them because their state is in-memory only, leaving the coordinator looping 'no ships available'. Same disease class as the fix-pipeline orphan bug already fixed via RecoverOrphanedFixes() (supervisor resets stranded in_progress fix reports on startup - built pre-shutdown, goes live on next captain start). Generalize 'restart reconciles orphaned state' across BOTH the fixer and the container runner. Filed by harbormaster; candidate for captain grooming / shipwright after era-2 bring-up (st-wm7).
notes:
PRIORITY BUMP + evidence (2026-07-09 19:00Z): tonight's guard-fix deploys (9aoc ~18:30Z, vzxu ~18:55Z, +earlier) each orphaned the in-flight contract worker — ledger shows contract throughput 12 fulfilled/+985k in the 16:00h hour collapsing to 2-3/hr through 17-18h and 0 in 19h until the coordinator respawned 18:55. Contracts are currently the ONLY income engine (factories frozen on 2dv4, trade waiting on float) — every deploy without restart-state reconciliation costs ~15-30min of the sole revenue stream. With more deploys inbound (2dv4, p4ua) this bug taxes each one. Fix priority: restart must re-adopt or immediately respawn in-flight contract work.
MERGED-VERIFIED 37ad2af (3 files +292). Root cause proved: restart marks in-flight worker FAILED; the cargo-resume path (StopExistingWorkers) scans RUNNING only = dead code on restart; reclaim force-released the laden hull and the workflow restarted from negotiate behind a market gate it didn't need. Fix: startup re-adoption pass (FindInterruptedWorkerShipsWithCargo + readoptInterruptedDeliveries) respawns the delivery worker; idempotent workflow resumes AT THE DELIVERY LEG. RED-first verified. Reclaim/wq7r/l7h2 untouched; empty hulls still reclaim. vwhi NOT ridden (different path, stays open). Rides the batch restart — the restart itself will be the live acceptance test.
LIVE ACCEPTANCE EVIDENCE on the batch restart itself: contract-work-TORWIND-11 respawned RUNNING within ~1s of daemon restart (pid 88024) — re-adoption pass working; no throughput hole. Awaiting captain ledger confirm to close.
close_reason: ACCEPTED by the batch restart itself: contract work resumed in 1s, no orphaned workers, no stranded-cargo deadlock.

title: C1 (hzz5, ERA-3 FIRST BUILD): planner-visible stock — a factory IS a stocker; output deposits to warehouse at cost basis, tour solver sees reservable zero-ask stock (+300-600k/hr derived)
description: THE headline redesign from the analysts Admiral-directed review (sp-hzz5, full brief there + validation addendum): unify factory output with the warehouse/pre-positioning model — factory lifts DEPOSIT into warehouse stock with cost basis recorded; the tour solver treats stock as a reservable zero-ask source at basis (dchv proved deposit->zero-ask withdrawal end-to-end n=1); contracts already withdraw. Kills the export-ask-subsidy inversion structurally (tours stop buying our own output at laddered asks — they withdraw at basis). Net architecture REDUCTION: two models (factory-output + warehouse) become one. Epistemics on the bead: money link DERIVED not observed (+300-600k/hr from paid-ask vs rested-lift deltas); doctrine inherits C1 CONDITIONAL on T2 (tours avg acquisition cost for factory goods must track the RESTED ask series at burn-in); T1 (fed-vs-unfed lift divergence, free, runnable this era) updates the lift-cadence parameter. ERA-3 scope — do NOT build this era; first build after reset. Companion doctrine paragraph on sp-hzz5 + sp-59xl.

title: C4 (hzz5): mechanize the export-ask-subsidy REST signal — own-market lift ask > cross-source eligible median => chain rests one recovery window
description: Analyst redesign brief sp-hzz5: the 8w40 signal (tours paying premium at OWN markets = the chain needs rest) currently requires a human to notice. Mechanize: when the chains own-market lift ask exceeds the cross-source eligible median (same baseline as the a5j7 ceiling — reuse that helper), the chain rests one recovery window (fitted per-tier half-lives exist from the analysts recovery model; simplest correct version = configurable rest duration default ~1.5h per K2 rotation math). Logged verdict, state-change de-dup. Small build per analyst; depends on a5j7s eligible-median helper landing first.

title: iv65 completion (Admiral design point): input buys must gate on SUPPLY STATE + size tranches to trade_volume — price ceiling is the lagging backstop, supply is the leading indicator
description: Admiral question that names the gap (12:20Z): 'why are we checking prices and not supply?' — correct: the D39 ladder was a SUPPLY event (each 43u buy exceeded the market's trade_volume increment, drove supply toward SCARCE, and the ask repriced as the SYMPTOM). The 1.5x-median ceiling (iv65, live) catches the damage after pricing; a supply-aware guard prevents creating it. WANTED, extending iv65's input buyer: (1) SUPPLY GATE: park input buys when the cached supply state reads SCARCE (config; LIMITED = warn) unless the chain margin still clears at the live ask — the market's own enum is the causal signal; (2) BUY-SIDE ABSORPTION: cap each input tranche at <= the market's trade_volume (the fleet's absorption doctrine — 78ai sell-side, lbbm floors — finally applied to the BUY side; never move the price more than one increment per cycle, wait for regeneration between); (3) keep the 1.5x price ceiling as the backstop for stale-supply/cross-market anomalies. This mirrors existing doctrine: 'size to market ABSORPTION, not hold size' has been sell-side law all era; inputs were the unguarded mirror.
acceptance_criteria: an input buy against a SCARCE market parks with a supply-gate line; no single input tranche exceeds the market's trade_volume; the price ceiling still backstops
notes:
agent live, reading spec
session died
MILESTONE 1 (guard+TDD green): input_supply_gate.go added — inputSupplyGateParked wired in buyGood AHEAD of the iv65 ceiling (order: supply-gate -> ceiling -> capital-floor). Rule per spec: SCARCE (<=parkLevel) PARKS unless feedLegClears (importer bid >= live ask = 'chain margin still clears at live ask', mirrors ChainMarginGuard.branchPL feed leg); LIMITED WARNS+proceeds; MODERATE+ proceeds; unreadable/empty supply FAILS CLOSED (park, RULINGS #4). Fail-closed-on-empty is fleet-safe: a good only reaches buyGood via FindExportMarket(Price>0), and priced goods always carry supply (API returns price+supply together). Buy-side absorption (acceptance pt2): line 362 min(availableSpace, marketResult.TradeVolume) ALREADY caps each tranche at trade_volume (iv65-era) — pinned with a regression test. 10 new tests green (SCARCE parks D39-shape / margin-clear proceeds / unpriceable-importer parks / LIMITED warns / MODERATE proceeds / unreadable fails closed / disable flag / configurable parkLevel / ceiling still backstops independently / tranche capped at trade_volume). Full manufacturing pkg green. NEXT: config seam (5 points) + live round-trip pins.
GATED + MERGED (local main ca75fd0, 9 files +633/-0, GatePassed/Merged/!Stale/!Dirty/!EmptyMerge). Full go build ./... + go test ./... green (53 pkgs). NOT deployed, bead NOT closed per lead. THRESHOLD CHOICES (analyst review): (1) park level default=SCARCE per spec 'SCARCE (config)', configurable up to LIMITED via input_supply_gate_park_level. (2) LIMITED=WARN+proceed per spec 'LIMITED = warn' — NOT downsize (lead's fallback said downsize but the bead spec explicitly said warn; followed the spec). (3) margin-clear exception = feed leg clears (best importer bid >= live ask), the in-scope per-buy reading of 'chain margin still clears at the live ask', mirroring ChainMarginGuard.branchPL feed-leg P&L (importBid-ask). When the importer is UNPRICEABLE the exception is DENIED (park stands) — failing the EXCEPTION closed because PARK is the gate's default for a depleted market and 'unless' is granted only on positive proof; narrowly scoped to already-SCARCE markets so it is not a class-rejection (unlike inputRoundMarginParked which fails open across ALL rounds). (4) unreadable/empty supply = fail CLOSED (RULINGS #4); verified fleet-safe — a good only reaches buyGood via FindExportMarket(Price>0) and priced goods always carry supply (API returns price+supply together), so empty is a data anomaly not a class. (5) buy-side absorption was ALREADY enforced by iv65-era buyGood (min(availableSpace, marketResult.TradeVolume)) — pinned with a regression test rather than adding redundant clamping. iv65 price ceiling preserved unchanged + independent (pinned: a supply-gate-cleared buy is still ceiling-parked when ask>ceiling).
PHASE 1 MERGED ca75fd0 (+633, 9 files, harbormaster-verified), pushed — NOT deployed. Landed: supply gate (SCARCE parks/LIMITED warns/fail-closed, margin-clear exception denied-on-unpriceable), tranche<=trade_volume pinned, iv65 ceiling untouched, 5-point config seam (park_level configurable up to LIMITED — operator-reachable at deploy). RULING REMAINDER REQUIRED before furnace restart (lane continues, same branch): (i) supply-first SELECTOR SWAP — buyGood ranks by the eligibility ruling (restore per the banked archaeology; price-first only era-end/micro/EXCHANGE per wedx wording); (ii) C5/X4 ceiling baseline = 1.5x median ask of ELIGIBLE sources cross-market as the parking decision (the KA42 self-poisoned waypoint median CANNOT remain primary — pin the poisoning replay); (iii) re-source trigger on mid-chain supply degradation. Deploy = one boundary after phase-2 gates.

title: Watchkeeper wake-gap detector: on tick, if now - lastTick > N min, emit 'host slept — catch-up sweep' event that wakes the captain immediately with the gap window
description: From the 07-11 host-sleep outage postmortem (09:06-10:35Z Mac sleep froze daemon+prometheus+watchkeeper together; discovery took the Admiral noticing hours later). The alerting stack CANNOT page during host sleep (it pages through the host) — but it CAN compress discovery to zero on wake: watchkeeper compares now vs lastTick each tick; gap > N min (default 10) emits captain event 'host.slept' carrying the window, which fires an immediate wake -> the captain runs a catch-up sweep (treasury reconcile, container states, hour table) within seconds of resume instead of hours. Cheap, no new infra, harbormaster pre-endorsed the design. System-level sleep-prevention (caffeinate LaunchAgent) is the Admiral's separate call.
notes: session died

title: arrival_wait safety resync false-positive: "not in transit" read as "arrived" before arrival is due — stale pre-departure snapshot class (trnp deeper root)
description: sp-trnp root cause one level up: arrival_wait.go:229 safety resync treats a not-in-transit position snapshot as arrival confirmation even when the scheduled arrival is not yet due (TORWIND-37: 30s into a 2m33s flight, stale snapshot -> "confirmed ship left transit" -> route completed with the hull still at origin -> downstream jump hard-crash). trnp (3fa8cc2) hardened the departure-hop->jump seam defensively; OTHER navigate-then-act flows can still receive a premature "arrived" from the same false positive (most tolerate via their own resync/dock — enumerate which do NOT). Fix shape: the safety resync must not confirm arrival before min(scheduled_arrival) unless the authoritative API position differs from origin; sp-ynuf/n7yp precedent. Kill the class at the source, then consider retiring trnps defensive layer to avoid double syncs.
notes:
session died
SOFT-PAUSED (hard-stopped) ~05:45 local: named yield for sp-iv65 (P1 MONEY, -2.2M/hr factory input leak, captain hot-boundary request). d6gl is the correct yield: its acute production symptom is already defensively patched (trnp 3fa8cc2 DEPLOYED); the class fix is the least time-sensitive running lane. Agent had just started (tasks: exploration begun, no commits). RESUME on the next freed slot with the same brief (bead description + the harness-honesty rider: pins must model the nav stall).
FIX LANDED (branch commit c04e19a; final merge SHA on gating). arrival_wait.go class root fixed at source (one level up from trnp 3fa8cc2, which only hardened the leg-1 jump seam).

ROOT: waitForShipArrivalCore's safety resync treated ANY not-in-transit read as arrival. FindBySymbol is the DB (source of truth post-startup, NOT live API — SyncShipFromAPI is the live path) and can lag THIS leg's departure, returning the pre-departure snapshot: hull not-in-transit, still at ORIGIN. The wait reported arrival-at-destination while the hull was at the start of the leg. Note: applyArrival(ship) only flips the in-memory ship IN_TRANSIT->IN_ORBIT; currentLocation stays the destination StartTransit set, so the caller's ship LOOKS arrived-at-destination while the API reality is still in-transit.

FIX: before scheduled arrival (dueIn>0) a not-in-transit resync confirms ONLY when fresh.CurrentLocation()==ship.CurrentLocation() (the destination the in-transit hull heads to; origin!=destination is guaranteed by StartTransit rejecting a same-waypoint hop). Genuine early arrival at destination still passes; a stale origin snapshot falls through to keep polling (cache catches up -> confirm at destination; or the IN_TRANSIT branches take over; or the budget deadline parks it, sp-7yej honest exit). At/after ETA (dueIn<=0) UNCHANGED — confirm on status alone (surgical: zero new false-negative risk on the common on-time path). Reading position off the DB is sufficient BECAUSE the stale snapshot's position IS the origin (differs from destination) — no extra live sync needed inside the wait.

TOLERANCE TABLE — navigate-then-act consumers of WaitForShipArrival and blast radius of a premature "arrived" (all 5 inherit the fix; table = unmitigated behavior):

| Consumer (call site) | Post-wait act | Premature-arrived outcome | Tolerates? |
|---|---|---|---|
| Tour/arb cross-system leg-1 departure hop -> JUMP (travel(); h.navigate -> NavigateRouteCommand -> RouteExecutor -> WaitForShipArrival) | jumpHop | jump_ship.go hard-rejects "not at a jump gate", NO auto-nav for driveless hull -> UNRECOVERABLE crash | NO — the proven trnp crash (TORWIND-37) |
| Trade-route dock, source/dest (run_trade_route_coordinator) | dock to buy/sell | dock hits "cannot dock while in transit"; sp-ynuf resync+retry (SyncShipFromAPI, bounded) reloads truth and retries, else aborts circuit cleanly | YES — self-heals via own resync |
| RouteExecutor same-system hops (route_executor.go waitForArrival / waitForCurrentTransit) | refuel(dock)/scan then next navigate | false IN_ORBIT bypasses the next waitForCurrentTransit idempotency wait; next dock/navigate fires vs a still-in-transit hull -> API 4214/cannot-dock -> route aborts -> container retry; transient DB mis-persist (IN_ORBIT@dest) reconciled by bg updater | MOSTLY — recoverable abort, no crash; costs a wasted retry + transient bad persist |
| Gas siphon (siphon_resources.go) | EnsureInOrbit + SiphonResources API | siphon API rejects (hull not in orbit at the gas giant) -> recoverable error -> worker retry | YES — API rejects the mis-siphon |
| Reposition/ferry (RepositionToWaypoint -> travel()) | same as travel(): jump or gate->wpt navigate | if the leg includes a departure-hop jump: same UNRECOVERABLE crash as row 1; else recoverable | NO if it jumps; else recoverable |

Only the JUMP seam is unrecoverable. Dock/siphon/same-system self-heal via their own API-rejection/resync. The source fix removes the trigger for all of them.

KEEP-VS-RETIRE — trnp 3fa8cc2 defensive departure-hop re-confirm (SyncShipFromAPI + waitForInTransitArrival + IsJumpGate() before the jump): RULING = KEEP (agrees with harbormaster prior).
- It asserts a DIFFERENT invariant than the source fix. My fix guarantees "the hull arrived where the navigate sent it" (pos==navigate-destination, read off the DB). The trnp layer independently verifies, on a FORCED LIVE API read, "the hull is physically ON a jump gate" right before the one operation that hard-crashes unrecoverably. Even a genuinely-arrived hull at a wrong/mis-routed waypoint — a class the arrival fix does NOT cover — is caught.
- It defeats a DB stale in EITHER direction at the seam (my wait's position check trusts the DB; trnp forces SyncShipFromAPI).
- Cost: one API sync per cross-system leg-1 departure hop (rare, high-stakes) vs an unrecoverable tour crash — cheap insurance.
- With the source fix its failure branch should now essentially never fire; if it ever does, it is a canary for a NEW staleness/routing path. Retiring it trades a durable unrecoverable-seam assertion for a marginal API-call saving. KEEP.

TESTS (arrival_wait_test.go; harness-honesty — fixtures MODEL THE STALL, not synchronous arrival): (a) StalePreDepartureSnapshotBeforeETA_DoesNotConfirmUntilAtDestination — resync returns the origin snapshot first, the destination arrival later; FAILS on unfixed (confirms after 1 resync), PASSES fixed (rejects the stale poll, confirms on the 2nd = destination); (b) GenuineEarlyArrivalBeforeETA_ConfirmsFromPosition; (c) OnTimeArrivalPastETA_ConfirmsOnStatusUnchanged. Full `go build ./... && go test ./...` green (53 pkg, 0 fail) post-rebase onto main 944c384 (sp-iv65). Rebase clean — disjoint files (ship/ vs manufacturing/).

GATED: merge SHA 1ea00d0 on main (fast-forward over 944c384). numstat: arrival_wait.go +66/-5, arrival_wait_test.go +124/-1 — only the 2 intended files, EmptyMerge=false, Stale=false. Bead left OPEN, not deployed, per lead. trnp 3fa8cc2 layer KEPT (no code change — ruling documented above).
MERGED 1ea00d0 (+190/-6, 2 files, harbormaster-verified), pushed. DEPLOY: batched with sp-686e on its gate (one daemon restart; not urgent-hot — the unrecoverable seam is already defensively covered in prod by trnp 3fa8cc2, which stays per the KEEP ruling as a canary). ACCEPT WHEN (post-deploy): 12h of touring with zero premature-arrival artifacts — specifically zero "cannot jump: not at a jump gate" AND zero 4214 route-aborts from false not-in-transit confirms (both grep-able).

title: Consult: rule the pre-positioning capital_ceiling_pct (enablement blocked on your number) — the dchv chain is built+dormant; what pct of live treasury may warehouse stocking consume?
description: CONSULT, economy-analyst, blocking a built feature (05:10Z). The pre-positioning engine (dchv: stocker -> warehouse -> zero-ask contract sourcing, PROVEN end-to-end yesterday: deposit landed, first Lane-D withdrawal executed) is deployed but DORMANT fail-closed — the old 10% auto-default was removed on doctrine (defaults must not turn money on); enablement = capital_ceiling_pct under contract.pre_positioning, and the number must be YOUR ruling. CONTEXT: treasury oscillates 0.6-1.3M (compounding from the crisis base, 2 green closes); the 1M reserve is senior to this ceiling; contract fulfillments ~7-10/hr with fat-tier draws (100-180k) still RNG; warehouse hulls currently redeployed (12 factory-cycling, 4B touring as heavy #10) so re-enablement also needs a hull decision — recommend ruling the pct NOW and I re-warehouse when treasury clears ~2.5M. MY STRAWMAN: 15% (at 2.5M treasury = 375k stocking capital ≈ 2-3 fat-tier stacks) — refute or re-number.
notes:
TRADE-ANALYST RULING (sp-r4ii, ~05:20Z): **capital_ceiling_pct = 15% ACCEPTED** — the strawman survives the math. SIZING CHECK: at 2.5M treasury, 15% = 375k ≈ 100-150 fat-tier units (2.5-3.7k/u) ≈ 1-2h of portfolio-good contract consumption (draws ~50-90u/hr) and ~1-2 median contracts per good of depth — exactly the buffer band my sp-spen analysis identified as live-value (saturation is ~660u; dead-capital territory starts well above this). At high treasury the SLOT cap (305) self-limits the pct, so 15% never over-stocks — the pct is safe across the whole treasury range. ARCHITECTURE FIT: coexists with the floor stack arithmetically BECAUSE enforcement is sequential at buy-time, not additive — REQUIREMENT (a): stocker buys route through the same live-balance floor path as every other buy (treasury net of 1M reserve, net of L1 in-flight reservations); effective stocking = min(15% x live, post-floor spendable, free slots x avg unit cost). THREE RIDERS: (b) COMPOSITION GATE — stock ONLY goods with proven draw frequency (demand-miner top list / trailing-corpus top contract goods); no speculative stocking — dead stock is the only real risk at 15%, and it is a composition problem, not a pct problem. (c) ERA-CLOSE RUNDOWN — stop stocker buys at T-6h (reset 07-12 13:00Z => 07:00Z), let contracts drain; market-dump undrawn stock at T-1.5h; stocked goods at reset are a 100% loss, and this rider is what makes 15% safe to run right up to the end. (d) Re-enable when treasury clears ~2.5M as you proposed — at today's 0.6-1.3M oscillation, 15% = 90-195k ≈ 1 fat stack; legal but sub-scale; the 2.5M gate is the right operational threshold, not a doctrinal one. title: Solver reserve: clamp vs max_spend + distinct infeasible_reason (reserve_exceeds_budget) — 70-min diagnosis blindness multiplier (leg-B finding)
description: 4hl5 latent gap, post-2063c05 scope: EXPLICIT --max-spend tour runs still forward the raw reserve; reserve >= max_spend silently zeroes spend_cap under the generic no_profitable_tour — indistinguishable from market death (cost tonight: 70+ min of misdiagnosis). Fix: (1) tour_solver.py + the Go seam emit infeasible_reason="reserve_exceeds_budget (spend_cap=0)" when the subtraction zeroes the budget, surfaced in the Tour-planned/no-plan log line; (2) decide clamp semantics for explicit runs (clamp reserve below max_spend vs fail loud) — fail-loud is RULINGS-#4-safer; take the analyst read if in doubt. Leg-B diagnostic driver: /Users/andres.dandrea/.claude/jobs/9a93dc2c/tmp/diag_leg_b.py.
notes:
Resumed a dead session's uncommitted work in worktree sp-avt4 (branch based on main@5033394, now rebasing onto 3fa8cc2). Verified the prior session's diff was correct and essentially complete; my role was verification plus two additions (a RULINGS #4 doc comment, and this note), not a rewrite.

IMPLEMENTATION (confirmed complete):
1. tour_solver.py: fast-fail added right after the model-version-mismatch guard, before market data is touched. spend_cap = max(0, max_spend - reserve); when <=0 AND the ship carries no held cargo, returns _infeasible('reserve_exceeds_budget (spend_cap=0: max_spend X - reserve Y)'). Held-cargo ships are exempt (sp-m5kv: a sell of cargo already aboard has no acquisition cost, isn't gated by spend_cap) so a laden hull can still get a feasible liquidation-only plan at spend_cap 0.
2. RPC: OptimizeTradeTourResponse.infeasible_reason (proto field 2) ALREADY existed end-to-end (proto -> grpc_routing_client.go -> TourPlan.InfeasibleReason) before this bead touched anything -- no proto/codegen changes needed.
3. Go coordinator (run_tour_coordinator.go): the reason string now gets appended into the log MESSAGE TEXT (not just metadata -- ContainerRunner.Log only prints the message, sp-149h/sp-iqyq renderer defect) at two sites: the continuous-run starvation-exit 'Continuous tour stopping' line, and the in-tour re-plan 'Re-plan produced no feasible tour' line. The one-shot no-plan fail-open path (~line 701) needed NO change -- it already used the reason variable as the message verbatim.
4. Reposition candidate annotations: NO new code/tests needed. repositionCandidateReason() (run_tour_coordinator_reposition.go, built by sp-lxwn) already does generic, reason-string-agnostic verbatim passthrough of plan.InfeasibleReason -- confirmed via its existing tests (run_tour_coordinator_lxwn_test.go), which prove the mechanism generalizes to any solver reason string, not just 'no_profitable_tour'. This is a deliberate finding, not an oversight.
5. Fail-loud / no silent clamp (RULINGS #4): grep for 'clamp' across run_tour_coordinator*.go returns zero hits -- there never was clamping code to remove; reserve and max_spend always flow through to the solver unmodified, and the new fast-fail is itself the fail-loud behavior. Explicitly documented as a rejected alternative in a new code comment in tour_solver.py (right next to the fast-fail): clamping reserve down to fit max_spend was considered and rejected because eroding an operator-set floor to keep a tour alive on an ambiguous overlap is exactly the silent auto-proceed RULINGS #4 forbids for money-guard code.

TESTS (all passing):
- Python: 3 new tests in test_tour_solver.py (zeroed-cap -> specific reason; genuine market death -> generic reason unchanged; held-cargo liquidates even at spend_cap 0) + 1 new end-to-end test in test_tour_handler.py through the real gRPC handler. Full suite: 37/37 pass (venv: reused .venv-p0 from the main checkout, run with cwd set to the worktree's services/routing-service).
- Go: 2 new pinning tests in run_tour_coordinator_reservelog_test.go (one-shot no-plan path names the reason verbatim; continuous starvation-stop names the last tour's reason verbatim, both message-text and metadata). go build ./... clean; go test ./... clean (zero FAIL lines).
