title: VERIFY+FIX: yqx4 pct stamp gap on one launch path — container_ops_tour.go:88 comment says pct=0→40%% floor, code only stamps when pct>0 (leg-B finding)
description: sp-4hl5 leg-B diagnosis found a comment/code mismatch: container_ops_tour.go:88 claims the 0/absent pct resolves to the 40 default, but the stamp is gated on pct>0 — so at the live config (no pct key) THAT path may run the ABSOLUTE 1M buy-time floor (deadlock class yqx4 exists to prevent). CONTRADICTING evidence: 6x counter-cyclical engagement INFO lines at 23:51 + a 149k tour buy clearing at 614k balance at 03:31Z prove the proportional floor engages on the daemon-launch path. VERIFY FIRST which paths stamp (launch vs recovery/adoption vs CLI), enumerate with tests (guard-rejects-a-class lesson: DB-enumerate affected hulls per path), then fix the gapped path + the comment. Money-guard adjacent: fail-closed semantics must be preserved.
notes:
session died
VERIFICATION COMPLETE — NO GAP FOUND. Comment-only fix applied (container_ops_tour.go:88); no behavior change.

ROOT CAUSE of the leg-B "mismatch" reading: two different resolution points were conflated.
(a) container_ops_tour.go:88 persists the RAW configured pct (0 too) into launch config.
(b) command_factory_registry.go's buildTourCoordinatorCommand (the ONE place tour_run
    commands are constructed in production) calls resolveReserveTreasuryPct(cfg.OptionalInt(
    "working_capital_reserve_treasury_pct", 0)) at build time — 0/absent/negative -> 40.
(c) run_tour_coordinator.go:558's `if cmd.WorkingCapitalReserveTreasuryPct > 0 { ctx = ... }`
    only ever SEES the value AFTER (b) has already resolved it, so in production it is
    always >0 and always stamps. The >0 guard reads false only for a command built directly
    in a test (bypassing the registry) — exactly how sp-agzj/sp-ggk2 absolute-floor-only
    assertions keep working. Confirmed by grep: RunTourCoordinatorCommand{ and
    RunFactoryCoordinatorCommand{ each have exactly ONE non-test construction site in the
    whole repo (command_factory_registry.go:900 and :744) — there is no production path that
    can reach the Handle()-time guard with an unresolved pct.

TRUTH TABLE (path -> command type -> builder -> resolves 0/absent->40? -> stamps ctx?):
| Fresh launch (container_ops_tour.go:StartTourRun)              | tour_run                 | buildTourCoordinatorCommand (via buildCommandForType)                     | YES | YES | NO GAP |
| Restart recovery (daemon_server.go:recoverContainer)            | tour_run                 | same buildTourCoordinatorCommand, same seam as launch                    | YES | YES | NO GAP |
| CLI/gRPC direct (daemon_service_impl.go:StartTourRun)           | tour_run                 | hardcodes pct=0 by design -> same buildTourCoordinatorCommand            | YES | YES | NO GAP |
| trade_fleet_coordinator relay (LaunchTour -> StartTourRun)      | tour_run                 | pure pass-through of raw value -> same buildTourCoordinatorCommand       | YES | YES | NO GAP |
| trade_route circuit (bp6f)                                      | trade_route              | buildTradeRouteCoordinatorCommand — struct has NO pct field              | N/A (by design) | NO (deliberate) | CONFIRMED stays unstamped |
| Fresh launch (container_ops_goods.go:StartGoodsFactory)         | goods_factory_coordinator| buildGoodsFactoryCoordinatorCommand (+ resolveManufacturingConfig inject)| YES | YES | NO GAP |
| Restart recovery                                                 | goods_factory_coordinator| same builder, same seam                                                  | YES | YES | NO GAP |
| manufacturing_coordinator (launch + recovery)                    | manufacturing_coordinator| buildManufacturingCoordinatorCommand — struct has NO pct field, NO floor enforcement anywhere in the purchaser | N/A | N/A | Pre-existing, already documented as a separate follow-up; not a yqx4 regression (no enforcement to have a gap in) |
| arb_run / idle-arb                                                | arb_run                  | buildArbCoordinatorCommand — struct has NO pct field (absolute WorkingCapitalReserve only) | N/A | N/A | Out of scope for yqx4 (never claimed to have the proportional floor); no gap |
| lookback/reposition buy (run_tour_coordinator_lookback.go)       | (inside tour_run Handle) | inherits ctx from Handle/execute, never reconstructs it                  | inherits | inherits (stamped at line 558 before any buy) | NO GAP |

Also checked: resolveReserveTreasuryPct's <=0 check receives cfg.OptionalInt(...,0), whose
fallback (0) fires identically for "key absent" and "key present but fails intValue
coercion" — both correctly land on the 40% default, so OptionalInt (vs PresentOrFailInt) is
the right choice for this specific knob: unlike working_capital_reserve (sp-ggk2, where a
malformed value silently defaulting to a WRONG low floor was the bug), a malformed pct
defaulting to the same 40% "no captain override" default carries no silent-wrong-value risk.

FIX APPLIED: reworded the container_ops_tour.go:88 comment to name
buildTourCoordinatorCommand/resolveReserveTreasuryPct explicitly and to state outright that
Handle()'s >0 gate is therefore always true in production — closing the exact gap in
understanding that produced the leg-B "mismatch" reading. No functional/behavioral change.
go build ./... and go test ./... both green after the change.
close_reason: VERIFIED NO GAP (10-path truth table on bead): every production tour/factory build path resolves pct 0/absent->40 at the registry seam BEFORE the Handle-time >0 gate — the gate is always true in production; leg-B conflated the raw persist site with the resolved build site. bp6f circuit + arb correctly unstamped by design; manufacturing_coordinator purchaser has NO floor enforcement at all (pre-existing, tracked separately — flagging in close for visibility). Comment-only fix merged 034bba2 (+8/-3), pushed. Side-finding: OptionalInt fallback semantics are correct for pct (malformed->40 = no-override, no silent-wrong-value risk unlike ggk2 class).

title: Stranded-hull escape: TORWIND-2C dark at X1-PD21 (gate-inaccessible, zero durable adjacency) — needs captain-authority extraction now + an auto-escape/alert mechanism
description: Post-4hl5 residual: 2C sits at PD21 where the gate is probe-backed-off (ikx1 markers, attempt_count=2) and gate_edges has NO durable adjacency for the origin — both discovery paths correctly return empty, so the hull can never self-reposition. exploration done. title: Pre-positioning parked at capital ceiling 0 — deposit funnel fail-closed WARNs every tick since deploy (handoff: one fix from live)
description: Since the 2026-07-10 23:50 restart, tour containers WARN repeatedly: "Pre-positioning verdict: 0 deposit candidate(s) — capital ceiling unreadable or zero (fail closed, RULINGS #4) ... ceiling=0(known=true) free=0 ... warehouse=none". 14 lines in first 5 min across hulls (2B, 39...). Behavior is CORRECT per RULINGS #4 (fail closed), but the feature is dead and the WARN spams. sp-59xl handoff calls pre-positioning "one fix from live" — this ceiling looks like that fix. Needs: (1) identify the capital-ceiling source (config key never set? computed from treasury?), (2) the enablement decision is captain/analyst territory (money), engineering delivers the seam + a calmer parked-state log (WARN once per container, not per tick). NOT dchv (still open/undispatched) — this is an already-deployed surface.
notes:
session died
DIAGNOSIS (sp-13tl, worktree sp-13tl). WHICH STAGE BINDS: **capital**, not the funnel. BuildDepositCandidates (tour_deposit_candidates.go:162-166) returns at its FIRST guard (!ceilingKnown || ceilingCredits<=0, reason 'capital ceiling unreadable or zero') BEFORE the warehouse lookup / free-space read / demand-miner call. So free=0, miner_rows=0, stock_eligible=0, after_whitelist=0 in the verdict are ARTIFACTS of that early return — the funnel is never executed, not observed empty. miner_rows=0 does NOT mean the miner is empty; it means the miner was never called.

WHY ceiling=0(known=true): depositCapitalCeiling (run_tour_coordinator.go:1590) = min(pct%*credits, credits-reserve) floored at 0. Live config (gobot/config.yaml): contract.pre_positioning.enabled=true, capital_ceiling_pct UNSET (=> pct defaults to defaultDepositCeilingPct=10), and trade_fleet.working_capital_reserve=1000000 (restored tonight, sp-1278, config.yaml:150-151). reserve threads execute:518 -> ... -> depositCandidates. Shared agent treasury is currently at/below 1M, so credits-1M <= 0 => the junior-to-reserve clamp floors the ceiling to 0. known=true because GetAgent SUCCEEDED — the zero is a real computed value, not a read error.

ROOT DESIGN FLAW: capital_ceiling_pct defaults to 10 when unset = 'a default that turns money movement ON'. With enabled=true, the feature would auto-fund 10% of treasury into deposits the moment treasury exceeds the reserve, with NO analyst having ruled the ceiling. Today it is parked only by the accident of treasury<1M reserve, and it WARNs every re-plan while parked (the spam). Pre-tonight (50k reserve) the same treasury would have made the ceiling positive = money ON.

FIX (implementing, TDD): (a) tour ceiling becomes an explicit enablement — depositCeilingPct<=0 => PARKED (fail closed, dormant), NOT auto-10%; a positive capital_ceiling_pct authorizes it. Stocker (separate explicitly-launched hull, its test relies on the 10% default) + shared defaultDepositCeilingPct const left untouched. (b) parked-state log de-duped per container (mutex-guarded map on the shared handler; mirrors ae64f03 per-hull map + ikx1 per-tick->state-change), levels mirror the stocker precedent (dormant/ceiling-0 => INFO once, unreadable/unwired => WARNING once). BuildDepositCandidates only called when ceiling>0 & known, so its funnel verdict fires only with real capital, never the parked spam.
IMPLEMENTED + MERGED to local main SHA 277faca (numstat +476/-39 across 5 files: run_tour_coordinator.go 158, config.yaml.example 12, contract.go 11, + 2 new test files 282/52). Gate: GatePassed=true Merged=true Stale=false Dirty=false EmptyMerge=false (full go build ./... && go test ./... green). NOT deployed, live config.yaml NOT touched, bead left open per instructions.

WHICH STAGE BINDS (answer): **capital**, unambiguously. The funnel is never evaluated — BuildDepositCandidates short-circuits at the ceiling guard before the warehouse/miner run, so miner_rows=0 etc. are early-return artifacts, not an empty upstream.

CHANGE: (a) depositCandidates now gates the CAPITAL stage in the handler: depositCeilingPct<=0 => PARKED (dormant, fail closed) instead of the old auto-10% default; unreadable balance / treasury<=reserve also park; BuildDepositCandidates is only reached with ceiling>0 & known. depositCapitalCeiling returns (0,true) on pct<=0 (belt-and-suspenders, no default substitution). Stocker + shared defaultDepositCeilingPct const untouched (stocker is explicitly launched; its 10% default + test intact). (b) parked verdict de-duped once per container per distinct state via a mutex-guarded map on the shared handler (mirrors ae64f03 per-hull map + sp-ikx1 per-tick->state-change); levels mirror the stocker (dormant/ceiling-0 => INFO, unreadable/unwired => WARNING). Line prefix changed from 'Pre-positioning verdict:' to 'Pre-positioning parked:' for the capital-stage (greppable, distinct from the funnel verdict; note for sjxi consumers).

LIVE END-STATE after this merge+restart: config.yaml has pre_positioning.enabled=true and NO capital_ceiling_pct => CapitalCeilingPct=0 => tour pre-positioning PARKED, ONE INFO line per hull ('Pre-positioning parked: pre-positioning dormant — no capital ceiling configured ...'), no per-tick WARN spam, zero money movement (fail closed).

=== ENABLEMENT RUNBOOK (captain, once the analyst rules the ceiling %) ===
1. Edit /Users/andres.dandrea/IdeaProjects/cities/spacetraders/gobot/config.yaml. Under the existing
     contract:
       pre_positioning:
         enabled: true
   add one line (indented 4 spaces, sibling of enabled):
         capital_ceiling_pct: <N>
   VALUE SHAPE: positive integer = percent of LIVE treasury the tour deposits may tie up (e.g. 10 = up to 10%). It is held JUNIOR to trade_fleet.working_capital_reserve (currently 1000000), so the effective ceiling is min(N%*treasury, treasury-reserve) floored at 0 — it only becomes positive once treasury EXCEEDS the 1M reserve. Also junior to the sp-w3he cross-container cap.
2. RESTART the daemon (sp-ts82 live-config: SetPrePositioning reads cfg.Contract.PrePositioning at every boot; an already-running daemon does NOT pick it up until restart).
3. Confirm: the per-hull 'Pre-positioning parked: ... dormant' INFO stops; once treasury>reserve, tours begin offering deposit candidates (the funnel verdict 'Pre-positioning verdict: N deposit candidate(s)' resumes). To PARK again: remove the key (or set 0) + restart.
SEAM VERIFIED EXECUTABLE by round-trip pin tests: contract_prepos_test.go (config.yaml capital_ceiling_pct:N -> Config.Contract.PrePositioning.CapitalCeilingPct==N; absent -> 0) + run_tour_coordinator_prepos_ceiling_test.go (depositCeilingPct=0 -> parked once; >0 & funded -> funnel entered). main.go:957 passes pp.CapitalCeilingPct into SetPrePositioning (direct int, no transform).

FOLLOW-UP (noted, not done): the STOCKER (dedicated hull) still auto-applies the 10% default and logs its 'Stocker verdict' without de-dup — out of scope for sp-13tl (its cadence is not the spam source), but the same enablement principle could apply if a captain wants the stocker parked-by-default too. Also: BuildDepositCandidates' own funnel verdict (no-warehouse/full/filtered) is NOT de-duped — it only fires post-enablement (ceiling>0) and is arguably wanted then; de-dup it if it becomes noisy.
MERGED 277faca (harbormaster-verified +476/-39, 5 files), pushed. DIAGNOSIS BANKED: capital stage binds; funnel zeros were early-return artifacts. Latent flaw fixed: 10%% auto-default with enabled=true = a default that turns money ON — now explicit enablement (pct<=0 -> PARKED fail-closed; positive contract.pre_positioning.capital_ceiling_pct authorizes, junior to reserve). DEPLOY: batched daemon boundary (with ae64f03 backoff + e01f7da flowfeed; holding for sp-x8i5 gate, ship without it if >45min). ACCEPT WHEN (post-deploy): ONE "Pre-positioning parked:" INFO per hull, zero per-tick WARN spam, zero deposit legs until the captain flips the config key. Enablement runbook on bead — the analyst owes the pct ruling (route via captain).
DEPLOYED 02:03 boundary. LIVE ACCEPTANCE MET: 10 "Pre-positioning parked:" INFO (one per hull) + 1 WARN total since restart (was 14+/5min per hull). Feature dormant fail-closed; enablement = captain config key per runbook.

title: Reposition candidate discovery returns EMPTY from DP51 ('no jump-reachable candidate with cached market data') while direct-gate neighbor GQ92 sits 1-min fresh — origin-specific discovery failure (canary finding #3)
description: Watch canary evidence (st-wisp-3i8ls): a canary tour at DP51 3-struck correctly, then 'Reposition ranking' found NO candidates — 'no jump-reachable candidate system with cached market data'. DB FACTS (harbormaster-verified): gate_edges holds DP51<->GQ92 BOTH directions, under_construction=f; GQ92 market_data = 158 rows, newest 1 MINUTE old (abundantly inside the 75-min lxwn filter). DISCRIMINATOR: discovery WORKED tonight from ZC66 (ranked PA3) and UQ16 — so the path is origin-sensitive, not globally broken. SUSPECTS for the lane (check in order, replay from DP51 like lxwn): (a) era_id scoping — gate_edges carries era_id; if DP51's edges persist from an earlier sync/era while the reader filters current-era, they vanish (the k7q5 lesson: era-scoping bugs hide in exactly this shape); (b) the jump-reachability check needing an origin GATE WAYPOINT property (gate_waypoint column / charting state) that DP51's row lacks or the hull's position fails; (c) direction-sensitivity in the graph read (system_symbol vs connected_system) combined with which row direction DP51 got; (d) the candidate market-data read scoped to a snapshot that excludes GQ92 for a non-freshness reason. FIX + a permanent log improvement: when discovery returns empty, LOG WHY per rejected neighbor (no-edge / era-mismatch / no-gate-waypoint / stale-data) — 'no candidates' cost a canary flight to diagnose; the reason should be free. Regression: replay-shaped test from the DP51 origin with the real edge+fresh-data shape.

title: Capture out-of-horizon exotic lanes (~200-400k/hr): multi-hop travel model OR long-haul arb path
description: Follow-up to sp-mtvg. sp-mtvg PROVED the exotic 'blind spot' is a 1-gate-hop DISCOVERY horizon shared by the tour coordinator (tourSystems) AND the autonomous arb scanner (scanLanes -> neighborSystems): source and sink of LASER_RIFLES/HOLOGRAPHICS/QUANTUM_DRIVES sit 3+ gate hops apart, so neither optimizer ever sees both in one snapshot. sp-mtvg added observability only (tour_candidates_dropped_total{reason=counterparty_system_unreachable}) — it does NOT capture the value. Capturing it is Admiral-level architecture and was intentionally deferred: the tour solver's _make_travel_fn prices EVERY inter-system crossing as ONE gate hop (2*GATE_HOP+JUMP_COOLDOWN), so simply widening allowed_systems beyond 1 hop mis-prices a 3-hop lane ~3x and corrupts cph selection; and MAX_TOUR_SYSTEMS=2 is an explicit Admiral decision. Options: (a) give the solver a real multi-hop travel matrix (gate-graph path cost) so the horizon can widen safely; (b) a dedicated long-haul single-good arb path not bound by neighborSystems (directed like the p4ua one-shot, but autonomous, lane-selected by spread). Watch the new counter to size the leak before choosing. Evidence: bd remember sp-mtvg-pinned-replay-proven.

title: Tour solver: look-back loading to kill deadhead jumps - 34% of cross-system transitions fly EMPTY (31/90 in 6h); HU21->UQ16 corridor 7/7 empty despite salable exports
description: Trade-analyst measurement (2026-07-11 ~01:20Z, Admiral-directed optimizer review): heavies' system transitions over 6h: 59/90 arrived loaded (first action=SELL), 31/90 arrived EMPTY (first action=BUY) = 34% deadhead rate. Worst corridors: HU21->UQ16 7 empty/0 loaded (HU21-D46/D47/E50 export parts/plating/adv_circ/assault_rifles that UQ16+downstream import!); UU57->ZC66 7/6; JP61->UQ16 4/5. MECHANISM GAP: solver appears to plan each system's manifest on arrival rather than loading departure-system exports for downstream sinks. FIX: at jump-planning time, enumerate departure-system EXPORT rows x (destination + next-hop) IMPORT rows as manifest candidates (look-back loading); even a modest 150k manifest on half the empty jumps ~ +300-500k/hr fleet-wide. SECOND-ORDER: string sequencing - chain source->sink->source topologies (ZC66->UU57 works, 10 loaded/4 empty) over radial out-and-back. Test corridor for acceptance: HU21->UQ16 empty-rate drops from 100% to <30%. Companion evidence on sp-mtvg (good-level blind spot) - same review, same solver.
notes:
GATED SILENTLY (report crossing): MERGED 177c491, already on origin. Pre-verified from the repo per discipline; full reconciliation on the agent's report. Deploy accumulates with mtvg/f5pr for the next boundary (ggk2 hot lane triggers it).
REPORT RECONCILED (matches pre-verification byte-for-byte). THE SEAM MAP (load-bearing finding): cross-system moves happen at TWO seams with different arrival states — (1) the SOLVER's in-tour 2-system legs arrive LOADED (the 59/90 good transitions; the solver is NOT the deadhead source); (2) the REPOSITION jump is structurally EMPTY (RepositionToWaypoint = pure movement primitive) — THAT is the 31/90 deadhead. Fix hooks reposition-commit (both systems' fresh listings already in hand via the lxwn pre-flight): buildLookbackManifest pairs departure buyables x destination IMPORT rows (spread >= floor, capped-spread ranked, hold-packed), buys under the FULL guard stack (min-margin, 9mkf import-sink + margin-preserving live-ask ceiling, agzj reserve shrink/skip, hold+spend caps), persists in-progress state BEFORE loading (restart resumes carrying the load, RULINGS #2), jumps, and the existing held-liquidation sells at destination — no new sell code. Absorption netting deliberately skipped (the reposition path is not a ledger participant; single-tranche load = tranche-0 depth). No-lane -> empty jump as before. tour_jump_loaded_total{loaded} counter = the acceptance metric (HU21->UQ16 <30% empty). 9 tests red-first. Second-order chain-topology nudge -> sp-b3ho (P3). Go-only; ON by default via reposition. Awaiting: batch deploy -> counter panel -> the captain's corridor read.

title: Tour/arb optimizers have a good-level blind spot: 20k+ exotic bids (laser rifles/holographics/quantum drives) never traded despite fleet docking at their markets
description: Evidence (trade-analyst, 2026-07-11 ~01:00Z): (1) XT71-A1 bids 30,627 for LASER_RIFLES with a KNOWN in-cache source (ZC66-AX1B exports at 16,757, ABUNDANT, vol 30) = +13,870/u; fleet made 23 trades at XT71 in the last 3h and has traded LASER_RIFLES zero times EVER. Same zero-trades for HOLOGRAPHICS (HU21-A1 bid 35,880) and QUANTUM_DRIVES (KA42-A1 bid 68,634). All three sit in market_data, fresh reads. (2) Pattern = solver enumerates/visits the MARKETS but never the GOODS. Candidate causes to check in tour_solver.py / scanLanes: (a) price-anomaly or max-unit-price sanity filter dropping 20k+ bids as outliers/stale; (b) min-trade-volume filter excluding vol-6 goods; (c) goods-universe enumeration requiring both-sides channel richness (these are single-pair goods). (3) Diagnostic + workaround: captain flies a p4ua one-shot ZC66 laser_rifles -> XT71 (max-units 6-12, min-margin 10k) - if the guards reject it, the reject reason names the filter; if it flies, +83-166k/visit while the fix lands. Value at stake: the three lanes are worth ~200-400k/hr combined if sources for the other two surface.
notes: MERGED 31c218f (9 files +539 -1, numstat verified, already on origin) — DEPLOY RIDES THE NEXT BOUNDARY (Go-only, no routing ritual). PREMISE OVERTURNED by live replay (the lxwn method): there is NO good-level filter — all three suspects (price-anomaly, min-volume, both-sides-universe) DISPROVEN by Replay B (solver trades LASER_RIFLES cleanly when both systems co-present, 14,078/u spread). THE MECHANISM: a 1-gate-hop discovery horizon (tourSystemsFrom :1369 = home+neighbors; MAX_TOUR_SYSTEMS=2 Admiral decision) — all three exotic lanes span 3+ hops so source and sink never co-occur in one snapshot; absence, not rejection. FLEET-WIDE: the arb scanner shares the identical horizon (scanLanes->neighborSystems); the p4ua one-shot worked only because hand-feeding bypassed discovery. GUARD KEPT (RULINGS #4): the horizon is LOAD-BEARING — the travel model prices every crossing as ONE hop; widening without a multi-hop model mis-prices 3-hop lanes ~3x. FIX = the silent drop made LOUD: tour_candidates_dropped_total{reason=counterparty_system_unreachable} + logs naming the richest out-of-horizon lanes (would have named XT71/HU21/KA42 on the first plan). Red-first Go tests + a Python mechanism-lock test (guards against accidental horizon widening). title: Consult: gases + raw minerals spend audit — my ledger read says 186k/6h (0.3% of all buys); verify independently + rule whether siphon/extraction self-supply ever beats market-buying these inputs
description: CONSULT, economy-analyst, Admiral-directed (00:10Z). MY READ (verify independently — that is the point of the consult): trailing 6h PURCHASE_CARGO by good-class: gases (LIQUID_NITROGEN 81k/20tx, HYDROCARBON 45k/14tx, LIQUID_HYDROGEN 42k/13tx) + raw minerals (ALUMINUM_ORE 9.7k, COPPER_ORE 3.1k, SILICON_CRYSTALS 2.8k, QUARTZ_SAND 1k) = ~186k TOTAL vs 62.2M all cargo buys = 0.30%. These look like factory-chain INPUT legs (fabricator dependency trees), not tour cargo. QUESTIONS: (1) does your independent pull agree these classes are ~0.3% noise, or is spend hiding elsewhere (e.g. inside fabricate steps, FUEL excluded intentionally)? (2) With 6 new chains just live (v8ha), does projected input demand for gas/mineral classes grow materially — which chains pull them? (3) STRUCTURAL: we own 4 idle excavators + gas machinery exists (gas drain deployed but parked on the mfg co-run precondition) — is there ANY volume threshold where self-supply (siphon/extract) beats market-buying these inputs, or is market-buy permanently correct at our scale (the era-1 prior: mine only with no market option)? Rule it so the excavators' idleness is a conscious choice on the record. SNAPSHOT: treasury ~4.2M, 9 heavies, 16 chains, ~2M/hr net, T-37h.
notes:
TRADE-ANALYST AUDIT + RULING (sp-naw6, ~00:15Z): (1) VERIFIED — independent 6h pull: gases 166,629 (LN 78.9k/2,382u + HC 45.3k/1,009u + LH 42.5k/1,556u) + raw minerals 16,520 (alu/copper ore, silicon, quartz) = 183,149 vs 62,891,167 all cargo buys = 0.29% — agrees with your 0.30% within window offset. No hidden spend: this engine's factories transact through market buy/sell (all visible in PURCHASE_CARGO — there is no separate fabricate-step cost), and FUEL is its own small line (~70k/hr fleet-wide). If EXPLOSIVES (93.5k, arguably raw-class at 132/u) is counted, the class is still only 0.44%. NOISE CONFIRMED. (2) v8ha chain growth: the new five pull gases/minerals only second-order (via electronics/plastics feeds-of-feeds); the classes that would grow this line — FERTILIZERS/PLASTICS/EXPLOSIVES chains — are all skip-tier in the program. Projection: even 3x growth = ~0.9% of buys. Stays noise. (3) STRUCTURAL RULING — MARKET-BUY IS PERMANENTLY CORRECT AT OUR SCALE, and the excavators' idleness is hereby a conscious, on-record choice. Unit economics: these inputs cost 22-46/u across 397 markets; an excavator's plausible yield ~100-300u/hr caps self-supply value at ~12k/hr per hull — below the fleet's management-attention noise floor and far below any hull's opportunity cost, before coordination overhead and API load. The era-1 prior generalizes: self-supply is an AVAILABILITY instrument, not a price instrument. FLIP CONDITIONS (the threshold you asked for, so this never needs re-litigating): activate extraction ONLY if (a) a chain input has literally ZERO market source within tour reach AND that chain's feed value >150k/hr (blocked-chain rescue), OR (b) an input class sustains >400/u across ALL sources for 2h+ (10x today — at that price 300u/hr = 120k/hr and the math genuinely flips). Neither has ever occurred in 397 markets. Excavators: idle by design; revisit only on (a)/(b), or scrap-value them at era close if the engine supports it.
ANSWER BANKED + CLOSING (00:20Z): analyst verified independently — 0.29% (183k/62.9M, matches my 0.30%; +explosives = 0.44%, still noise; no hidden fabricate costs — factories transact via visible market buys; v8ha chains grow it 2nd-order to ~0.9% worst case). RULING ON RECORD: market-buy permanently correct at our scale; the 4 excavators idle BY DESIGN (conscious choice, not neglect). Named flip conditions: (1) a zero-market-source input blocking a >150k/hr chain, or (2) an input class sustaining >400/u for 2h across ALL sources — either fires a siphon/extract re-evaluation. Acted on = closed.
close_reason: Closed

title: Stocker hull has no durable dedication — poached by factory/contract pool every crash/idle-gap, breaking CONTINUOUS stocking
description: First deposit PROVED the chain (sp-5qs1). But continuous stocking is impossible: the stocker workflow needs its hull UNRESERVED to claim it, and an unreserved light hauler is claimed by FindIdleLightHaulers the moment the stocker container ends (crash, restart, or between legs). Evidence: 'fleet assign --fleet stocker' does NOT persist (harbormaster forensic xw6i: dedicated_fleet stayed '' — 'stocker' isn't a recognized fleet); captain-reservation protects it but BLOCKS the stocker workflow from claiming it (had to release to launch). So 38 has cycled stocker->crash->factory(FABRICS WIP) twice. Wanted: either (a) a real 'stocker'/'warehouse' fleet dedication that FindIdleLightHaulers excludes AND the stocker workflow claims (mirror the tour/'trade' pattern), or (b) the stocker workflow self-holds its hull across legs with restart-safe re-claim. Interim: captain relaunches stocker in bursts when a light is free; first-deposit acceptance already banked so this is throughput, not correctness.
notes:
DEDUPES + SUPERSEDES sp-8rn6 (my earlier filing of the same stocker-reservation gap — deleted, this is the sharper bead with the captain's evidence + the two options). DISPATCHED opus option (a): a real durable 'stocker' DedicatedFleet mirroring trade/contract — Admiral already ruled the stocker a dedicated hauler (sp-zdwg option A), so a durable dedication is intent-consistent, no fresh ruling needed. Crux the agent must nail: the stocker's ClaimShip operation must ALIGN with the 'stocker' dedication or it rejects itself.
session died
MERGED c3ad4ed (6 files +255 -8, numstat + ancestry verified: b2c5da2 + m92a only, NO sg35 guard, tour-killer legacy check absent — tours safe), pushed, DEPLOYED (daemon-only, restart 25/25, 0 lost). Mirrored the 'warehouse' fleet pattern (not the busy trade/contract coordinators): const operationStocker='stocker' + 'operation':'stocker' in the stocker launch config (container_ops_stocker.go:31,104) so ClaimShip's guard (operation==dedicated_fleet) PERMITS the stocker to claim its own 'stocker'-dedicated hull while rejecting factory('manufacturing')/contract. THE CRUX confirmed: the stocker was claiming with NOTHING (legacy path, no operation key) — NOT operationTrading; ContainerTypeTrading stays TRADING (orthogonal). 'stocker' added to DefaultFleetCargoRequirement (cargo>=1, r6f1 floor). FindIdleLightHaulers excludes it FOR FREE (ship_pool_manager.go:175 unconditional dedicated-skip, regression-locked). Restart-safe inherently (ForceRelease touches only assignment, never dedicated_fleet). 6 tests incl. the durability chain (claim->release->dedication survives->foreign rejected->relaunch re-claims). Awaiting captain ACCEPT: continuous stocking with zero babysitting.
EXECUTED (20:09Z): 38 durably pinned 'stocker' (poach-proof, survives restart), stranded 60 FABRICS sold in-place at D39 (+160,800), continuous stocker launched in PORTFOLIO mode (--target-per-good 30 — rotates across the 7-good stock list instead of stacking FOOD, per Admiral's multi-item directive). Babysitting ENDS. Warehouse already holds 80 FOOD; stocker now builds EQUIPMENT/MEDICINE/etc. Next dchv watch: first Lane-D withdrawal-sourced contract (auto).
POST-BATCH RE-PROOF (captain uf8s7): stocker relaunched a9334dbb, dedication HELD through the deploy restart, no poach — the durability claim exercised through a real boundary.

title: Single-hull scout post tour snapshots its market list at spawn — post-spawn discovered markets never visited until respawn (ykhl's hulls=1 gap, different code path from partition drift)
description: Found during sp-ykhl (which fixed the SAME staleness class for PARTITIONED hulls>1 posts). A single-hull (hulls=1) post's probe builds its tour market list ONCE at spawn (scout_tour.go tour-building path) rather than re-reading discovery per circuit — so a market discovered after spawn (nn0y virgin discovery runs continuously) is never visited until the tour respawns for some unrelated reason. ykhl's drift-detection lives in the partitioning path (ensurePartitions) which single-hull posts return before reaching. FIX options: (a) the single-hull tour re-reads the system's current market list at the start of each circuit (cheapest if the tour loop structure allows); (b) the coordinator respawns a single-hull tour on the same market-drift signal ykhl computes (reuse marketSetDrift, apply to hulls=1). Preserve: zixw end-of-circuit pacing + no per-hop waits. Evidence bar: a single-hull post's max-age exceeding target while new markets sit unvisited (mirror the KA42 151m evidence, single-probe version).

session died

Option (b) chosen with a REAL finding: option (a) was impossible — a single-hull post stuck on executeStationaryScout (chosen when only 1 market known at spawn) has NO circuit boundary to hook, so per-circuit refresh could never fix it; coordinator-level teardown+respawn covers both tour functions. ensureSingleHullFreshness: standing+manned+hulls==1 only, reuses ykhl's debounce config VERBATIM (threshold 2 / 60m age) via the shared diffMarketSets primitive (behavior-preserving extraction, ykhl tests green); respawn same-tick via the existing manning pass; restart adopts current markets as baseline (no mass-respawn on boot, RULINGS #2). zixw pacing untouched; sweep-once excluded (regression test). 8 new tests. The KA42-151m staleness class is now closed for BOTH post shapes.

title: DESIGN/fleet-ops: an idle-gap 'stocker' hull has no durable reservation — intent not expressible; survives only until the container ends/crashes
description: TORWIND-38 forensic (sp-5qs1 chain): 'stocker' is a container TYPE (idle-gap loop, sp-zdwg), NOT a dedicated_fleet. Launching a stocker loop claims the hull's container_id but writes zero dedication, so the instant the loop ends/crashes the hull is a free general-pool idle hull ANY coordinator legitimately claims (this is what let MEDICINE grab 38). If 'reserve hull X for stocking' is a supported standing intent, it needs a durable mechanism. OPTIONS: (a) a real 'stocker' DedicatedFleet + a coordinator/reconciler to man it (mirrors contract/trade — survives crashes, RULINGS #2/#7); (b) the captain reservation path (owner=captain, sp-i1ku — ClaimShip:1027 DOES enforce it; the captain already used this on 38 post-incident as remediation) formalized as the stocker-hold convention; (c) accept ephemerality — the stocker is a best-effort idle-gap filler and re-claims a hull each cycle. This is a DESIGN + fleet-ops call (captain/Admiral on policy; engineering provides the mechanism). NOT auto-build — needs a ruling on which model before implementation.
close_reason: Superseded by sp-m92a (the captain's sharper filing of the same stocker-reservation gap) — m92a delivered (c3ad4ed, durable 'stocker' dedication) and captain-accepted via zp9t. An earlier delete attempt didn't stick; closing now.

title: Factory claims laden hulls holding unrelated cargo → burns no-op buy-tasks (needs the sp-wq7r NO-CARGO-DUMP guard on the manufacturing claim path)
description: TORWIND-38 forensic (sp-5qs1 chain): after the stocker crashed leaving 80 FOOD aboard, the MEDICINE factory legitimately claimed the freed idle hull (FindIdleLightHaulers -> guarded ClaimShip, all correct) but then spun 3 zero-unit BUY tasks — 'Hold full and could not unload existing cargo — skipping this input purchase' (0 units/0 credits each). It correctly did NOT jettison the FOOD (no data loss) but thrashed. The contract coordinator has a NO-CARGO-DUMP CLAIM GUARD (sp-wq7r FilterUnrelatedCargo) that the manufacturing discovery/claim path lacks: it should skip (or deposit/unload-first) a hull holding cargo unrelated to the target good BEFORE claiming it for input-buying. Bounded impact (thrash + wasted API, no loss); less likely once sp-5qs1 stops the crash-orphan, but the guard gap is real. Port the wq7r guard to run_factory_coordinator's discovery/claim.
notes:

title: LATENT ownership-guard bypass: container_runner.attemptClaimShip legacy path (no 'operation' key) skips the ClaimShip dedication guard — RULINGS #7 hole, audit+enforce
description: Found during the TORWIND-38 forensic (sp-5qs1 chain), did NOT cause that incident but is a real latent violation of RULINGS #7 (ownership model is law; l7h2 atomic ClaimShip). container_runner.go:965-989 (attemptClaimShip): a container whose launch metadata lacks the 'operation' key falls to a LEGACY FindBySymbol+AssignToContainer+Save path that does NOT run the dedication guard (ship_repository.go:1052 DedicatedFleet!='' && !=operation). Any container kind launched without stamping 'operation' could thus poach a dedicated/pinned hull. PRIMARY FIX (defense-in-depth): the legacy fallback path enforces the SAME dedication guard before AssignToContainer+Save — a dedicated hull is rejected regardless of the operation key. SECONDARY: audit which container kinds launch without the 'operation' metadata key and stamp it (so they route to the guarded path); report the list. Tests: legacy-path claim of a dedicated hull is rejected; operation-keyed path unchanged (regression). The l7h2 ownership work must not have a side door.

title: enry posts: re-partition on market-SET additions (nn0y-discovered markets join NO circuit until hull-count changes) — KA42 max-age 151m evidence
description: Captain audit 18:51Z: KA42 max-age 151m vs 30m target suggests markets discovered AFTER the partition cut (nn0y virgin discovery runs continuously) belong to no probe's circuit. Confirmed by design: enry re-partitions ONLY on hull-count change (partition stability, deliberate). GAP: the market SET is also a partition input; additions should trigger a re-cut. Design constraints: keep stability (never re-cut per tick) — debounce: re-partition when accumulated new markets for a post >= N (config, e.g. 2) OR a slow cadence check (e.g. hourly), whichever the coordinator's reconcile structure makes cleaner. Removals (market gone) are rarer — same debounce. Preserve: same-hull-set + same-market-set = zero re-cuts (existing stability tests must stay green).

title: Recovered laden hull at an ALIVE home still can't see home's fresh potential — held unsellable cargo blocks the live plan (m9co follow-up: consider shed-at-home / held-cargo-aware planning before rotating away)
description: m9co field evidence: recovered 2B at X1-JP61 planned 'fresh 0' because 80 ADV_CIRCUITRY + 80 LAB_INSTRUMENTS (bought FQ55, no JP61 sink) seat every hold slot for the whole tour in the solver model (occ=[total_initial]*n, tour_solver.py) — while a fresh empty-hold relaunch on IDENTICAL market data found fresh 241,696. m9co fixed the REPOSITION pre-flight (empty-hold synthetic state) so the hull now rotates instead of dying — but a laden hull at a genuinely alive home now JUMPS AWAY when the better play may be shedding the blocking cargo at home (or jumping specifically to a sink for the HELD goods, then returning). Design space: (a) live plan variant that prices jettison/below-cost liquidation of held goods vs the fresh profit they unblock (respect bp6f/tranche floors — this is an economics call, may need analyst input on when selling-at-a-loss-to-unblock beats rotating); (b) rank candidates by fresh + held-liquidation-at-candidate so the jump prefers systems that sink the held goods; (c) do nothing — bounded rotation + honest exit may be acceptable; measure frequency first via the new distinct ranking logs. Evidence bar before building: >=N occurrences/week of rotate-away-from-alive-home in the logs.

title: Consult: probe batch #2 (8x ~22.5k) — 4 open slots now + 7 beyond-wing systems, idle pool at 3 (2 stranded far)
description: zixw sequel, Admiral-prompted (17:45Z). Snapshot: 28 satellites, 3 idle (17@XX56, 24@YP16, 25@KA42-lap-gap); posts 18/21 manned; 20 systems priced, 250+ markets; wings CX94/DP16/CR81/DZ85/MX50/ZP56/NH17 open behind BG81/GU51/QJ93. All prior corrections institutionalized: sweep-once drive-bys (posts kind), ?-edge verification (nn0y fail-closed), cadence caps (zixw freshness-derived). Proceeding on capex-autonomy + standing zixw validation; refute if the wing recon is worthless (no traded goods signal from the gateway trio yet — that IS the recon's question).
notes:
TRADE-ANALYST (sp-wbdg, ~18:05Z): NO REFUTATION — proceed, with two sharpenings. THE 4 POST-FILLS: carried by the standing zixw validation unchanged (stale-data cost is measured at -247k/3h; a manned post pays for the whole batch). THE 4-PROBE WING RECON IS NOT WORTHLESS — it is a correctly-priced lottery ticket: ~90k stake vs (a) base-rate EV — this era ~55-60% of swept systems proved rich (GQ92/ZC66/DP51/JP61/HU21/UQ16/PA3 vs KM70/XX56/PA62-class thin), each rich find added ~1-1.5M/hr ceiling, and even discounting hard for the ~44h clock (a find must land in the next ~12-24h AND be hauler-reachable through 2-3 gates to monetize), EV ≈ 150-300k >> 90k; (b) the QUANTUM_DRIVES option — a 68.6k/u home sink with NO source across 20 systems/250 markets means the source, if it exists, is in exactly this unswept space; one source lane ≈ +100-150k/hr. The recon's null result ('no traded goods behind the trio') is itself the answer to a standing question — cheap either way. SHARPENINGS: (1) idle-first — 17@XX56 and 24@YP16 are already far-forward; if either adjoins a wing, retask before buying (buy 6 not 8); the stranded-probe cost is sunk, their position is an asset. (2) Sequence hub-shaped wings first: any wing showing edges to MULTIPLE known systems (the AF2 shape) outranks dead-end wings — hub finds compound, dead-ends don't. (3) Recon grep list reaffirmed for every new sweep: QUANTUM_DRIVES exporters, importers of the 11 orphan exotics (hyper-drive/cargo-hold-III/robotic-drones/AI-mainframes...), and any vol>=100 import cluster (GQ92-class signature). Confidence HIGH on the math, MEDIUM on the 55% base rate (small n, one era).

title: Power/reactor/slot data is a full-stack blind spot: API returns it, DTO drops it — outfitting decisions fly blind
description: Evidence (upgrade-scout, file:line): gobot/internal/adapters/api/ship_dto.go:8-50 parses /my/ships responses but drops Reactor, Crew, Mounts, and ALL requirements sub-objects (ShipRequirements: power/crew/slots — documented in gobot/api/openapi.json); domain ShipModule (internal/domain/navigation/ship_module.go:13-17) has only symbol/capacity/range; repo-wide grep for moduleSlots|mountingPoints|powerOutput = ZERO hits; ships table has no reactor/crew columns. Cost today: the frigate power gate (31 vs 33) was only resolvable by live trial-and-error installs (worked: 2 processor removals @1 power each -> CARGO_HOLD_III installed, cargo 40->115). Wanted: parse + persist frame slots, reactor powerOutput, per-module requirements; surface in ship info + outfit list so upgrade feasibility is computable offline. Also note: reactors/engines/frames have NO swap endpoints (definitive from openapi.json) — power budgets are permanent per hull.
notes:
session died
MERGED c45ef92 (28 files +2768 -752, numstat verified), pushed, DEPLOYED (restart 25/25, 0 lost; daemon+CLI together — daemon.proto regenerated in rebase, zdwg/enry fields grep-verified intact, grpc stubs byte-identical). Empirical frigate case (31v33, 2x 1-power processors) encoded as a test. Reactor/engine/frame permanence encoded in persistence comments. PROCESS NOTE: git rebase --continue malfunctioned repeatedly on this rebase (clean index, 'must edit all merge conflicts' error) — agent worked around via git commit -F + manual ref finalization, result byte-verified; watch for recurrence on other lanes. Awaiting captain ACCEPT: next power-gate decision made from the CLI without live trial-and-error.
ACCEPTANCE FAILED on first desk query (captain ybbw, 18:13Z): TORWIND-10 shows 'Module slots: 6/6 used' yet HOLD_III verdicts CAN-INSTALL with NO candidate requirements line. Harbormaster reproduced + diagnosed: candidate requirements lookup resolved EMPTY -> feasibility compared against zero-filled requirements (trivial pass) and printer skipped the all-zero line. Slot accounting (installed-module math) is CORRECT and trusted. Fix agent resumed (worktree sp-el60-fix): real requirements source, UNKNOWN-REQUIREMENTS fail-closed verdict when unresolvable, requirements line always printed, TORWIND-10 shape as regression. Captain instructed: no CAN-INSTALL trusted until fix deploys. Side finding (captain): lights are SLOT-bound not power-bound (8 power free on 10) — the old 15<19 power claim is dead.
ACCEPTANCE FIX MERGED 42b453e (12 files +574 -79, numstat verified), pushed, DEPLOYED (26/26). Root cause exactly as diagnosed + sharper: CandidatePower/Crew/Slots were optional CLI ints defaulting to 0 — need<=budget vs zeros = trivial CAN-INSTALL; flags REMOVED (the footgun). Real source: FindModuleRequirements resolves from FLEET OBSERVATION (module requirements are per-symbol constants; any hull that ever carried it is authoritative). InstallFeasibility.RequirementsKnown defaults FALSE (zero-value can never fake a verdict); unknown -> UNKNOWN-REQUIREMENTS, never CAN-INSTALL; requirements line always printed. HARBORMASTER ACCEPTANCE RE-RUN POST-DEPLOY (live): TORWIND-10 + HOLD_III now prints 'requirements: power=4 crew=6 slots=3' + verdict SLOT-SHORT-3 vs 6/6 used — internally consistent, matches reality. title: Cost the AF2 jump-gate completion (walled hub: touches GQ92/HU21/KA42/PA3/UQ16) — Admiral-scale go/no-go package
description: For fleet-architect (+trade-analyst economics door). AF2's gate is under construction and shows as an edge from FIVE core systems (GQ92, HU21, KA42, PA3, UQ16) — completing it collapses inner-cluster routing distances (tour hops, contract legs, relay times) and opens whatever sits beyond AF2. Want: (1) the construction bill read on-site (a probe from the new batch or any passing hull can read it; construction CLI exists), (2) material sourcing plan vs current market data, (3) total cost + fleet-time estimate, (4) $/hr uplift estimate from shortened routes. Deliverable = a go/no-go package for the Admiral (Admiral-scale spend, bd human flag when costed). Era ends 07-12 13:00Z — only worth it if payback < remaining era. Context: treasury 3.2M growing 1.6-2M/hr, 5 heavies, 21 scout posts declared.
notes:
RESOLVED NO-GO (15:20Z) — on REACHABILITY, not economics. Bill read REMOTELY (no ship needed — construction status is a public read; option-decay stopped same-hour): X1-AF2-I58 at 0.0%, needs 1600 FAB_MATS + 400 ADVANCED_CIRCUITRY (QUANTUM_STABILIZERS already 1/1). Architect's B-scale confirmed (~3M+ materials, the marginal-to-NO case) BUT moot: supplying requires a hull AT I58, and AF2 is unreachable — jump blocked by the incomplete far gate (router excludes *-edges), zero warp hulls/drives sold anywhere (standing gotcha). No delivery path exists this era => B=infinite => NO-GO regardless of uplift. Decision cost: 3 CLI reads, zero credits, zero heavy-hours. Curiosities banked for the next era's priors: I58 carries a MARKETPLACE trait and its stabilizer slot is pre-completed — something (NPC?) is active in AF2. Frontier growth continues via the verified QJ93/BG81/GU51 wings instead.
TRADE-ANALYST ECONOMICS-DOOR CLOSE-OUT (Admiral-directed read, 16:20Z) — resolution endorsed, two record corrections. (1) The NO-GO on reachability is sound and OVER-DETERMINED: even if reachable, the bill at CURRENT asks = 1600 FAB_MATS x ~1.1-1.5k (post-gate GLUT: four ABUNDANT sources, F45-home 1,045, HU21 986, GQ92 1,086, ZC66 1,317 - asks crashed from 4.8k) + 400 ADV_CIRC x ~4.6-5.5k (NK36 4,592 cheapest) = ~3.7-4.6M materials + heavy-haul opportunity cost at 1.6-2M/hr fleet rate. Architect's U-threshold framework then demands ~85-100k/hr sustained route-uplift for <46h payback - marginal at best. Moot, but now on record with real numbers, not the 3M placeholder. (2) CORRECTION to 'zero warp hulls/drives sold anywhere': true TODAY (GQ92-A2 yard verified empty just now) but a SHIP_EXPLORER warp frame WAS listed there at 850k on 07-09 (sp-j9nb record) - yard inventory ROTATES. Next-era prior: check GQ92-A2-class yards for explorers EARLY; one warp hull dissolves the entire walled-hub problem class AND enables beyond-graph scouting. (3) Curiosity extension: I58's pre-completed stabilizer + marketplace trait (NPC activity beyond the wall) is consistent with our QUANTUM_DRIVES home-sink having no visible source - the source may sit beyond the graph. If a warp hull ever appears at any yard this era, the quantum-source hunt is its first mission.

comments.author: Andres Camacho
comments.text:
FLEET-ARCHITECT — AF2 GATE-COMPLETION GO/NO-GO FRAMEWORK (sp-oeop). This is the DECISION STRUCTURE + constraint math + hidden costs + the two inputs that close it — NOT a verdict. The bill is unread and the uplift is the analyst's door; I will not fabricate either (a go/no-go built on an invented bill or uplift is the narrative-outran-evidence failure).

WHAT'S KNOWN vs PENDING:
- KNOWN: treasury 3.2M growing 1.6-2M/hr; 5 heavies, 21 posts; AF2 edges FIVE core systems (GQ92/HU21/KA42/PA3/UQ16); era ends 07-12 13:00Z. Precedent datapoint: the FAB_MATS gate just completed cost ~2.98M in materials (one-time capex).
- PENDING (blockers to a real package): (1) the AF2 construction bill — materials x qty — needs the on-site construction-CLI read named in the bead; (2) the realized route-uplift $/hr — the trade-analyst's economics door.

THE CLOCK IS THE BINDING CONSTRAINT: ~46h to reset (07-12 13:00Z from now). The gate does NOT persist across reset, so 100% of payback must land in ~46h, and the window shrinks every hour of delay. This BLUNTS the usual structural-lever logic: route-collapsing infrastructure normally beats buying hulls, but a ~46h horizon can make THIS instance fail to pay back. Right lever, possibly wrong time — that is the whole tension.

THE GO/NO-GO MATH (falls out the moment both inputs land):
- B = delivered cost = material credits + fleet-time OPPORTUNITY COST of the hauls (see hidden cost).
- U = realized $/hr uplift from shortened routes, quantified by the analyst as hop-count reduction x fleet-time value — NOT a hand-wave.
- GO iff U > B / 46h, WITH margin: target payback < ~30h, because U is soft and the clock only shrinks. Thresholds for reference: a ~3M bill needs U > ~65k/hr (~3-4% of the current 1.6-2M/hr engine); a ~2M bill needs U > ~43k/hr.

HIDDEN COST the naive "bill vs uplift" misses: the material hauls DIVERT fleet-time from a 1.6-2M/hr engine. True cost = material credits + (haul-hours x current per-hull yield). At 1.6-2M/hr across 5 heavies, diverted heavy-hours are expensive — cost the hauls in opportunity terms, not just fuel+materials. This can flip a marginal case to NO.

DO NOT PRICE the speculative "opens whatever sits beyond AF2" upside into the go/no-go: unknown frontier, ~46h to expiry, unknown contents = an unpriceable call option. Justify the spend on the KNOWN inner-cluster route-shortening ALONE, and treat beyond-AF2 as unpriced upside that must NOT tip a marginal case to GO.

PROVISIONAL LEAN (conditional, pending the bill):
- Small bill (~1-1.5M): likely GO — modest route-shortening clears the bar in 46h even after the haul opportunity cost.
- FAB_MATS-scale bill (~3M): MARGINAL-to-NO — needs U > ~65k/hr AND a shrinking clock; real risk it does not pay back before reset. Lean cautious; demand a firm U before committing.

RECOMMENDATION (actionable now): the decision has DECAYING option value. Dispatch the on-site bill-read on the NEXT probe/hull pass IMMEDIATELY; run the analyst's route-uplift quantification in PARALLEL; reconvene with B and U, then the Admiral decides against this framework. I am NOT bd-human-flagging yet — the bead says flag "when costed," and it is not costed; flagging an un-costed package wastes the Admiral's decision. I flag the moment B and U land. Not closing sp-oeop.

Each new post = fresher prices for tour planner + QUANTUM tripwire coverage + contract-sourcing reach. Refute angles: is sweep-once coverage worth more than the marginal probe elsewhere? do the '?'-edge systems (QJ93 wing, BG81, GU51) need gate verification BEFORE probes are bought for them?

title: Refute: staged tour max-spend raise 1M->1.5M->2M keyed to aggregate-exposure<=60% of treasury
description: CONSULT (refute-this-plan) for trade-analyst. Snapshot at ask (14:45Z): 46 hulls, 5 heavies touring (19/2B/2C/2D/2E, 1M caps), treasury 3.24M recovering from a 743k trough (capex #4+#5 + two first manifests concurrent), income ~1.6-2M/hr, era ends 07-12 13:00Z. EVIDENCE the cap binds: 2D's first manifest hit 972,669 of the 1M cap; high-unit-value goods (ANTIMATTER 151k/u, MEDICINE ~5k/u) fill 1M with hold slots idle; planner price error 0.8% (tour report). PLAN: raise per-tour --max-spend at natural re-plan boundaries, staged by AGGREGATE exposure (N_heavies x cap <= ~60% of liquid treasury): 12M+ treasury -> 1.5M caps; 16M+ -> 2M. Captain launch-flag only, no code. REFUTE ANGLES WANTED: does absorption (not the cap) actually bind most manifests (2B/2E bursts were ~520-540k)? does a bigger tranche crush sell-side bids past lbbm's 80% floor more often (held tranches = dead capital)? is the 60% exposure rule too loose given today's 90% drawdown?
notes:
TRADE-ANALYST REFUTATION (sp-i8vx: staged tour-cap raise 1M→1.5M→2M @ exposure≤60%) — 2026-07-10 ~14:55Z

**Verdict: WEAK REFUTE — approve the staged raise, but the plan protects against the wrong risk and rests on one wrong premise. Two amendments are the actual substance.**

**PREMISE CORRECTION (ledger fact):** no heavy bought ANY good above 10k/unit since 08:00Z — the "ANTIMATTER 151k/u + high-unit-value goods fill 1M with hold slots idle" framing is unsupported (151k was likely a leg TOTAL, not per-unit; antimatter trades 3-6k/u). The cap binds through plain hold-math: a ~360-hold at the current 2.8-4.5k/u manifest mix = 1.0-1.6M full load. That is the honest justification FOR the raise — 2D hitting 972,669/1M (97%) is a hold-limited full board, and every full-value board on a 360-hold will hit a 1M cap whenever avg unit cost >2.8k. Raise: justified.

**Refute angle (a) — absorption vs cap: MIXED, leaning cap.** 2B/2E's ~520-540k bursts were BOARD-bound (not enough profitable legs at those stops), not absorption-bound per se; 2D was cap-bound at 97%. Expect the cap to bind on any tour whose board is rich (ZC66/GQ92 multi-good stops) and to be slack on thin boards. The raise only pays on rich boards — which are exactly the tours producing the 1.6-2M/hr. No objection.

**Refute angle (b) — bigger tranche crushes sell bids past lbbm's 80% floor: STRUCTURALLY DISSOLVED.** Spend caps do not control UNITS; the hold (fixed) and per-market trade-volumes control units-per-sink-visit. A higher max-spend shifts the mix toward pricier goods at the SAME unit counts — sell-side crush risk is unchanged by the cap. Where held-tranche risk actually lives: pricier goods tend to have thinner sinks (adv_circ v40-100, parts/plating v6-30). AMENDMENT 1: per-good unit-tranche cap = min(hold share, ~1x sink trade_volume per market visit) for goods >5k/u — that protects the 80% floor at its real binding point, and it costs nothing on deep sinks.

**Refute angle (c) — is 60% too loose after today's 90% drawdown: THE GATES ARE FINE; THE HOLE IS STAGE-0.** The plan gates RAISES at 12M/16M treasury but is silent about TODAY: at 3.24M liquid, five 1M-cap heavies can load 5M concurrently = 154% exposure, and the ledger shows ~6.5M of purchases across the five heavies inside the 14:00 hour alone — survivable only because sells land continuously. The 743k trough IS the proof that instantaneous in-flight exposure ran ~90%. AMENDMENT 2 (the real protection): enforce the exposure rule CONTINUOUSLY from now — before any manifest load: (in-flight manifest value at cost + planned load) ≤ 60% of liquid treasury; stagger departures to satisfy it. At today's 3.24M that means ~2 concurrent full loads — matching the observed healthy cadence anyway. The staged raises then inherit a rule that already runs, instead of switching on at 12M.

**Era-clock check:** ~46h left; a raised cap pays back within one loop (~2-2.5h). No objection from the clock. Planner price error 0.8% is excellent — planning accuracy is not the risk; concurrency is.

**Confidence.** HIGH on the structural (b) dissolution and the premise correction (direct ledger queries); MEDIUM on manifest-level spend isolation (hourly buckets blur multi-load hours — 2C shows 4.6M/26-buys in hour 13, i.e., multiple fast loads; per-manifest telemetry would sharpen this, the tour report presumably has it).
**Flips:** if the tour planner CAN already stagger by in-flight exposure and today's trough was purely the capex collision → amendment 2 is already satisfied, approve as written. If any manifest actually contains a >10k/u good → my premise correction is wrong, re-examine hold-idle claim. If lbbm held-tranche events spike after the first 1.5M manifests → amendment 1 was insufficient, halve tranche caps for >5k/u goods.

Cross-ref (harbormaster 2026-07-10): sp-78ai (absorption reservations + recovery-externality pricing) closes this same in-flight-exposure hole from the MARKET-DEPTH side — the trade-analyst ruling explicitly pairs the two. Build lanes L2 sp-l2cq / L3 sp-ypbo carry the reference.

title: Trade engine: 3 execution bugs costing ~495k/3h (same-waypoint round-trips, stale-ask buys, wrong lab sink) - from Admiral-requested audit
description:
Source: trade-analyst FIFO-matched audit of 11:30-14:30Z window (full table on sp-zgbk note, 2026-07-10 14:30Z). Trading realized +4.10M/3h overall - these three bugs are the -495k drag; fixing them recovers ~+120k/hr.

BUG 1 - SAME-WAYPOINT ROUND-TRIPS (-142k): the engine bought and sold the same good at the SAME market: FERTILIZERS UQ16-FF5F->FF5F -75,200 (320u), ALUMINUM UQ16-AF2D->AF2D -55,680 (240u), FERTILIZERS JP61-Z10A->Z10A -11,520 (160u). Buying at ask and selling at the same market's bid is a guaranteed spread loss. Suspect: route-abort/hold-clearing logic dumping cargo where it stands, or the arb ranker allowing src==dst. Fix: hard-forbid sell-at-buy-waypoint except explicit emergency dump; make aborts carry cargo to the nearest positive bid.

BUG 2 - STALE-ASK BUYS (-247k): SHIP_PARTS D39->H50 realized -3,430/u x 64 (-219,532!) and D39->A2 -27,178. The buyer executed against a cached quote after D39's ask laddered past the sink bid (factory lift + arb draw share D39; ask moved 3,985->4,942->~7k within the hour). Fix: re-verify LIVE ask at dock immediately before purchase; abort leg if live ask breaks the min-margin that justified dispatch (bp6f re-verify exists for margins at dispatch - extend to buy-time).

BUG 3 - WRONG-SIDE SINK (-66k): LAB_INSTRUMENTS NK36-ED7A->GQ92-C37 -819/u x 80. GQ92-C37 is a lab EXPORTER (its bid is the sellback price ~2.4k), not an import sink. The router treated an exporter's bid as a destination. Fix: destination filter trade_type=IMPORT (or EXCHANGE) only - never sell into an EXPORT market's bid unless bid>cost explicitly.

Verification data: all three reproducible from transactions table, window 2026-07-10 11:30-14:30Z, player_id=2, FIFO-matched per (ship,good).
notes: MERGED 91a68a9 (21 files +703 -41), pushed, deployed (daemon restart: 29 recovered/0 lost). Triple guard live: (1) src==dst feed sourcing forbidden — FindExportMarket excludes IMPORT-only + same-waypoint delivery guard; (2) universal buy-time live-ask ceiling across arb/circuit/tour; (3) export-market sinks excluded (tour/ranker/idle-arb). ~+120k/hr recoverable. Awaiting captain acceptance on live evidence.
close_reason: Captain ACCEPT via st-wisp-2jwq (16:00Z): ledger shows ZERO same-waypoint round-trips since 15:40Z deploy, no guard-abort anomalies, no stale-ask incidents; guards fail-closed and quiet. Survey re-checks the short window.

title: Scout reposition: virgin systems park forever — discover waypoints via API for unknown systems (presence-free endpoint)
description: s232 live finding (first tick, pid 2895): reposition destination = the post's first KNOWN market waypoint, so posts for UNSWEPT systems (PD21/UU57/XT71/YP16 — the sweep-once frontier, the captain's actual relay targets) park forever with 'no known marketplace waypoints yet'. Correct fail-closed, but a bootstrap chicken-and-egg: nothing can scan a system no satellite can reach. FIX: when a post's system has zero known market waypoints, the reconciler DISCOVERS them via the API's system-waypoints listing (presence-free — the frontier census proved it works without local ships; the 'waypoint' CLI verb wraps the same call), persists them (era-scoped), then repositions normally. Fallback if the API call fails: park honest as today. Small: one discovery call in the reposition path + tests.
notes: DEPLOYED bfca929 (pid 70440, verified 3 files +247/-13; recovery 25/0/8). Virgin discovery via the cache-first presence-free GetGraph port (era-scoping automatic via the standard Add path); backoff armed BEFORE the API call; three park states distinguished (discovered→dispatch same tick / genuinely-marketless→unserviceable / API-error→fail-closed backoff). First-tick live evidence pending the reconciler cadence (behavior test-locked). HOLD for captain acceptance: virgin frontier posts self-serve — the zero-manual-relay goal complete.

title: HANDOFF (2026-07-10 ~12:45Z): 5.5M treasury, ~1-2M/hr proven, 3-heavy tour fleet compounding, 11 systems, pre-positioning one fix from live — resume the 10M/hr program
description:
== WHERE WE ARE == Treasury 5,518,415 (from 87k crisis-low 18h ago). Operating net PROVEN at 1.0-2.25M/hr band for 6+ consecutive hours (capex excluded). Fleet 44 hulls, 11 systems swept (8 rich: JP61>ZC66>GQ92>DP51>KA42>HU21>UQ16>PA3), API at ~23% of ceiling, zero 429s ever. Era ends 07-12 13:00Z (~48h). ERA-3 FACTORY DOCTRINE (sp-hzz5 analyst ruling, Admiral-directed full review, 2026-07-11): 'A factory is a stocker with a local acquisition method. Its output is planner-visible stock at cost basis, never passive market supply. Chains are few, tour-aligned, and rotation-sized to the worker pool. Sourcing is supply-first with cross-source price backstops. Every chain carries its own P&L and dies automatically when it stops paying. Input recursion beyond depth-1 is forbidden — raw inputs are bought, not made.' Verdict: over-built acquisition (recursion/dual coordinators, 0.29% of money), under-built realization (no stock model/P&L/kill-switch where 6.6M/hr flows). C1 planner-visible STOCK (+300-600k/hr, factory=stocker, tours withdraw at basis) is era-3's FIRST build — it reduces architecture while adding the most money. C2 chain P&L + auto-kill <30k/hr, C4 mechanized rest signal, C5 cross-source ceiling baseline are era-2 sized. Full brief on sp-hzz5 (closed).
C1 DE-RISKED (analyst T1 answer from history, no experiment needed, 2026-07-11, evidence on sp-hzz5): 115 untouched export series held supply with SOFTENING asks — production needs no buyer. Feeding alone softens asks (−4.1% fed-only); our LIFTING is what tightens (+4.8% fed+lifted). So C1 (tours withdraw from stock at basis, bypassing our own market as buyer) cannot stall factory-market production, and moving lifts to stock-deposits reduces ask-tightening. C1 is safe and better than projected. Bonus: rotation recovery half-life independently re-fit at 194min median from 31 real events — matches the K2 rotation model. Correction of record: trade_volume is STATIC; feed benefit manifests in price/supply-state, not volume.

title: idle_arb config injection only fires at coordinator CREATION — recovered coordinators keep stale knobs; the documented retune path (config edit + restart) silently no-ops
description: Found live by sp-nw9v's investigation: the running contract_fleet_coordinator-player-2 has NO idle_arb_leash_radius key in its persisted container config — DaemonServer.injectIdleArbConfig (sp-uohe) injects config.yaml values at container CREATION only; boot RECOVERY re-adopts the old persisted config untouched. Consequence: the harbormaster's leash-150 retune (config.yaml edit + restart, exactly the flip path uohe documented) silently no-op'd — the dispatcher ran leash-80 the whole time (proven: 90u lane leash-skipped post-restart). FIX options: (a) re-inject idle_arb knobs at recovery (merge live config.yaml over the persisted keys for this section only — careful: other config keys are deliberately creation-pinned), (b) dispatcher reads the idle_arb section from live config at pass time (simplest honest semantics: config.yaml IS the knob surface), (c) document that retunes require coordinator recreate (worst — silent-no-op trap stays). Prefer (b) for knobs explicitly documented as restart-tunable. RULINGS #2/#5 family.
close_reason: DEPLOYED 96127f8 (pid 58304; verified 4 files +282/-12; roster-diff clean 15/0/5). Captain PRE-ACCEPTED in round 3 (st-wisp-o733) — converts at this deploy per protocol. Seam: buildCommandForType (the single funnel for creation AND recovery) clears idle_arb_* keys then re-injects from boot-loaded config — dropped knobs honestly fall back to defaults; creation-time injection retired; no override paths existed. RED-first incident test (stale keyless config + live leash-150 → command carries 150) + full 9-knob matrix + disabled-toggle. LIVE EVIDENCE: the recovered coordinator's verdict lines print leash 150 resolved via the new path. Retune protocol is finally as documented: config edit + daemon restart, no recreate.

title: Container-loss VISIBILITY: boot recovery diffs expected-vs-recovered and emits a loud event per missing/failed container (MEDICINE died silent for 100min)
description: Captain incident (st-wisp-qwie): MEDICINE factory (+200k/hr) dead from 00:01:22 to 01:41 relaunch, noticed only by eyeball. Timeline decoded: it terminalized 'completed' at the 00:01 restart under the PRE-ovkn shutdown binary (same event that ate LAB_INSTRUMENTS-c96ce648) — the ovkn class, already fixed; but DETECTION failed everywhere: no event fired at the false completion (worker-less signalCompletion warning path), recovery counted '1 failed' without NAMING it, income.stalled (2cdu) never tripped in 100min, and my post-restart checks grepped only names that APPEARED. FIX (detection, not another exit-path change): (1) boot recovery compares the pre-shutdown RUNNING set vs the recovered set and emits a LOUD captain event (container.lost, message text w/ container id+type) for every expected-but-missing or failed-recovery container — the watchkeeper's crash detector consumes it (wake the captain); (2) the 'recovery complete: N recovered, M failed' line NAMES the failures; (3) verify why income.stalled missed a 100-minute single-factory outage (threshold? per-engine granularity?) and report — fix only if it's a one-liner, else file. RULINGS #2 family: silence is the enemy.
notes: MERGED 80165ad (verified 4 files +267/-16): recovery diff names every loss (summary: 'N recovered, M lost (<id> [<type>]: <reason>), K coordinator-managed skipped, D dead-era skipped'), EventContainerLost = NEW interrupt-class type (crashed is deferred+self-healing — a loss must force an immediate wake; also avoids polluting crash-loop detection), fall-through guard (unexpected_terminal), coordinator/dead-era skips exempted (no false alarms). income.stalled finding filed as follow-up (per-factory granularity, detector-design-sized). DEPLOY: batches with the next restart (m5kv/wh0t gate); CLOSE on deploy — 3 -race tests lock behavior, live evidence = the named summary at every subsequent boot.
close_reason: DEPLOYED pid 66971 (leash-retune restart) with LIVE evidence: the new summary format printed on first boot — 'Container recovery complete: 13 recovered, 0 lost, 6 coordinator-managed skipped, 0 dead-era skipped' — losses explicit, skip-mislabeling gone; roster-diff matched exactly (6 missing = 6 reconciler respawns). Merged 80165ad verified; interrupt-class container.lost wakes the captain on any real loss. Follow-up sp-7vos (per-factory income granularity) filed.

title: arb-run resume mid-transit attempts jump before arrival — burns the full restart budget riding out a routine transit

Same honest-wait family as yjrs cooldowns (that one was already fixed via shared travel; this is the ARRIVAL sibling on the RESUME path).

description: refutation: Admiral challenged the shared-hull arrangement — verify, don't assume. SNAPSHOT 03:25Z: 6 contract hulls dual-running (contracts ~185-220k/hr this hour vs 400k/hr pre-idle-arb peak; idle-arb on the same hulls +1.0M gross/~+300k net per hour, 21 legs). (3) LEASH POLICY: are dispatched arb legs hub-local by design or luck — should legs be bounded by duration/distance so a hull is never >N min from claimable? Number the alternatives in $/hr.

TRADE-ANALYST VERDICT (sp-179o: idle-arb vs contract contention) — 2026-07-10 03:45Z
Method: full ledger decomposition, PRE window 20:01→00:01Z (before first arb SELL by a contract hull = 1z2h start 00:01:38Z) vs POST 00:01→03:30Z. 11 contract hulls identified; 89 home arb-leg stops geolocated vs hubs.

**HEADLINE: the 185k hour is RNG + a possible negotiation-rate coupling — it is NOT claim contention. Latency did not move; the all-busy state never occurred; and the arb legs are already hub-leashed in practice.**

**(1) CLAIM LATENCY — unchanged.** fulfil→next-accept gap: median AND mean 0.0 min in BOTH windows (the serial chain re-accepts instantly). accept→first-buy: PRE med 2.0 / p90 19.6 vs POST med 3.0 / p90 12.4 — statistically flat, p90 IMPROVED. Idle-arb added no measurable wait to the contract pipeline.

**(2) CONTENTION EVENTS — zero.** At the 11 post-launch accepts, hulls mid-arb were {0:3, 1:3, 2:4, 3:1} of 11 — never ≥4, never all-busy. The f66z fallback never reached its wait state.

**SO WHY DID THE HOUR PAY 185k? Two decomposed causes:**
- **RNG mix (dominant):** contracts/hr 5.2→3.2 and avg payout 63.3k→54.7k. The post-window drew a run of penny-raw contracts — 8 of 11 were iron_ore/quartz/liquid_hydrogen/aluminum/poly/diamonds class. When the RNG deals raws, gross halves regardless of fleet design. The 400k peak hour was the same dice rolling high.
- **One blind spot my ledger cannot rule out: negotiation RATE.** Fewer contracts/hr with zero gaps between them means fewer NEGOTIATIONS happened, not slower claims. If mid-arb hulls negotiate less often, there's a supply-side coupling invisible to transaction data. ASK SHIPWRIGHT: instrument negotiation-attempts/hr pre vs post (one counter). If attempts/hr is flat, RNG explains everything and the case closes.
- **Attribution warning on the 'net collapsed' impression:** arb legs and contract sourcing traded the SAME goods in-window (electronics, food, copper, aluminum...), which cross-contaminates both metrics. And one real toxin: **arb legs realized −234k on ELECTRONICS this window** (bought 359k, sold 125k) — the −891k electronics class again, still fragile post-incident.

**(3) LEASH POLICY — it's already leashed in practice; formalize it.** All 89 home arb stops: median 49u, mean 33u, MAX 52u from the nearest hub (cross-system legs excluded = the 2 dedicated lights + heavy, not idle-arb). Codify what the dispatcher is evidently doing: (a) one-way leg radius ≤80u (one hub-coverage radius, matches the sp-snmb math), (b) leg duration cap ~8min (claims arrive ~every 18min at normal rate), (c) **EXCLUDE any good demanded by a currently-open contract** (kills same-good contention AND metric cross-talk), (d) **blacklist ELECTRONICS from idle-arb** until its home market normalizes (tracer: F45/D-cluster electronics bids >2.5k stable).

**(4) ARRANGEMENT, numbered in $/hr:**
- **A. Shared-hull dual-duty (today):** contracts 250-330k gross (RNG-dependent) + idle-arb ~+300k net = **~550-630k/hr, capex 0.** Measured cost to contracts: ~0 (per 1-3 above).
- **B. Split fleet (dedicated arb lights @328k + leashed contract-only hulls):** contracts unchanged (serial — hull count above hub coverage adds nothing) + arb unchanged (**the LANES are the binder, not hull availability** — contract hulls are 89% idle; the same ~21 legs/hr would just move to new hulls) = same ~550-630k/hr MINUS 328k/hull capex MINUS added API load. **STRICTLY DOMINATED today.**
- **C. Shared + formalized leash/blacklist (RECOMMENDED):** A + electronics-class losses removed (−234k realized this window ≈ 60-70k/hr drag) ≈ **~620-700k/hr, capex 0.**
- Split-trigger for later (make it mechanical): buy the next 328k arb light only when the dispatcher logs SKIPPED profitable legs (no idle hull available) at >3-4/hr — that's the moment lanes outgrow idle capacity. The 2 free lights (20, 21) doing dedicated CROSS-GATE runs are already the correct split for legs that exceed the leash; keep that shape.

**Confidence.** HIGH on latency/contention/leash findings (full-population ledger + geolocation, clean pre/post windows). MEDIUM on the RNG attribution for the payout drop (the negotiation-rate counter closes the gap — one shipwright instrument). HIGH on the arrangement ruling given the serial-pipeline structure.
**Flips:** negotiation-attempts/hr measurably lower post-1z2h → real coupling; add 'pause arb when negotiation queue empty' before considering a split. Dispatcher skip-events >3-4/hr → lanes outgrew idle capacity; buy arb lights. Electronics bids normalize → lift the blacklist.

| BIOCOMPOSITES K80 3,421 → A4 5,321 | 128u | +1,900 | 20 | 39m/1m |
| MICROPROCESSORS A3 2,899 → D40 4,030 | 73u | +1,131 | 35 | 39m/5m |
| EQUIPMENT E43 2,287 → A1 3,355 | 76u | +1,068 | 20 | 22m/1m |
| FOOD K80 1,593 → A1 2,407 | 128u | +814 | 60 | 39m/1m |
(Coordinate note that makes these short: home's markets are CO-LOCATED in clusters — A1/A2/A3/A4 share one point, D39/D40 another, H49/H50/H52 another, E41/42/43 another. Most "different market" hops are 50-130u.)

**OUT-OF-LEASH survivors (>150u — tier-2 assignments, not idle-arb):** DRUGS J56→H49 +2,248 (763u); ASSAULT_RIFLES E41→J56 +2,426 and FIREARMS E41→J56 +2,270 (671u — E41's weapon asks REGENERATED cheap: 4,509→2,403 / 4,473→2,112, which is what created these); FOOD K80→J56 +895×60 (623u); EQUIPMENT E43→C37 +1,085 (124u, borderline in-leash actually); PARTS D39→C37 +3,536 (203u).

**(b) REPURPOSING ANSWER: none needed — un-blind the dispatcher.** Until the bug is found: (i) p4ua ONE-SHOT guarded runs on the table above harvest ~+40-70k each at vol-20 depth (adv_circ D40→A4 alone: 20u × 2,461 ≈ +49k per 73u hop); a hub hull can clear 2-3 of these per idle window. (ii) Tier-2 long lanes → cap at 2 hulls concurrently (contention analysis says 0-3 busy is safe) or hand to lights 20/21 between frontier runs. (iii) The earlier short-lane exhaustion the dispatcher DID see was real but CYCLICAL — recovery half-life ~1-1.5h, so with ~23h of era left there are ~15 more harvest cycles; the dispatcher should re-scan hourly, never declare dead. Era-dead is the wrong frame: the board breathes.

**Rate story impact:** if the dispatcher is fixed, idle-arb at home resumes at roughly its launch-hour rate (~+300k net/hr) on top of tours+factories+frontier — do NOT re-size the fleet on the exhausted-home premise.

**Confidence.** HIGH — this is a direct data contradiction, not an estimate: the lanes above are in the daemon's own market_data at ≤39min age; either the dispatcher doesn't enumerate them or its live verify disagrees with its own cache, and both are engine facts the shipwright can check in minutes.
**Flips:** if the dispatcher's live API quotes genuinely differ from cache (prices moved in <39min across nine independent lanes — implausible but checkable), then the CACHE pipeline is the bug instead; either way an engine bead, not a market verdict.
close_reason: CONSUMED: not contention (latency flat, p90 improved, zero all-busy); 185k hour = penny-raw RNG. C-option adopted (shared+leash+blacklist, spec filed sp-new); 24/25 purchase re-gated on skipped-legs >3-4/hr with frontier posts as the alternative absorption.

title: Container iteration-error restart: no backoff between the 3 attempts + dead-stays-dead after transient dependency outage
description: Found during sp-g5ct's caller audit (pre-existing, NOT introduced by the lazy-conn change — any handler error triggers this path). Two gaps at container_runner.go:393-404 + container.go:66/299: (1) MaxRestartAttempts=3 retries run back-to-back with ZERO backoff — against a fast-failing dependency (routing down = instant connection-refused on localhost) a container burns all 3 in milliseconds and dies; (2) once dead it STAYS dead (supervisor doctrine: found-dead stays dead until human relaunch) even after the dependency self-heals — the routing CLIENT now auto-reconnects (e471723) but a container that died during the outage needs manual relaunch. Fix direction: exponential backoff (or dependency-aware wait) between restart attempts; consider a park-until-dependency-reachable state instead of death for infra-unavailable error classes (rhymes with vwhi park-until-affordable). RULINGS #2 family. 7yej lifecycle-contract sibling.
notes: MERGED 4bdf5e3 (verified 2 files +298/-14): escalating backoff 5s/30s/120s (~2m35s ride-out) via named restartBackoffSchedule table, clock-injected through the generalized sleepOrCancel primitive, lock-free during waits, cancel exits promptly, exhaustion terminalizes identically. 3 clock-driven tests incl -race. Adjacent flagged change: startup jitter routed through injectable clock (prod behavior identical under RealClock; makes real execute() testable). DEPLOY: batched with sp-qr3v's imminent gate (one restart carries both). CLOSE on deploy: behavior is test-locked; live surface only manifests during outages (no flyable acceptance).
close_reason: DEPLOYED pid 4938 (batch w/ qr3v, 6c7b729 tip) with LIVE evidence within minutes: arb-run-TORWIND-21 resumed mid-transit, iteration errors rode the exact 5s/30s/120s schedule ('Retrying after error in 2m0s (attempt 3)' in the log) and the 120s wait outlives the 62s arrival — the pre-h0kr binary would have burned 3 instant retries and died laden. Merged 4bdf5e3 verified (+298/-14), 3 clock-driven -race tests. Follow-ups filed: sp-u64h (auto-relaunch doctrine), arb resume-transit wait (new bead above).

title: Tag tour ledger writes operation_type='tour' — tour report baseline self-corrects
description: P1b's tour report computes the single-lane $/hr GRADUATION baseline as a per-player PROXY: net trade credits / window hours, filtering operation_type<>'tour' — but tour buys/sells currently land UNTAGGED through the shared cargo-tx primitives, so tour activity contaminates the single-lane baseline it is measured against. Thread a tour marker through the cargo-tx write path (operation_type='tour' on ledger rows written by run_tour_coordinator legs) so the report's filter actually excludes tours. Deliberately deferred from P1b (out of scope/risk: shared primitives). The D39-graduation gate readout depends on this being honest once tours run at volume.
notes: DEPLOYED pid 57322 (00:2x, batch w/ xwa1): tour coordinator sets shared.WithOperationContext(ctx, 'tour_run') → cargo-tx writes operation_type='tour' (NormalizedOperationType case added; every other mapping pin-tested unchanged). Verified 6 files +307/-4. End-to-end baseline-honesty test in CI. Deployed BEFORE the first accepted tour so the ledger is clean from tour #1.
