title: Contract-goods inventory pre-positioning: trade engine stocks cheap cross-system goods at home; contract workers source inventory-first (Admiral-endorsed)
description: Admiral design ruling 2026-07-10 (companion to RULINGS #14): contracts stay single-system; the PARALLEL trade engine does the cross-system logistics instead. SHAPE: (1) DEMAND SIGNAL — mine contract history (380+ this era) for recurring goods + quantities => a demand distribution per good (never speculative; RULINGS #6 spirit: stock to measured recurrence). (2) HAUL — tour/trade legs (sp-1ek0 P2 gains a 'deliver-to-home-storage' leg type; idle-arb may prefer contract-recurrent goods on hub-local legs) buy at cheap cross-system asks and deposit into HOME storage (storage ops domain exists — StorageOperationModel; check sp-8yf1's storage-pickup gap first). (3) SOURCE INVENTORY-FIRST — the sourcing optimizer treats home inventory as a zero-ask source ahead of market buys (PlanDeliverySourcing gains an inventory check; never-skip unaffected). (4) ECONOMICS GUARD — pre-position only when (home_ask − foreign_ask) × recurrence-weighted units > haul opportunity cost + capital carry; Guard-1-style logged decisions. DEPENDENCIES: sp-1ek0 P1b (the hauler), contract-demand stats from ledger, a storage-withdrawal path for contract workers (interacts with sp-8yf1). Sequencing: post-P1b; candidate for the sp-1ek0 P2 wave alongside absorption reservations + recovery-externality pricing (all three are 'market-state economics' siblings).
design:
# DESIGN — Contract-goods inventory pre-positioning (sp-dchv)

Investigation: read-only recon, 2026-07-10. Every load-bearing claim cited file:line.
Framing: RULINGS #14 keeps contract legs single-system; THIS feature feeds that constraint
by pre-positioning cross-system goods into HOME storage so the contract worker sources
in-system. The dormant cross-system machinery on the bead is REUSED, not re-invented.

## 0. The load-bearing facts (what actually exists on main)

- **Sourcing seam is ready.** `PlanDeliverySourcing` builds `best` from the in-system market
  candidate at `internal/application/contract/sourcing_optimizer.go:183`; both live callers pass
  `nil` reachability = in-system only (`run_fleet_coordinator.go:390`,
  `queries/evaluate_profitability.go:116`). The `SourcingPlan` struct (sourcing_optimizer.go:115)
  and its consumer `purchaseMarket := plan.Market` (`run_fleet_coordinator.go:401`) are exactly
  where an inventory-first branch slots.
- **Cross-system finder is real and wired.** `FindCheapestMarketsSellingAllSystems`
  (`internal/adapters/persistence/market_repository.go:230-267`, SQL `sell_price ASC`, player-scoped)
  behind interface `CrossSystemMarketFinder` (sourcing_optimizer.go:83). This is the "cheapest
  foreign ask per good" half of the demand→market join.
- **Storage warehouse primitives are real and general.** `StorageShip`
  (`internal/domain/storage/storage_ship.go:22`) holds ARBITRARY goods (`cargoInventory map[string]int`)
  with BOTH halves: deposit (`ReserveSpace`:194 / `ConfirmDeposit`:229 / `ReleaseReservedSpace`:212)
  and withdrawal (`TryReserveCargo`:264 / `ConfirmTransfer`:288 / `CancelReservation`:316). The
  `StorageCoordinator` (`internal/application/storage/coordinator.go`, in-memory) exposes the deposit
  path (`ReserveSpaceForDeposit`:460, `ConfirmDeposit`:491), the withdrawal path (`WaitForCargo`:153),
  and availability query (`GetTotalCargoAvailable`:430).
- **Deposit path proven** in gas siphon: `run_siphon_worker.go:255` does reserve→`TransferCargoCommand`
  →confirm (`TransferCargo` primitive `internal/adapters/api/client.go:987`, command
  `internal/application/gas/commands/transfer_cargo.go:13`).
- **Withdrawal path proven** in manufacturing: `storage_acquire_deliver_executor.go:153` does
  `WaitForCargo`→transfer→`ConfirmTransfer`:189; discovery via
  `StorageSourceFinder.FindRunningOperationForGood` (`storage_source_finder.go:47`).
- **RULINGS #2 restart model:** the coordinator is IN-MEMORY but survives restart because
  `StorageRecoveryService` reloads running `storage_operations` rows and re-reads each storage
  ship's LIVE cargo from the API (`recovery.go:116-135`). Source of truth = physical ship cargo +
  a persisted operation row. No new cargo table needed.
- **Demand signal half-exists.** `HistoryRepository.ContractsStats(...).ByGood`
  (`internal/adapters/persistence/history_repository.go:261-357`, field :52) already yields
  per-good CONTRACT FREQUENCY (count of contracts needing each good), era-scoped by player_id;
  contracts are never scrubbed (`era_repository.go:122-132` omits contracts). **Gap:** `ByGood` is
  a frequency count, NOT a units distribution — units live in `DeliveriesJSON`
  (`models.go:208`), unmarshaled in Go, no SQL good-filter.
- **No global home anchor.** HQ is stored but unused for routing
  (`register_player.go:134` → `player.Metadata["headquarters"]`, only read for CLI display
  `cli/player.go:263`). "Home" today is emergent = a ship's current system
  (`run_tour_coordinator.go:737`).

**THE decisive constraint (single-system withdrawal):** withdrawal is a physical ship→ship
`TransferCargo` at the storage ship's waypoint; the contract worker must fly there in-system
(RULINGS #14). So pre-positioned stock ONLY helps a contract whose **delivery system == the
storage system**. Pre-positioning targets ONE home system in v1; only home-system contracts
benefit. This is by design, not a limitation to fix.

## 1. Minimal v1 (delivers the Admiral's intent)

One dedicated storage hull parked at a chosen HOME waypoint, holding a whitelist of goods that
contract history proves we repeatedly need and that are cheap in a reachable foreign system. The
EXISTING trade/tour engine buys those goods cheap cross-system and DEPOSITS them into the
warehouse (a new sink leg). Contract workers, for home-system contracts, source INVENTORY-FIRST
at zero ask before any market buy. A fail-closed economics guard decides WHETHER to stock.

**In scope v1:** demand miner (frequency + units, home-system-scoped, current-era prices);
warehouse storage-op seam; haul-to-storage deposit leg (pseudo-sink); inventory-first sourcing
with reservation; deposit-side economics guard.

**Deferred (explicit):** multi-system warehouses; the "home never sells the good at all"
unsourceable-contract trigger (highest value but SPECULATIVE on price → violates #6/#4 spirit
without a known home ask — needs its own ruling); dynamic capital-carry pricing (v1 uses a
static min-savings-margin floor + a spend cap); auto-provisioning/buying a storage hull (v1
dedicates an existing parked hull, RULINGS #6/#7); idle-arb PREFERENCE for contract-recurrent
goods on hub-local legs (nice-to-have layer on top of the deposit leg).

## 2. Storage seam decision (with evidence)

**Reuse main's `StorageShip`/`StorageCoordinator` machinery; do NOT revive the parked sp-8yf1
branch; add ONE small warehouse constructor.**

- Rejected — revive parked sp-8yf1 (`d4a7866`, branch `shipwright/sp-8yf1`): it builds
  `STORAGE_COLLECT_SELL` (gas DISPOSITION — collect from storage and SELL at market), which is
  the OPPOSITE data flow (out of storage → market) and is NOT on main. Its `GasDispositionProducer`
  (threshold + dedup + idle-hauler decision engine) is a useful STRUCTURAL PRECEDENT for our
  pre-positioning producer, but we do not depend on the branch.
- The blocker: `NewStorageOperation` requires ≥1 extractor ship (`operation.go:77-79`). A warehouse
  has ZERO extractors — cargo arrives from a HAULER (tour leg), not an extractor. So reuse needs a
  `NewWarehouseOperation` constructor (OperationTypeCustom `operation.go:21`, 0 extractors, ≥1
  storage ship, supportedGoods = the whitelist) OR relaxing the extractor invariant for CUSTOM.
  Everything downstream (`StorageShip`, coordinator, recovery, `StorageSourceFinder`,
  `storage_operations` persistence, RULINGS #2 reconstruction) then works UNCHANGED.
- This is the cleanest seam: no facility model, no new persistence table, no new transfer
  primitive — the warehouse is a parked hull whose cargo IS the inventory, deposited and withdrawn
  by the two already-proven protocols.

## 3. Economics guard sketch (#4-compliant, fail CLOSED)

Guard lives on the DEPOSIT (stock) decision, not the withdrawal. Withdrawal from inventory is
always correct (paid-for = zero marginal ask), subject only to reservation + never-skip fallthrough.

Stock good G only when ALL are known and true (any unknown → do NOT stock; #4):
- `recurrence_weight(G)` from `ContractsStats().ByGood` ≥ `minRecurrence` (measured, never
  speculative — RULINGS #6). Requires per-good UNITS too (miner gap, Lane A).
- `home_ask(G)` KNOWN (cheapest in-home-system ask) AND `foreign_ask(G)` KNOWN (cheapest reachable
  foreign ask via `FindCheapestMarketsSellingAllSystems`). Unknown either → fail closed, skip.
- `savings = (home_ask − foreign_ask)` > 0 AND `savings × expected_units > minMarginFloor`
  (static floor stands in for capital-carry pricing in v1).
- `warehouse_capital_outstanding + foreign_ask × units ≤ spendCap` (a new pre-positioning spend
  cap sibling to sp-w3he's cross-container cap; must not touch the non-tunable 50k reserve #4/#5).

**Haul opportunity cost is EMERGENT, not a separate guard.** The deposit leg is offered to the
tour solver with a synthetic bid = `home_ask` (so the solver's `margin = home_ask − foreign_ask`
= the real savings, `tour_solver.py:282`). If a normal arb SELL leg is more profitable, the
solver picks that instead — the solver's own profit maximization prices the opportunity cost.
We only owe an HONEST pseudo-price (discounted for carry/uncertainty to stay fail-closed).

## 4. Build lanes (ordered, independently gateable)

**Lane B — Warehouse storage seam  [RISKIEST — load-bearing, RULINGS #2]**
Files: `internal/domain/storage/operation.go` (add `NewWarehouseOperation` / relax extractor
invariant for CUSTOM), `internal/application/storage/recovery.go` (ensure the owning coordinator
invokes recovery so cargo reconstructs on boot), a coordinator that owns the warehouse op +
registers the storage ship. Acceptance: a warehouse op persists a `storage_operations` row;
after a simulated restart its cargo is reconstructed from the ship API and re-registered
(RULINGS #2); a deposit→withdraw round-trip via the coordinator succeeds in an integration test.
Riskiest because it touches aggregate invariants, restart resilience, and a physically dedicated
hull (RULINGS #7 ownership).

**Lane A — Demand miner (candidate list)**  [parallel with B]
Files: extend `HistoryRepository.ContractsStats` (or a new `demand_miner.go` in
`internal/application/trading`) to emit per-good FREQUENCY + expected UNITS (unmarshal
`DeliveriesJSON`), filtered to the HOME system's delivery destinations, joined against
`FindCheapestMarketsSellingAllSystems` for foreign ask + `FindCheapestMarketSelling` for home
ask. Output: ranked `[good, recurrence, expected_units, home_ask, foreign_ask+waypoint,
projected_savings]`, fail-closed rows dropped. Acceptance: given seeded contract history +
market_data, returns the correct ranked home-system candidates with savings; drops goods with
unknown home OR foreign ask; respects `minRecurrence`.

**Lane C — Haul-to-storage deposit leg (pseudo-sink)**  [needs A + B]
Files (cross-language): `services/routing-service/utils/tour_solver.py` (three spots — the
`bid>0` pairing gate `:249`, the sell-pool quote `:233`, the margin `:282` — accept a synthetic
storage sink with bid = `home_ask`), `handlers/tour_handler.py`, `pkg/proto/routing/routing.proto`
(new trade/leg kind, mirrors bc27's `held_liquidation` field addition), `grpc_routing_client.go`,
`internal/domain/routing/tour.go` (leg kind — today only `TourTrade.IsBuy` bool `:72`),
`internal/application/trading/commands/run_tour_coordinator.go` (`executeSell` branches to a
deposit primitive reusing siphon `reserve→TransferCargo→ConfirmDeposit`; inject `StorageCoordinator`;
BYPASS the live-price re-verify `:572` for storage legs — no live bid exists). Interacts with
bc27 held_liquidation split (a deposit is the structural mirror: buy-with-no-sell vs
sell-with-no-buy). Acceptance: solver emits a deposit leg when savings justify it and NOT when a
better arb sell exists; executor deposits into the warehouse; no live-price crash on the sink leg.

**Lane D — Inventory-first sourcing + withdrawal**  [needs B]
Files: `sourcing_optimizer.go` `PlanDeliverySourcing` (before the in-system market candidate at
`:183`, check `StorageSourceFinder` for a running warehouse op IN the delivery system supporting
the good with `GetTotalCargoAvailable > 0` → emit a zero-ask `SourcingPlan` with a new
`Source=inventory` marker + `Market = storage waypoint`, `UnitAsk = 0`); add a delivery-system
filter to `StorageSourceFinder` (`storage_source_finder.go:47`); `run_fleet_coordinator.go` around
`:401` branches: inventory plan → fly to storage waypoint + withdraw (`WaitForCargo`→transfer→
`ConfirmTransfer`, mirror manufacturing) instead of market buy. Acceptance: a home-system contract
for a stocked good sources from inventory at zero ask; when inventory is exhausted MID-RUN it
falls through to the market candidate unchanged (never-skip, RULINGS #1); a reservation prevents
two contracts double-claiming the same units.

Ordering: **B (∥ A) → C, D in parallel.** C and D both consume B's warehouse; C also needs A's
candidate list to know WHAT to haul; D needs A only indirectly (it consumes whatever B holds).

## 5. Open questions (need Admiral/captain ruling before C/D dispatch)

1. **Home/storage anchor:** default to stored HQ metadata (`register_player.go:134`) as the home
   system, overridable by a `--storage-waypoint`/`--home-system` flag (RULINGS #5)? Confirm the
   anchor — there is no global home today.
2. **Storage hull:** v1 dedicates ONE existing parked hull as the warehouse (pinned, no purchase,
   RULINGS #6/#7) — confirm, and confirm which hull class / capacity target.
3. **Goods whitelist:** fully auto from the miner (top-N recurrent + cheap-cross-system, gated by
   `minRecurrence`), or an Admiral-approved allowlist on top? Recommend auto + a max-goods cap.
4. **Pre-positioning capital budget:** what ceiling on credits tied up in warehouse inventory
   (the `spendCap` in §3), and its relation to the 50k reserve and sp-w3he cross-container cap?
5. **"Home never sells the good" case:** the highest-value scenario (home-system contracts for a
   good no home market sells are currently un-sourceable in-system and stall) but SPECULATIVE on
   price. In scope for v1 as a separate trigger, or deferred to a v2 with its own ruling?

Confidence: HIGH on the seam decision, the sourcing hook, and the single-system constraint (all
verified on main). MEDIUM on the miner units-aggregation cost (JSON unmarshal, no SQL filter) and
on Lane C's cross-language solver change + live-price bypass (the second-riskiest work).
notes: DIAGNOSIS (ed15ea1 deployed): the zero-deposit escalation was a DIAGNOSIS BLIND SPOT — behavior is CORRECT ECONOMICS. Funnel/139 re-plans: 83% zero-assembled because the hull was >1 gate from home (graph-adjacent assembly ALREADY implemented + correct); the 17% that assembled lost the hold honestly to richer arb/liquidation (verified vs the real solver: deposits ride when hold has room, lose when arb margin > savings). Warehouse fills only at the near-home+spare-hold+weak-arb confluence — never hit in 3h. FIX DEPLOYED: one verdict line per re-plan naming the distinct zero-reason + funnel counts (silent zeros impossible); candidate values byte-identical. DESIGN CALL ESCALATED TO ADMIRAL: (a) dedicated home-anchored deposit hauler [agent + harbormaster recommend — savings-rate vs the hull's idle-arb alternative is directly priceable from the demand table], (b) deposit quota in tour economics [RULINGS #4 territory], (c) scheduled home-detour, (d) let the new verdict data quantify missed-deposit value first. The verdict lines feed sjxi.

title: Test harness defaults sqlite foreign_keys=OFF — FK violations invisible to every real-DB test (1hp9 class)
description: Found in sp-1hp9: NewTestConnection leaves PRAGMA foreign_keys=OFF (mattn/sqlite default), so even tests through the REAL persistence layer cannot catch FK violations — the idle-arb DOA bug survived port fakes AND would have survived real-DB tests. FIX: default foreign_keys=ON in NewTestConnection; run the full suite; triage any tests that were silently violating FKs (each one is a latent prod bug, fix or explicitly document); keep an opt-out only if a test legitimately needs it. The gorm-AutoMigrated DDL already carries the constraints (verified in 1hp9's test).
close_reason: Merged a427768 (verified +65/-0, 7 files): PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON is the NewTestConnection default (after AutoMigrate, error-checked). 20 latent FK-violation test cases surfaced across 6 files — ALL fixture gaps (missing parent container/player rows real producers always write), zero production bugs, zero enforcement escapes (grep clean). Full suite green in the gate. Test-infra bead: acceptance is CI-side (gate-proven), no live surface — closing as orchestrator. The 1hp9 fake-blindness class is now visible to every real-DB test.

title: sp-1ek0 model recalibration cadence + tier-at-time extraction (post-pf60)
description: Two follow-ups from the P0 completion: (1) RECALIBRATION CADENCE: make calibrate-market-model is manual; run it before P1b's first supervised tour and ~daily during the graduation window (each run is gate-protected — form+coverage must PASS or the old artifact stands). Automation (cron/CI) optional later. (2) TIER-AT-TIME JOIN: once sp-pf60 data accumulates (~1-2 days of price-history rows carrying supply/activity), extract.py should PREFER the history row's tier-at-observation over the market_data tier-now join for ladder tagging — closes the D39-class labeling gap for real. Small extract.py change + a re-fit; artifact fit_version bumps to 2.
notes: LANDED via captain-gate: main b2872e6 (squash-merge on 6309950). Gate: go build clean + go test ./... all green + squash-merge (GatePassed/Merged true, not stale, not empty). First gate attempt hit a transient shared go-build-cache corruption (env, not code — main built clean on retry). Rebased clean onto 6309950 (no model/ overlap with P1a). Real calibration: FORM PASS (0.9494/step, drop err 5.7%); COVERAGE PASS (all 19 well-populated sides in bounds, per-side floor); tier_at_time 95.5% (4595/4812). Shipped fit_version 2 artifact. B-PLUS methodology: units-weighted per-tranche estimator (HIGH|GROWING sell 0.7928->0.9191, thin/ungated at 23 obs) + per-side COVERAGE floor + mark_thin_sides. 22/22 model pytest. Worktree kept, NOT pushed, bd NOT closed per brief. Recovery-with-tier-at-time deferred (future sp-bkjz refinement).
close_reason: COMPLETE: tier-at-time extraction (nearest-before history join, 95.5% coverage) + pf60 KeyError fix + B-PLUS methodology (units-weighted estimator Σlog r/Σ(units/tv) + per-side 30-floor) MERGED-VERIFIED b2872e6 + PUSHED + v2 artifact ACTIVATED (routing restarted). Both gates PASS (FORM 0.9494 byte-invariant; COVERAGE 19/19 sides). HIGH|GROWING exhibit double-protected (0.9191 + thin-flagged). Cadence procedure: recalibrate before P1b graduation runs + ~daily (this run satisfies pre-first-tour). Future refinement (not a bead yet): recovery-with-tier-at-time.

title: market_price_history lacks supply/activity — historical ladders can't be tier-tagged at observation time (blocks time-consistent model calibration)
description: Found by sp-hqrb refinement: the model fit tags impact ladders with the CURRENT market_data supply/activity, but tiers drift — 204 severe sell-steps exist yet none carry their at-the-time tier (D39's LIMITED|WEAK incident observations now read as RESTRICTED-era tiers or are gone entirely). FIX: record supply + activity columns on market_price_history at capture time (scouts already hold TradeGoodData.Supply/Activity when writing history — two columns + write-path change + backfill-not-possible note). Enables time-consistent tier tagging for all future sp-1ek0 calibrations; also lets extract.py join tier-at-time instead of tier-now. Small Go change + migration.
notes:
session died
PREMISE CORRECTED (tier-capture agent): the Go capture path has existed since 2025-11-24 (6ea64647) — supply/activity are columns on market_price_history from its ORIGINAL migration, and market_scanner.recordPriceChanges threads them. Zero prod Go changes needed. What the agent shipped: full test coverage for the capture chain (3 new test files, domain/persistence/scanner) + extract_control_series now exposes tier-at-time (COALESCE). The REAL residual gap = extract_ladders still joins tier-NOW — moved to sp-bkjz.

Fix riding sp-bkjz's merge (drop tier cols pre-merge, preserving pre-pf60 recovery semantics + honest regression test w/ the real frame shape). title: Routing service is a HARD daemon dependency: boot fails if routing down (grpc.WithBlock), no post-boot fallback — resilience gap
description: Found during sp-4xn4 (routing-venv agent): daemon main.go:162-166 dials the routing service with grpc.WithBlock+5s at BOOT and returns error if unreachable -> daemon won't start when routing is down; post-boot, if the routing service dies, routing RPCs error with no mock/degraded fallback (MockRoutingClient is only reachable when address is empty pre-defaults, i.e. never on the boot path). Given RULINGS #2 (we patch live; restarts must be resilient) and that launchd KeepAlive can race daemon boot vs routing boot: (a) consider non-blocking dial + lazy connect w/ clear degraded logging, or ordered launchd dependency, or bounded retry-at-boot; (b) decide + document the intended behavior when routing dies mid-flight (scout tours, fueled tours, PartitionFleet callers). Also relevant to sp-1ek0 P1a: the tour planner treats routing as hard-dep at the DAEMON level while the tour design is fail-open at the TOUR level — keep those consistent.
notes: MERGED e471723 (verified 3 files +149/-10): grpc.NewClient lazy conn (auto-reconnect, no WithBlock), boot-time 2s reachability probe logs honestly either way, 3 hermetic resilience tests. Caller audit CLEAN: no call site assumed conn-established; tour fails open (verified at run_tour_coordinator.go:233-239), PlanRoute/PartitionFleet propagate to the runner's bounded restart. DEPLOY BATCHED with sp-wj0h's imminent gate (rides the same restart). CLOSE after boot log shows the new reachability line — that's the live evidence the new path executed.
close_reason: Live evidence at deploy pid 76445: boot log prints 'Routing service reachable at localhost:50051' — the new lazy-conn + probe path executed. Merged e471723 (verified 3 files), hermetic resilience tests green, caller audit clean. Follow-up gap filed as sp-h0kr (in build).

title: CONSULT (fleet-architect + analyst refute): buy 1-2 light haulers (~250k ea) as dedicated arb-run flyers — the standing board has more certified legs than hulls
description: refutation: sought from fleet-architect (this bead) — REFUTE the buy before credits move; fire-and-forget per rule 3. SNAPSHOT 23:00Z: treasury 1.17M (1M capex trigger crossed); era ends 07-12 13:00Z (~62h). THE CASE: analyst's standing board holds MORE certified ≥2k/u legs than free hulls — lab C37→JP61 +3,710×20, adv_circ home→GQ92 +2,129, plating D40→GQ92-C37 +3,783 (proven +28.5k/pass), bio K79→A4 +2,800 (proven +35.3k/pass) — while our only arb hulls are the heavy (loop duty) and 12/B (factory-worker pool, contested). A dedicated arb light (~250k = 21% of treasury, inside the 25% line) flying 3-4 one-shots/hr at ~+25-35k each ≈ +75-140k/hr per hull, payback ~2-3h. REFUTE ANGLES: (1) do the legs SURVIVE a dedicated flyer (sink vol/tranche crush at 3-4 visits/hr — rotation math)? (2) is hull-time actually the binder, or does 1z2h's idle-arb on contract hulls already harvest these legs for free? (3) 1 or 2 hulls, and which yard/class (speed vs hold for one-shot legs)? (4) does the same 250k better spend on ANYTHING else right now?
notes: ARCHITECT RULE ADOPTED (23:10Z): route-idle-first executing — FRIGATE T1 (command-pinned, speed 36, 40 hold, idle at J56 all evening) routed to C37 for the board's top unflown leg (lab C37→JP61 +3,710×20); arb one-shots are NOT contract work so the Admiral's frigate-contracts ban doesn't apply, and 20u legs fit a 40-hold perfectly. 1z2h idle-arb capture = ZERO rows so far (40min live, contract hulls busy) — gap is real but 1z2h gets its acceptance window. DECISION GATE (next 1-2 wakes): if frigate covers 1-2 legs/hr AND idle-arb rows start appearing → BUY 0; if the board still outruns flyers → buy EXACTLY ONE fast light (never two — 25% line + four-leg board crush per architect). Heavy stays on its own loop legs (bulk class, n9vb separation honored).
close_reason: DECIDED: BUY 0 — architect's route-idle-first refutation vindicated exactly: idle-arb + frigate + heavy cover the certified board; a purchased light would capture only the delta of an already-harvested board. Capex redirects to the next constraint (frontier/absorption).

comments.author: Andres Camacho
comments.text:
FLEET-ARCHITECT REFUTE — sp-5cyp (buy 1-2 arb light haulers ~250k ea). Treasury 1.17M, era ends ~62h.

VERDICT: QUALIFIED REFUTE. This is NOT a structural NO — do not lump it with the dead 5x buys. The era clock FAVORS a fast-payback hull (2-3h payback over ~62h remaining = ~20x if the payback is real), and demand is partly certified (plating +28.5k/pass, bio +35.3k/pass are PROVEN passes, not projections). Refuting on the era-reset template here would be wrong. But the 1-2-as-framed overstates in three measurable ways. Net: route idle capacity FIRST (zero capital), then at most ONE fast light — not two.

THE THREE SEAMS:

(1) "MORE legs than hulls" undercounts IDLE capacity — the binder may be ASSIGNMENT, not hull-count. The case counts only "free arb-dedicated hulls" (heavy on loop, 12/B contested) and ignores idle hull-HOURS: T3 is unassigned and ~half the fleet sits docked-idle. Any idle hull with hold can fly these legs. So before 250k moves, route idle hulls + measure what 1z2h idle-arb ALREADY harvests on these four named legs. This is the captain's own strongest angle and it is correct: if idle-arb already skims them, the dedicated hull captures the DELTA, not the full +75-140k/hr — and the 2-3h payback (computed on 100% greenfield capture) stretches 2-3x. DEMAND: current idle-arb realized capture on lab/adv_circ/plating/bio before buying.

(2) +75-140k/hr is a GREENFIELD CEILING; tranche/sink-crush at 3-4 visits/hr is unmodeled. Per-pass margin IS evidenced (+28.5k/+35.3k proven); the 3-4 passes/hr FREQUENCY and its SUSTAIN are the assumptions. "lab +3,710x20" is a 20-unit tranche — 3-4 passes/hr on the same sink depress its bid and exhaust the tranche (absorption ~42u/tick baseline). ONE flyer rotating four legs ~= 1 visit/leg/hr = gentle, tranches replenish. TWO flyers, or one flyer concentrating the best leg, crush the very legs that justify them. DEMAND: per-leg sink depth + margin-decay-vs-cadence. Until measured, +75-140k/hr is a ceiling, not a run-rate.

(3) Cap at ONE, and a FAST LIGHT — not two, not a heavy. Rule-3 line: 1 @ 250k = 21% (inside); 2 @ 500k = 43% (breaches 25%). Two also multiplies crush on a fixed four-leg board. One-shot arb legs are tranche-limited (~20u), so CYCLE TIME (speed + dock overhead) binds, NOT hold — a fast light dominates a heavy for this duty. Do NOT conflate with sp-n9vb's bulk-loop heavy (360 cargo is dead weight on a 20u tranche; different job).

DECISION RULE for the captain (actionable within the hour):
- Route idle hulls (T3 + docked-idle) onto the four legs and read what 1z2h idle-arb captures. If idle capacity covers the board -> buy 0.
- If a gap survives routing -> buy EXACTLY ONE fast light, rotate all four legs (tranche-safe).
- Do NOT buy two (breaches 25% + crush risk on a four-leg board).
- Treat +75-140k/hr as a ceiling; the proven per-pass margins are real, the frequency/sustain is not yet.

THREAD LINKAGE (flag, not a block): these are TRADING legs. If the Admiral's still-pending 5x objection is that trading is near a ceiling, that REINFORCES "route idle before buying." But the fast payback means this tactical buy does not require resolving the trading-vs-contracts steer first — it stands on its own leg-specific evidence. Not closing sp-5cyp.

title: routing-service venv BROKEN (stale shebang -> /Users/andres.dandrea/projects/... path) — VRP/TSP likely degraded to MockRoutingClient

BLOCKS sp-1ek0 P1a (planner needs the live service).
notes:
session died
INVESTIGATION VERDICT (routing-venv): fleet is NOT on mock — real OR-Tools service alive on :50051 since Jul 6 (launchd com.spacetraders.routing, real Dijkstra logging today; zero mock fallbacks in daemon.log ever). Empty routing.address is rewritten to localhost:50051 by defaults.go:57. Breakage is NARROW: only pip/console-script shebangs + pyvenv.cfg carry the dead path (interpreter symlink resolves) — blocks pip installs (bit P0 setup), a durability landmine, not a live degradation. Severity: hygiene fix. Agent rebuilding via venv.new atomic swap + standalone proof on :50061, live :50051 untouched. My original 'likely degraded to mock' framing was WRONG — corrected.
Agent report: venv rebuilt IN PLACE + proven (real VRP discriminator on :50061: 2 ships x 4 markets balanced-by-geometry vs mock 1:1; Dijkstra path real). NEVER-MOCK verdict definitive (defaults.go:57 rewrites empty->localhost:50051; 0 mock fallbacks in daemon history). Production wiring CORRECT (launchd runs run.sh directly; Go manager binary unused). Remaining: pin+split requirements gate (in flight), then MY controlled routing restart + verify + close. Hard-dep resilience gap filed separately.
PROCESS DEVIATION (accepted): 4fbe4c7 landed via raw ff-merge, not captain-gate — agent self-reported; content verified identical to a gated result (zero Go files; go build green; no history rewrite possible or wanted). Codified as RULINGS #13: only captain-gate merges agent work to main; briefs must quote the full invocation.
close_reason: COMPLETE: venv rebuilt in place (correct shebangs) + deps PINNED to proven-live (grpcio==1.81.1, ortools==9.15.6755, protobuf==6.33.6; 4fbe4c7) + model deps split to requirements-model.txt + REAL-VRP proof on the pinned combo + controlled restart executed (new PID 20357, :50051 LISTENING, version-neutral by construction). Never-mock verdict stands (fleet was always on real OR-Tools). Follow-ups filed: sp-g5ct (hard-dep resilience), make-target for .venv-p0 = fold into sp-1ek0 P1a.

TRADE-ANALYST FRONTIER EVALUATION (sp-zgbk) — 2026-07-09 ~22:30Z. Board run: all-pairs on live cache; KM70 reads 1.5-7.5h; ZC66 1.4-1.6h (F's stream WORKING); JP61 2.6-2.8h; home ≤1.5h. PA3/UQ16 still 21-22h — 18's sweep had NOT landed in market_data as of this run.

**(1) KM70 VERDICT: PENNY-CLASS ORE BELT — no lane, no loop-C. Not remotely GQ92-class.** 17 reads, 5 waypoints: three ore exporters (IRON/COPPER/ALUMINUM_ORE ask 40-50, vol 120, ABUNDANT), one quartz/silicon exporter (F26Z ask 16/26 — marginally cheaper than home B7 exchange for F45 feeds, not worth the jump), one ANTIMATTER exchange (Z29D 5,248/5,845 v36 — vs home I53 ask ~5,050: +198/u, dead). **KM70 imports NOTHING — zero sinks.** Its only fleet value: (a) cheap ore sourcing IF an ore contract's source assignment ever routes there (it shouldn't — home J56/B7 are equal-priced without the jump); (b) probe 15 can idle there as a jump-point sensor. **(2) PA3/UQ16 LANE-#1: VERDICT DEFERRED — the promised fresh read is not in cache yet** (all legs still 21-22h). So the captain can act the moment it lands, here is the PRE-REGISTERED RULE (no further consult needed): **REVIVE lane #1 if, on the fresh read: EB1Z SHIP_PARTS ask ≤4,500 AND avg(XE3X,DF9E) parts bids ≥6,500 AND FF4B clothing ask ≤3,200 AND EB1Z clothing bid ≥4,500** → projected ≥300k/loop, fly it. **RETIRE if any TWO legs fail.** The 22h-old values (3,994 / 7,238&6,737 / 2,929 / 4,888) sat comfortably inside — mild decay still passes, so I LEAN REVIVE, pending data. **(3) PORTFOLIO CHANGES from the fresh board — three real ones:**
- **LOOP B (home⇄ZC66) IS NOW CERTIFIED** — F's continuous stream delivers: ZC66-BA9D parts bid 7,640 / adv_circ 6,647; AX1B adv_circ 6,507; A14A parts 7,446, all 1.4-1.6h fresh. Out-leg anchor: D39 parts 3,739 → BA9D +3,901/u. Check the firearms back-leg (A14A) at dispatch. A-B alternation is GO.
- **HOME ADV_CIRCUITRY fully recovered and PROMOTED**: D40 ask fell to 3,469 while home A4 bid re-read at 6,680 (1.5h) → in-system make-lane +3,211/u × 20. Unfreeze order updates to: MEDICINE (88k/pass by depth) → **ADV_CIRC (+3,211×20=64k, single stop)** → PARTS → PLATING → CLOTHING.
- **JP61 grew component sinks**: AD9C parts bid 7,738 (v15), BB5Z/F17D/EC2B adv_circ 6.3-6.6k, and home-C37's LAB_INSTRUMENTS export (ask 2,649!) → JP61-XZ7D 6,359 = **+3,710/u × 20** — the fattest fresh cross-gate leg on the board. Loop A's out-leg should now be SINK-FRESHNESS-ROUTED among GQ92/JP61/ZC66 rather than fixed to GQ92; JP61 carries lab_instruments no other loop uses.
- Bonus intra-home idle-gap lane for the J56 hub hull (feeds sp-5bmq lever 2): DRUGS J56 ask 2,948 → H49 bid 5,625 = +2,677/u × 20, both reads ≤1.1h.

**(4) STANDING FLAGGING — accepted, with the honest mechanics:** I don't self-wake (idle protocol; no monitors between wakes). I WILL run this all-pairs board on EVERY wake and flag any new ≥2k/u cross-gate lane as a dated one-line entry on THIS bead. title: EPIC: multi-hop trade-tour optimizer (Python/OR-Tools) — depth-aware, supply/demand-curve-calibrated planner + guard-bound Go executor
description: Admiral-designed 2026-07-09. A heavy runs an optimized multi-stop tour: buy at hop, sell profitable cargo at next, rebuy, continue — whole run optimized for credits/HOUR. VIABILITY CONFIRMED: the Python OR-Tools routing service already exists (routing.proto: PlanRoute/OptimizeTour/OptimizeFueledTour/PartitionFleet) — add an OptimizeTradeTour RPC. ARCHITECTURE (RULINGS-compliant): Python = STATELESS PLANNER ONLY (snapshot in -> ordered plan out: hops + per-hop buy/sell tranches + projected P&L/leg + $/hr); Go daemon = executor container (single-writer #3) reusing travel()/guards; plan BINDS routing, prices re-verified LIVE per leg (rqwm lesson: guards must bind); leg degrades below plan-by-X% -> skip + stateless re-plan RPC from current position/cargo; planner down -> fail OPEN to single-lane trade-route. PHASE 0 (Admiral requirement, THE differentiator): MARKET MODEL — price-impact + recovery curves fitted per (supply, activity) from OWNED data: ledger transaction sequences (D39 4-sell ladder 1844->1562/-15%%; K79 -47%%/churn; C37 vol-6 cliff = held-out validation incidents) + scout observation time-series for recovery half-lives. Concave sell / convex buy curves piecewise-linearized by tranche -> LP stays linear, milliseconds at our sizes (~50-100 markets, <=8 hops, hold 225-360). PHASE 1: OptimizeTradeTour RPC (single hull, $/hr objective, curve-aware) + Go executor, one heavy. PHASE 2: re-plan-on-abort; multi-hull ABSORPTION RESERVATIONS (w3he ledger pattern applied to market depth — prevents self-competition); contract-good opportunism (meshes with 1z2h); curve-predicted defer timing. PHASE 3: fleet-level tour partitioning (PartitionFleet shape exists). PREREQS: probe-flock scan cadence (fresher snapshots = trustworthy curves; captain buy pending st-wisp-onno); 1z2h phase 2 landed. Crude precedents in prod validating the concept: 2dv4 Guard-2 absorption bound, 1z2h ladder-breaker (stays as runtime backstop). Est: phase 0+1 = 1 fable (model+planner) + 1 opus (executor).
notes: P2 WAVE OPENING (Admiral ruled bc27 = C, 2026-07-10): bc27 held_liquidation split IN BUILD now (first wave lane — opens the proto). Wave remainder, in order: sp-dchv (inventory pre-positioning — dispatches at the next free slot; reuse constraint on its bead), then absorption reservations + recovery-externality pricing (solver economics — DESIGN PASS first per RULINGS #9 before any build lane; the spec sketches them but the pricing shape deserves a deliberate design).

title: CONSULT (trade-analyst): contract cycle-time decomposition + net-per-contract — where do the 30-min stall tails come from, and which contract classes are net-negative after sourcing+fuel?
description: refutation: n/a — measurement request feeding the improvement plan. SNAPSHOT 21:15Z: contracts ~250-280k/hr GROSS but 2h NET ≈ 150k/2h income-minus-sourcing (70% cost ratio); median fulfillment gap 5-9min with 30min+ tails (e.g. 20:17→20:50); 6/6 fleet, hubs+homing+cargo-fit live; API confirmed serial (zero overlapping accept→fulfill windows in 400+ contracts). QUESTIONS (bead-note): (1) Decompose the last ~30 cycles: negotiate / travel-to-source / buy / travel-to-deliver / fulfill — what makes a tail cycle 30min (far RNG source? negotiate latency? worker respawn)? (2) NET per contract class: bucket by payout size — are sub-10k contracts net-negative after goods+fuel, and what's their share of hull-time vs income? (3) Contract-hull idle share: % of time hulls sit homed between claims — if >25%, is hub-local idle-gap arb (one-shot arb-runs between contract claims) worth the claim churn? (4) Your top cycle-time lever ranked by $/hr recovered.
notes:
TRADE-ANALYST MEASUREMENT (sp-5bmq: cycle decomposition + net-per-class + idle + levers) — 2026-07-09 ~21:30Z
Corpus: 450 fulfilled contracts linked (full era), last-30 decomposed segment-by-segment, per-contract net = payment − sourcing (fuel attributed separately: refuels happen BETWEEN cycles during homing, ~2-3% of gross, flips nothing).

**(1) DECOMPOSITION, LAST 30 — first the good news: THE HUBS WORKED. Median negotiate gap is now 0.0 min** (was mean 25.7m before hubs/homing). Medians: gap 0.0 / accept→first-buy 0.7 / buy-dwell 0.0 / deliver 1.9 — a median cycle is ~3min end-to-end. The 30-min tails are INSIDE cycles now, and the autopsy of all 8 tails (>15min) shows exactly two causes:
- **FAR-SOURCE ASSIGNMENT (5/8):** prev-dest→source legs of 622-804 units (COPPER_ORE→J56 623u, AMMONIA_ICE→H51 761u, ELECTRONICS→D39 804u, IRON_ORE→J56 735u ×2). At spd-15 that's ~18-23min of travel. The chain hands the next contract to the hull that just fulfilled — even when a hub-parked hull sits next to the new source. NOT negotiate latency, NOT respawn.
- **BUY-DWELL SUPPLY-WAITS (4/8, one overlaps):** first-buy→last-buy stalls of 17-32min on SCARCE/LIMITED goods (ELECTRONICS 32.4m, ASSAULT_RIFLES 26.4m, IRON_ORE 18.8-19.3m, COPPER_ORE 16.5m) — the buyer sits at the market waiting for tranche/supply regen to finish the required units.
- **Worst single contract in the corpus: ELECTRONICS, net −891,812** — the buyer laddered home's post-feed SCARCE electronics ask into the sky to fill a contract. This is the catastrophic version of the dwell-stall class.

**(2) NET BY PAYOUT CLASS (full corpus, sourcing netted):**
| class | n | income | net | net/contract | net $/hr-of-hull-time | time share | income share |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| <10k | 135 | 754k | +393k | +2,911 | 11,939/hr | **46.0%** | **2.4%** |
| 10-25k | 73 | 1.19M | +708k | +9,701 | 70,809/hr | 14.0% | 3.8% |
| 25-75k | 76 | 3.30M | +1.84M | +24,220 | 230,285/hr | 11.2% | 10.5% |
| >75k | 166 | 26.3M | +13.8M | +83,107 | **666,244/hr** | 28.9% | 83.3% |
Sub-10k contracts are NOT net-negative (avg +2,911 — the Admiral's never-skip ruling costs nothing in cash) but they are TIME PARASITES: 46% of hull-time for 2.4% of income, 56x worse $/hr than the >75k class. The fix under the ruling is not skipping — it's making them cheap (lever 1 below assigns them to the nearest hub hull, where their true cycle is ~2-3min).

**(3) IDLE SHARE: fleet contract-utilization is 11%** (5.9 active hull-hours of 54 in the last 6h; per-hull 2-22%, best TORWIND-B 22%). The pipeline is SERIAL (confirmed), so this is structural: only ~1 hull is ever contract-active. Answer to the threshold question: idle is 89%, so YES — hub-local idle-gap arb clears the bar by 3.5x, with one design rule: gate each parked hull's one-shot arb to lanes INSIDE its hub radius (E42 hull → E42→D40 machinery micro-runs; J56 hull → J56 ore→F46/H49 legs; C37 hull → C37-local) so the hull is back before the next claim lands (claims arrive ~every 18min; a local run is 5-8min). Claim churn ≈ zero. ALSO: with 9 hulls on a serial line and 6 hubs, hulls 7-9 add nothing but positioning redundancy — reassign 2-3 to the trade fleet outright.

**(4) LEVERS RANKED BY $/HR RECOVERED:**
1. **NEAREST-HULL CLAIM ASSIGNMENT** (kill far-source tails): 8 of last 30 cycles lost 15-25min each to far legs; cutting mean cycle from ~12min to ~5-6min on a SERIAL pipeline nearly doubles contracts/hr → **+200-250k/hr**. One dispatch-rule change; hubs already position the hulls.
2. **HUB-LOCAL IDLE-GAP ARB** (per (3)): 2 local guarded runs/hull/hr at +15-25k across ~6 parked hulls → **+100-200k/hr**. Uses p4ua one-shots as already built.
3. **SOURCING-COST GUARD** (kill the −891k class): before buying, cap the ladder — if projected sourcing > payment − floor OR ask > 1.5x the good's recent min ask, DEFER the contract (not skip) and source at the alternate market (electronics: GQ92-F44 exports at 2,367 vs home's laddered 6k+; ores: split J56/B7). Average **+20-40k/hr**, but its real value is tail-risk: one prevented electronics-class hit pays a full day of it.
4. Sub-10k right-sizing folds into lever 1 (nearest = cheapest for micro-contracts).

**Confidence.** HIGH on decomposition, class table, idle math (full-population ledger, exact linkage). MEDIUM on lever $/hr estimates (serial-pipeline arithmetic, not simulation). Honest gap: refuel costs sit between cycles and are excluded from per-contract nets (~2-3% of gross fleet-wide).
**Flips:** if claim assignment is ALREADY nearest-hull (engine says so), then the far-source tails are hub-coverage holes instead → add H51/G48 to the hub set per my sp-snmb drift note. If supply-waits persist after the sourcing guard, the residual is API-serialization latency — architect's lane.

title: captain-gate stray-sweep: squash can carry repo-index dirt into a merge (k0di follow-up)
description: Second half of the sp-k0di gate hole, evidenced by 6947af6: the merged squash carried a 9th file (issues.jsonl, 119 lines) the agent NEVER staged — swept from the repo checkout's local bd drift at squash time. k0di (e035c26) closed the EMPTY-merge case (dirty-worktree refusal, 0-commits-ahead refusal, net-empty rollback) but its smoke tests don't cover stray-sweep: a REAL branch + dirty repo index still lets strays ride into the merge commit. FIX: squashMergeClean must commit exactly the branch's tree (commit-tree branch^{tree} semantics with no repo-index contribution) or hard-verify the repo index is clean pre-squash; TEST: dirty repo-local file + real branch commits -> merged diff contains ONLY branch files. Benign so far (bd jsonl only; dolt is source of truth) but any stray could ride in.
notes:
REINFORCING EVIDENCE (events2): (1) rtk git status HIDES .jsonl churn — agents using rtk for pre-gate cleanliness checks cannot see stray issues.jsonl staging (use raw git status for gate hygiene); (2) a background beads auto-committer re-stages issues.jsonl into worktree indexes between add and commit — clean commits needed private GIT_INDEX_FILE + commit-tree plumbing. The gate-side exact-branch-tree fix remains the real cure.
session died
MERGED-VERIFIED bf1dcd3. Gate binary rebuilt from it — squash now commits exactly the branch tree; repo-index strays can no longer ride into merges. Tool-side complete (no daemon component). Awaiting sweep-guard's report for test detail; close after its confirmation.
close_reason: MERGED-VERIFIED bf1dcd3 (2 files, +221/-30) + gate binary rebuilt. Root cause refined: post-k0di squash already used commit-tree branch^{tree}, but (1) the beads PRE-COMMIT HOOK stages root issues.jsonl INTO agents' branch commits so the branch tree itself carried the stray (the 6947af6 vector), and (2) a staged root stray self-bricked the merge via assertNoForeignStaged. Fix: private-index squash pinning all beads-export paths to main's blob; foreign files still abort loudly. RED-first tests on both vectors; k0di's 3 guards intact. Tool-side complete — gate now commits exactly the branch's code, immune to hook churn, racing auto-committers, and repo dirt.

title: Contract coordinator: dedicated-fleet is ADDITIVE not exclusive + cargo-fit selection never deployed (distance-only)
description:
Two contract-coordinator selection defects found validating the engine (coordinator 2b47d086, 2026-07-09 ~13:37Z):

(1) DEDICATED-FLEET IS ADDITIVE, NOT EXCLUSIVE. Started with --dedicated-ships TORWIND-6,8,11,3 (all registered/reconciled correctly), but the coordinator STILL grabs idle NON-dedicated pool hulls by distance. Evidence: immediately after reconciling the 4-ship fleet it logged 'Selected TORWIND-B (distance: 0.00)' — B is not dedicated — and pulled B into a 73-unit LIQUID_NITROGEN contract, DISPLACING B's existing 43-EQUIPMENT cargo (captain was mid-liquidating it; cargo lost/dumped to make room). NEEDED: an EXCLUSIVE mode — when --dedicated-ships is set, use ONLY those ships, never borrow from the pool. (Today the frigate/heavy are safe only because they're pinned to OTHER l7h2 fleets; every other idle light is fair game, so a dedicated contract fleet isn't actually sealed.) Also: grabbing a hull with existing cargo and dumping it is destructive — a claim should refuse/park if the hull holds unrelated cargo.

(2) CARGO-FIT SELECTION NEVER DEPLOYED. Coordinator selects purely by DISTANCE ('Selected TORWIND-X (distance: N)'), not by cargo-fit. sp-snmb's cargo-fit + frigate-last-resort element (spec'd, marked done) is NOT in the running behavior. This is why the frigate multi-tripped small contracts before the captain pinned it out. Benign with all-light fleets, but frigate-last-resort can't work without it. Reconcile sp-snmb 'done' vs actual behavior.

ACCEPTANCE: (1) --dedicated-ships (or a --exclusive flag) → coordinator uses ONLY dedicated ships, never pool hulls; refuses to claim a hull holding unrelated cargo. (2) selection considers cargo-fit (right-size hull to contract quantity), frigate/small hulls used last-resort for small contracts only.
notes:
PLAN (2026-07-09), both P2 sequenced after bp6f P0: Defect 2 (cargo-fit/frigate-last-resort never deployed, distance-only) = l7h2 Phase 3 (cargo-fit selection) - already queued. Reconcile: sp-snmb 'done' covered dedication+standby+parametrization ONLY; cargo-fit was always deferred to l7h2 (already noted on sp-snmb). Defect 1 (dedicated-fleet ADDITIVE-not-exclusive + DUMPS unrelated cargo) = fold into the l7h2 ownership build: (a) EXCLUSIVE mode — when a fleet is dedicated, coordinator uses ONLY FindIdleShipsByFleet, NOT the additive combined pool generalShips+dedicatedIdleShips (run_fleet_coordinator.go:178); (b) claim-guard — refuse/park a claim on a hull holding UNRELATED cargo (never dump cargo to make room — destructive). Links to sp-l7h2 + epic sp-5w18.
session died

title: Payload-vs-OpenAPI validation for outbound SpaceTraders API requests (sp-423c check c)
description:
sp-423c ('Gate hardening: catch real-API-contract mismatches pre-deploy') specified three checks in priority order: (a) handler-registration completeness, (b) real API error-shape tests, (c) payload-vs-OpenAPI validation of outbound request payloads.

(a) and (b) shipped in sp-423c itself. (c) was explicitly deferred per team-lead's stated fallback: "If too big, file a follow-up bead and ship (a)+(b)."

Investigation confirmed: no OpenAPI/Swagger spec file exists anywhere in this repo (searched for *openapi*, *swagger*, and any YAML containing an `openapi:` key — zero hits). SpaceTraders does publish a public OpenAPI spec (https://spacetraders.stoplight.io / https://github.com/SpaceTradersAPI/api-docs) that could be vendored in.

Scope for this follow-up:
1. Vendor or fetch the official SpaceTraders OpenAPI spec into the repo (e.g. api/openapi.yaml), with a documented refresh process (it may drift from the live API over time).
2. Build a validation layer (likely in internal/adapters/api, at the client.go request-construction boundary) that validates outbound request payloads against the spec's schema before sending — at minimum in tests/CI, ideally as an optional runtime guard behind a debug flag.
3. Acceptance: a deliberately mis-keyed payload (wrong field name, wrong type, missing required field) fails a test/gate check BEFORE hitting the real API.

This closes the last of the three real-API-contract mismatch classes the gate-hardening epic targeted; (a) and (b) already catch unregistered handlers and known error shapes, but nothing today catches "we're sending the API a payload it will reject" until LIVE acceptance.
close_reason: MERGED-VERIFIED 3a3be70 (spec vendored v2.3.0 + kin-openapi test-time contract check over all 11 payload-bearing write methods via the real client's serialization; RED-proven against the exact sp-n0x7 mis-key class, field named in the failure). TEST-SIDE — effective on main immediately (every gate runs it); no daemon deploy or live acceptance needed (k0di/jgtw precedent). Refresh process documented in gobot/api/README.md. sp-423c check c COMPLETE — all three gate-hardening checks now live.

If conversion fails persistently, those hulls are invisible/unusable to the daemon = lost capacity/income. title: orbit→navigate CLI chain races on claim handoff (~1s apart) — requires manual retry
description: Back-to-back 'ship orbit' then 'ship navigate' on the same hull (~1s apart) still races on claim handoff: navigate's Start() calls createShipAssignments before the orbit container's synchronous releaseShipAssignments("completed") has landed, so AssignToContainer fails with 'ship X is already assigned to container orbit-X-...'. Distinct from sp-sfoe (that's the captain-reservation block variant). sp-9xc0 mitigated the worst case (a completed/failed/stopped owner's stale claim now self-clears via 'ship refresh' reconciliation), but that's a periodic/on-demand sweep, not a synchronous guarantee, so the sub-1s window between orbit's own release and navigate's claim attempt can still lose the race. sp-cr86 (merged c66c5e4) fixed the DOWNSTREAM symptom - a losing claim attempt now terminalizes its row (FAILED) immediately instead of zombie-ing at RUNNING forever - but does not close the race itself, so the operator/script still needs a manual retry (observed recovering cleanly ~60s later). FIX OPTIONS (from sp-cr86): (a) serialize claim handoff - CLI 'ship orbit' blocks/waits for the container to actually complete+release before returning, so a scripted 'orbit; navigate' chain can't outrun the release; (b) navigate's claim path (createShipAssignments) retries briefly (e.g. a few short backoff attempts over 1-2s) before surfacing a claim failure, absorbing exactly this window. Acceptance: back-to-back orbit+navigate issued ~1s apart on the same hull succeeds without manual retry.
notes:
MERGED-VERIFIED ff17989 (4 files +301/-52). Root cause: LEGACY claim path (orbit/navigate) returned untyped errors — transient handoff race indistinguishable from standing rejection. Fix: typed ShipAlreadyAssignedError + bounded ctx-cancellable retry (7 attempts, 200ms->3s, ~9s budget) ONLY for the transient class; captain-reservation + l7h2 dedication rejections fail fast attempt-1 (test-pinned, -race green). STAGED — rides the next restart with rqwm (P0 leads).
DEPLOYED pid 59048 (rode the rqwm restart). Captain acceptance: chain orbit->navigate ~1s apart, should just work.
close_reason: Captain WRITTEN ACCEPT (st-wisp-c87l): zero orbit→navigate claim races since the ff17989 deploy (typed transient retry absorbing the handoff window). The 4244 dock-settle race is tracked on its own bead.

title: goods factory crashes on 'no cargo space available for purchase' — input-buy against a non-empty hold, new crash class distinct from vsfn refuel-500
description: SHIP_PARTS factory (ff2078ee) crashed 02:44:04Z (UTC-3): 'parallel production failed: level 0 execution failed: no cargo space available for purchase'. The coordinator tried to BUY an input good but the assigned hull's hold was full/insufficient — cargo-accounting or unload-ordering bug (didn't unload prior output before buying next input, or mis-sized the buy vs free space). DISTINCT from sp-vsfn (refuel-500). Same terminal-crash-not-park class though — killed the whole factory, manual relaunch (cf4f7a1a). FIX: check free hold before issuing a buy; if full, unload/deliver first or right-size the buy quantity to available space; and park+resume rather than crash (shared with vsfn). ACCEPTANCE: factory with a partially-loaded hull buys only up to free space or unloads first; no terminal crash on a full hold.
notes:
ACTIVE BLEED (captain st-wisp-ev78): factory crashes on 'no cargo space for purchase' — new class = buy-against-full-hold (distinct from sp-vsfn's refuel-500). Captain ranks it right after sp-snmb (factories dying). QUEUED behind sp-npyr — both touch the goods_factory coordinator, so serialize to avoid a merge conflict. Build when sp-npyr lands.
session died

title: claim-failure exit path leaves container rows RUNNING forever — zombie heartbeat_lost spam (6 instances tonight)
description: Containers that die BEFORE acquiring their ship claim ('Failed to create ship assignments: already assigned...' / '...reserved by the captain') exit without terminalizing their row: status stays RUNNING, watchkeeper emits container.heartbeat_lost forever, captain stops them by hand. 6 instances tonight: navigate-TORWIND-15-23cce323, -16-1689b678, -17-810f237d, -18-91f86853 (orbit→navigate claim race), navigate-TORWIND-19-584f3f41 + orbit-TORWIND-19-b6e423df (reservation block, sp-sfoe). Related-but-distinct from closed sp-9xc0 (that fixed claim RELEASE on death for claim-HOLDING containers; this is the never-claimed early-exit path). Also root-cause upstream: the orbit→navigate CLI chain STILL races when issued back-to-back (~1s apart) — either serialize claim handoff or make navigate retry-claim briefly. Acceptance: claim-failure exit terminalizes the row (FAILED, no heartbeats); back-to-back orbit+navigate on the same hull succeeds without manual retry; zero RUNNING rows for dead processes after a restart sweep.
notes:
session died
CORRECTION: NOT hung — prior agent COMPLETED + merged it (c66c5e4) before its session died silently (no report). Verified by zombie-rows2: go build + full test + -race all green. Terminalizes the container row on claim-failure (FAILED + EventWorkflowFailed; also satisfies sp-9xc0's 'claim-failure should be workflow.failed' note); RecoverRunningContainers already sweeps RUNNING rows post-restart (3rd acceptance bullet, no gap). Deploy-pending (batch). Orbit->navigate <1s CLI race is a DISTINCT root cause -> filed sp-ku8e (P2).
close_reason: DEPLOYED (daemon restart) — sp-q1ca lane-payload logging (verify cross-system), sp-npyr dwell logging, sp-cr86 zombie-row terminalization, sp-mu6u factory full-hold crash fix. Per-bead notes have SHAs + specifics.

title: DECISION: buy 1-2 SHIP_HEAVY_FREIGHTER @ZC66-A14A (2.07M ea, speed 36, ~360 cargo) — the productivity-per-hull flagship
description: refutation: sought from trade-analyst (fire-and-forget; Admiral surfaced live per novel-type rule — awaiting Admiral word this wake). SNAPSHOT 04:2xZ: treasury ~10.6M post-wave-1 (4 probes bought C37 ~195k); fleet 24; era ends 07-12T13:00Z (~81h). FIND: ZC66-A14A sells SHIP_HEAVY_FREIGHTER 2,074,909 HIGH supply — FRAME_HEAVY_FREIGHTER (~360 cargo est) + ENGINE speed 36 (2.4x our haulers). Cheaper than home's REFINING_FREIGHTER (2.39M) with no dead-refinery premium. ECONOMICS: 19.2% of treasury (under the 25% line); payback bar ~27k/hr net over remaining era — worst-hauler territory; realistic value: (a) contract quantities in 1 trip instead of 3-5 (cycle-time ruling synergy), (b) multi-stop router circuits past per-market absorption (needs wlev), (c) feed-doctrine bulk delivery cross-gate, projected 200-400k/hr on lane #2 depth → payback 5-10h. RISKS: wlev not live yet (until then: big contracts + single-system circuits only); heavy frame fuel burn (fuel ~70, cheap); coordinator adoption of new class (verify claim on arrival); positioning ZC66→home needs ZC66 gate connections (F charted A40D — read pending) via PA3 2-jump worst case ~1h at speed 36. REFUTE ANGLES: does absorption math actually reward 360-hold before wlev lands? is 2 heavies better than 1 heavy + 3 light haulers (same capex)? does the contract mix (micro-contracts included) waste a heavy?
notes:
TRADE-ANALYST REFUTATION (consult sp-n9vb: heavy freighter 2.07M @ZC66) — 2026-07-09 ~05:15Z

**Recommendation. Buy ONE — cannot refute it — but with three binding conditions that ARE the refutation of the plan as written: (1) EARMARK it to bulk trade circuits at purchase, never the contract pool; (2) weight its loads to ZC66, not PA3/UQ16; (3) heavy #2 is NOT approved — it's gated on two measurements, and on capital efficiency 3 lights currently dominate it.**

**(c) answered first because it's terminal: the contract-line rationale is DEAD ON DATA.** All 380 era-2 contracts fit a light hauler: max 78 units, median 47, ZERO above 80 (161 ≤40u, 219 at 41-80u; total pool pay 27.57M, avg 72.5k). "Contract quantities in 1 trip instead of 3-5" describes zero real contracts. A heavy on contracts wastes ≥78% of hold on every job. Its speed 36 does help cycle time — but T1 (speed 36) already owns that niche and chained 69 cycles/day. Earmark at purchase (the d-95 lesson): bulk circuits primary; contract fallback ONLY when otherwise idle.

**(a) Does absorption reward 360 cargo pre-wlev? YES — but only as MULTI-GOOD loads into the two deep systems, and ZC66's fresh sweep is what makes it true:**
- ZC66-AX1B (19 goods): ADV_CIRCUITRY bid 6,503 VOL 100, CLOTHING 4,997 VOL 100, MACHINERY 2,912 v100, EQUIPMENT 3,151 v100, FOOD 2,001 v300. ZC66-A14A: ADV_CIRC 6,305 v100 (that's 200u/tour of circuitry absorption at ~6.4k in ONE system), MICRO 3,382 v100, PARTS 7,769/PLATING 7,233 (v30 — shallow, don't overweight). Aligned absorption per ZC66 tour ≈ 400-600u. GQ92 tour ≈ ~360u mixed (components 100-140 + medicine 80 + drugs 80 + clothing 158). PA3/UQ16 sinks (vol 12-40) CANNOT absorb a heavy — deploying it there wastes 60-80% of hold; that part of the plan is refuted.
- Pre-wlev is not a blocker for single-jump manual circuits (lane-class loops already run). The heavy's loop: home collection tour (D40 adv_circ 100-150u + K80 clothing + D39 parts/medicine + E42 machinery) → ZC66 distribute (AX1B+A14A) → return leg FIREARMS (A14A exports ABUNDANT ask 2,512 v100 → home J56 bid 4,385: +1.9k/u × 100) → home. Est ~2-2.5h/loop, ~900k gross/loop → **~350-450k/hr, payback 5-6h** against 81h remaining. Buy-side note: 100-150u adv_circ at D40 vol 20 = 5-8 tranches ≈ +10-15% ask ladder — still clears at ZC66 bids; cap per-visit lifts and let the factory feed regenerate.
- API bonus (real at our margin): a heavy loop ≈ 20k credits/request vs a light's ~9k — at the API wall, cargo-per-request is exactly the lever; the heavy is the API-efficient hull class.
- Buy AT A14A and load the FIRST hold with ZC66 exports (firearms + fillers) so positioning home is itself a ~+190k leg, not dead-heading.

**(b) 2 heavies vs 1 heavy + 3 lights: REFUTED as a now-decision; sequence it.** Heavy #2 (2.07M) needs a second proven deep destination and a measured loop rate; 3 lights (~0.75M) on contract chaining earn ~300-370k/ship-hr IF supply holds → ~0.9-1.1M/hr for a THIRD of the capex — on raw capital efficiency lights dominate heavy #2 today. But that IF is the still-unrun chain-test. Gate it: buy heavy #2 ONLY IF (i) heavy #1's first measured loop ≥300k/hr AND (ii) the chain-test starves (contract supply can't feed more lights). If chain-test feeds, buy lights instead. Combined 2-heavy capex now would be 39% of treasury — also foul of the guardrail spirit mid-expansion.

**Ceiling coda:** ZC66's depth raises my useful-fleet ceiling from "low-20s" — one swept system just added ~50% to known aligned absorption, exactly the +1-1.5M/hr-per-big-system scaling I rated. The 5-8M/hr era-end path firms up; wave-2 probes just got more valuable (flip condition from the probe consult is live: ZC66 is the first of the "2 GQ92-class finds").

**Confidence.** HIGH on (c) (full-population query, not a sample) and on ZC66-weighted deployment (fresh reads ≤4.5h). MEDIUM on loop $/hr (transit formula + 2-jump topology via PA3 unverified — captain has A40D gate read pending). Fuel non-issue (ZC66 fuel 31-62, half-price at AX1B).

**What would change my mind.**
- Heavy #1's first measured loop <200k/hr → the class underperforms here; freeze heavy #2 permanently, re-examine load design before blaming the hull.
- Chain-test shows supply feeds 3+ more chained lights ≥300k/ship-hr each → lights win ALL remaining hull capex; heavy stays a 1-off specialist.
- ZC66 ADV_CIRC bids <5k after two heavy loops (vol-100 crush faster than modeled) → halve adv_circ weighting, re-rate loop to ~250-300k/hr.
- A40D gate read shows ZC66 is 3+ jumps from home → loop time balloons; GQ92 becomes the heavy's primary and loop $/hr drops ~30%.

EXECUTED (04:4xZ): bought ONE — TORWIND-19 @2,074,909 (A14A). CORRECTION vs consult: cargo capacity is 225 (not ~360 est) — still 2.8x hauler hold; analyst's 350-450k/hr estimate re-checks at 225x1.7k ≈ ~380k/loop on the adv_circ leg alone, payback ~5-6h unchanged. RESERVED to captain (claim-protected), positioning: A14A → A40D gate → jump PA3 → jump home → D40 load point; ~1-1.5h at speed 36. Earmark honored: bulk-circuits-only (contracts stay on lights — 380-contract median 47u). Heavy #2 gated on chain-test + first-loop realized margin. Treasury 8.63M post-buy (was 10.7M; wave-1 probes + heavy = ~2.27M deployed tonight, all consulted).
YARD SWEEP UPDATE (05:0xZ): ZC66-C18D (yard 3/6) sells SHIP_COMMAND_FRIGATE 905k HIGH speed-36 — the fast 60-cargo class (our T1's type). Relevant as sp-snmb HARDWARE once its right-sizing software lands (2nd frigate = dedicated micro-contract clearer); no buy — snmb first, then measure whether contract chain is frigate-starved. 19 jumps home in ~9min (cooldown).
HEAVY-#2 MEASUREMENT PATH SIMPLIFIED (06:2xZ): NOT hand-flying a measured loop (toil, esp. on Opus). Instead: 19 back on trade-route (7a97bea4); once q1ca lane-payload logging lands, the heavy's realized per-leg margin appears in logs automatically — that IS the measurement gating heavy #2, no manual loop needed. Heavy #2 deferred as non-urgent regardless (treasury ~9.2M, fleet near useful-ceiling per architect, ~55h to era end). Note: 19 carried 19x stray AMMUNITION from an interrupted prior circuit — trade-route/next sell should clear it.
HEAVY TARGET LANE (analyst arb-board 13:5xZ): the deliberate cross-gate run for TORWIND-19 (pinned 'trade' fleet) = JP61↔GQ92 TWO-SIDED, ~500k/loop. Fly it once bp6f (money floor) + l7h2 (pin, DONE) let me target+fly safely. Also top INTRA-system arb (single-system, no gate): JP61 lab_instruments +3k/u ×60, NK36 fabrics — candidates for a light positioned in-system.
HEAVY RUN — FULL SPEC (analyst sp-1xgq): JP61⇄GQ92 two-sided. OUT: 180 CLOTHING(+911)≈164k + 120 FOOD(+773)≈93k + 60 LAB_INSTR→GQ92-A4(+2151)≈129k = ~386k. BACK: 80 BIOCOMPOSITES K79→X20X(+1451)≈116k + filler = ~120-140k. TOTAL ~500-525k gross/round-trip, ~200-250k/hr. Heavy needs vol≥100 lanes (these qualify). Fly once bp6f+l7h2 safe.
CAPTAIN DEFER (st-wisp-93mg): treasury 326k vs 2.07M price. Purchase gate: Loop A measured >=300k/hr AND treasury >=8.3M (25%-rule). Money-guard precondition already met; this is now purely a treasury/throughput gate.
close_reason: Captain DECIDED + EXECUTING (st-wisp-knla): heavy #4 purchase container running at ZC66-A14A — the 25%-rule gate evidently satisfied by current treasury. Decision bead complete.
