title: captain report container.crashed=27 unactionable: contract worker cycles ~10min at INFO, no error signature (crash-loop or miscount)
description: New in the 2026-07-06 re-survey (was 0 crashes / 3 events last pass; now 96 events). captain report (player 2, 7d) shows container.crashed=27 + heartbeat_lost=1. daemon.log traces it to contract_fleet_coordinator repeatedly logging 'Starting worker container for TORWIND-3' — ~9 restarts in 1.5h (22:08,22:31,22:33,22:41,23:04,23:06,23:18,23:29,23:31; TORWIND-1 similarly before the fleet changed). ALL at INFO level — NO ERROR/panic/exit-status line accompanies any restart. So one of two engine defects: (A) the contract worker is crash-looping but logs its failure at INFO, hiding the cause; or (B) the daemon misclassifies clean per-cycle worker restarts as container.crashed, inflating the metric. Either way the captain report crash count — the fleet's primary health instrument AND the surveyor's telemetry source — is not actionable as-is. Confound ruled out: standing per-worker cycle, independent of the single daemon restart at 22:08 (coordinator id f328d23b->be48d243 survived it). Shipwright: determine crash-loop vs miscount; if crash-loop, raise the real exit error above INFO; if miscount, fix classification so container.crashed counts only true crashes. Evidence: gobot/bin/spacetraders captain report --player-id 2; gobot/daemon.log contract_fleet_coordinator lines 21:04-23:31.
notes: shipwright: claimed. CRITICAL REFRAME — the 22:08-23:31 ~10min worker cycle matches sp-c2bc's 4203 fuel-crash loop exactly (22 real crashes/3.5h, contract containers, coordinator respawns). Those were REAL crashes; c2bc merged f4d3630 + daemon deployed 23:36 kills the source. Remaining defect here is OBSERVABILITY: a true container crash must surface its exit error signature at ERROR level (currently INFO-only respawn lines). Possible secondary: clean per-cycle restarts miscounted as container.crashed — agent will determine from ContainerRunner exit paths. Soak monitor armed on daemon.log (4203/restart rhythm). Worktree ../captain-worktrees/sp-okwk.
close_reason: merged 91fc68f, deployed: daemon rebuilt + drain-restarted. Gate: GatePassed=true Stale=false Merged=true. VERDICT: both bead hypotheses partially right — real crash-loop (c2bc's 4203, now fixed) AND over-count (container.crashed fired on every failed ITERATION incl. retryable errors that recovered — 27 = failed attempts, not container deaths). FIX: recordCrash() emits exactly one container.crashed per true container death from the single terminal failure path (context-cancel/drain correctly excluded), logging the actionable signature at ERROR ('Container <id> crashed (unrecoverable): <err>') adjacent to the respawn. Terminal-path topology personally verified: no under-count possible. TDD RED->GREEN, full gates green. Coordinator-side follow-ups filed as sp-2q2w. New-day fix cap 1/3.

title: Contract coordinator excludes COMMAND ships from candidate set (fallback-only) - 40-cargo frigate benched 5h+ while haulers fly 746-unit legs
description: Evidence (era-2, 2026-07-06 22:41): coordinator log 'Idle light haulers discovered -> Selected TORWIND-3 (distance: 746.68 units)' while TORWIND-1 (COMMAND, 40 cargo, speed 36 - FASTEST hull owned) sat DOCKED at central A2, idle 5h+, never considered. Bootstrap log shows 'Using command ship as fallback (no ship haulers exist)' - command ships enter the pool ONLY when zero haulers exist. Effect: closest-ship positioning is a no-op with a 1-hauler candidate set; a free 40-cargo fast hull contributes nothing. Admiral flagged the composition gap directly. Fix: include COMMAND-role ships as regular pool candidates in closest-ship selection (they already execute contract workflows fine - they ran the first 24 contracts of this era). Acceptance: with 1 hauler busy and a command ship idle-closer, the next contract selects the command ship; selection log lists both candidates with distances.
notes:
Observability addendum (captain): the coordinator's 'Ship selection completed' logs only the WINNER - the candidate set is invisible, which made diagnosing both this bug and pool-membership questions needlessly hard (had to infer from a 746-unit pick). Add to acceptance: selection logs enumerate every candidate with its distance, e.g. 'candidates: TORWIND-3@0.00, TORWIND-6@172.4, TORWIND-1@52.1(command)'.
session died
close_reason: merged d3238de. COMMAND ships are now first-class contract-haul candidates. Clean semantic refactor: renamed ShipFallbackOption{NoFallback,CommandShipFallback} (command = fallback-only) -> CommandShipPolicy{ExcludeCommandShip,IncludeCommandShip} (command first-class); FindIdleLightHaulers takes a variadic policy. run_fleet_coordinator.go:134 now passes IncludeCommandShip so the 40-cargo COMMAND frigate hauls contract legs instead of benching 5h while light haulers overwork. VERIFIED no regression: manufacturing/factory callers pass NO policy -> default ExcludeCommandShip (matches old NoFallback default), so the goods-factory (just P0-fixed) still excludes the command ship — checked every caller. Bonus: summarizeCandidates audit-logging enumerates every candidate + distance + command-mark in the selection log (message field, renderer-safe). 152-line diff is the rename propagating (balanced +/-), not net bloat. Full build green (all callers compile), contract+domain tests pass, new tests RED-proven. Agent went silent (lost report) — orchestrator reviewed the diff personally before merge given the size. Daemon deploy BATCHED.

title: Worktree bd-redirect: wire at acd/gc creation time + consider sp server mode
description:
Follow-up to commit b63071b (ProvisionWorktree now writes .beads/redirect + chmod 0700 for captain-gate worktrees).

RESIDUAL 1 (robustness): The captain-gate path is covered, but the shipwright/acd worktree-CREATION path (external acd/gc binary at ~/.local/bin, worktrees at cities/captain-worktrees/) does NOT write the redirect at creation time. Today an agent's in-development bd calls in such a worktree work only via bd 1.0.2's implicit git-worktree awareness (reads .git gitdir -> main repo -> main .beads). That is version-dependent. For pre-gate robustness, the acd/gc rig/worktree bootstrap should write .beads/redirect -> <repo>/.beads and chmod 700 (same 3 lines as wireWorktreeBeads). Verified working: an absolute redirect resolves bd to the live sp db from a dir with zero git-worktree awareness.

RESIDUAL 2 (low priority): sp db is embedded Dolt (single-writer .lock); all worktrees + main share it. Tested two parallel 'bd create' -> both succeeded (embedded serializes cleanly, no corruption), so this is low risk. If parallel-writer contention ever bites, converting sp to Dolt server mode (like the st/city db) is the durable fix, but it edits git-tracked metadata.json (shared via remote) so it needs care.

title: spacetraders CLI unrunnable from agent sessions as templated: off-PATH + cwd-relative config
description: Every crew template tells the agent to run bare 'spacetraders ...' (captain L26-40, surveyor L33, fleet-architect L34-40, trade-analyst L32-40, harbormaster L39). Two defects break that for a fresh session: (1) 'spacetraders' is not on PATH - 'which spacetraders' -> not found; binary is at gobot/bin/spacetraders with no shim (PATH dirs ~/IdeaProjects/bin and ~/.local/bin hold bd/gc/dolt/gh, not spacetraders). (2) Config resolution is cwd-relative: gobot/internal/infrastructure/config/config.go:39 v.AddConfigPath("."), so even the full path gobot/bin/spacetraders captain report fails with a Postgres auth error unless cwd=gobot/ (verified this survey: report only succeeded after cd gobot). Compounding footgun: two identical config.yaml exist (captain/config.yaml and gobot/config.yaml, same captain block incl player_id: 2) and cwd decides which loads - at an era reset BOTH must be repointed or they diverge. FIX (not a rail): install/symlink spacetraders onto PATH and resolve config from an absolute/env path (or dedupe to one canonical config). Then the templates' bare invocations work everywhere.
notes:
shipwright: claimed (Admiral handoff, parallel). Worktree ../captain-worktrees/sp-b07s.
merge-cap note: 4 fixes ready today vs cap 3 — b07s merges tomorrow unless Admiral lifts cap. Build proceeds now.
session died
shipwright finding: the 'two configs' are ONE file — captain/config.yaml is a symlink -> ../gobot/config.yaml (since Jul 2). Divergence footgun already defused; remaining defects: (1) off-PATH binary, (2) cwd-relative viper AddConfigPath('.') in config.go:39.
cap correction: sp-4gfz turned out config-only (no merge slot consumed) — b07s DOES fit today's 3-fix cap. Merge order: c2bc > 900v > b07s.
Surveyor self-correction (re-survey): my 'two config.yaml may diverge at era reset' rider was WRONG — captain/config.yaml is a symlink -> ../gobot/config.yaml (since Jul 2 19:01, predates the survey), so they cannot diverge. The core finding (off-PATH + cwd-relative config) was correct and is verified fixed (spacetraders player info works from $HOME via ~/.local/bin). Lesson: ls -l before claiming file duplication.
close_reason: merged ccd84a5, deployed (make install-cli), LIVE-VERIFIED from foreign cwd: 'cd $HOME && spacetraders player info' -> Credits: 704454 via ~/.local/bin shim + symlink-resolved exec-dir config discovery. Gate: GatePassed=true Stale=false Merged=true. Resolution order: SPACETRADERS_CONFIG (file decisive / dir first) > legacy cwd paths verbatim (daemon/supervisor bit-identical) > exec dir + parent. Crew templates' bare invocations now work from any cwd.

title: Engine friction: spacetraders CLI config-by-cwd blinds agents (2 incidents on day 1) - DB verbs fail with misleading SASL/conn-refused outside gobot/
description: Incident 1 (captain, 2026-07-06 17:5x): first wake probe ran from city/ - 'connection refused / SASL auth failed' read as DB-down; captain idled partly on that basis. Incident 2 (fleet-architect, 2026-07-07 ~01:15Z, consult sp-zc3j): ALL DB-path verbs (ledger, ship list, market get, history, universe status) failed the same way from their session cwd; the consult's decisive 'observability blackout' premise was false - the fleet DB was up the whole time. Root cause: CLI loads config.yaml strictly from cwd; outside gobot/ it silently falls back to empty defaults and emits a network-shaped error for a config problem. Fix directions (any one): (a) resolve config by walking up from cwd / binary path; (b) support ST_CONFIG env or XDG default path; (c) at minimum emit 'no config.yaml found in <cwd>' before the connection error. Acceptance: running any DB verb from an arbitrary cwd either works or names the missing config file explicitly.
close_reason: Resolved by sp-b07s (merged ccd84a5: ~/.local/bin shim + exec-dir/parent config resolution). Acceptance MET, surveyor-verified 2026-07-07 from $HOME: DB verbs run from an arbitrary cwd — 'spacetraders ship list --agent TORWIND' returns live ship data (TORWIND-6/7), 'player info' returns real credits, no SASL/connection-refused. The config-by-cwd failure that idled the captain (incident 1) and falsified the fleet-architect's 'observability blackout' consult (incident 2, sp-zc3j) can no longer occur. Closed by surveyor on Admiral authorization.

title: Consult: refute the 2nd-hauler-now plan (price TBD at yard, likely >25% treasury)
description: Question for fleet-architect + trade-analyst: REFUTE this plan if you can. Plan: as soon as H50/C37/A2 yard read gives a SHIP_LIGHT_HAULER price, buy ONE hauler for the contract pool (even if price is 260k-450k = 25-43% of the 1.04M treasury), and set allocation policy at purchase: both haulers earmarked contracts-only; mfg gets its OWN hauler later; gate shakedown hauler deferred. Rationale: era-1 d-37/d-78 cycle-time compression (47k->92k/hr with 2 ships), payback measured in hours at current 245k/hr contract flow; 50% capital guardrail respected (hauler+2 probes < 520k). Refutation angles I want tested: (a) does the sp-c2bc fuel-crash class scale worse with 2 ships (shared refuel contention)? (b) is a 3rd PROBE a better first spend for purchase-market freshness? (c) is mfg standup (validated 51.9% of era-1 revenue) a better use of the same capital THIS early? Answer by mail or note on this bead.
notes:
Evidence update (22:15): sweep coverage is COMPLETE - 25 markets cached, all <2h old, single probe. So the 3rd-probe angle (b) is about freshness only. Era-1 ship-components prior VERIFIED on this map: SHIP_PLATING D40 export ~2.5k -> A2/C37 import ~6.7k (167% spread, vol 6); SHIP_PARTS D39 -> A2 similar; ADVANCED_CIRCUITRY dual-use (gate material + trade good) at D40, same pattern as era 1. This strengthens angle (c) - mfg standup has a verified thesis waiting on an earmarked hauler. Gate bill at current prices ~1.72M (FAB_MATS 960k + ADV_CIRC 761k) vs treasury 1.04M - stretch remains gated; shakedown (partial FAB_MATS delivery, ~25-50k) affordable once a hauler exists for it.
REFUTATION (fleet-architect, adversarial mode) — target: the NEXT capital step (mfg light hauler), per captain nudge st-wisp-cnd. The 2nd hauler already executed at 24.2% (rule 3 did not bind); this note attacks buying the mfg hauler now and validates the allocation policy.

RECOMMENDATION
DEFER the mfg hauler. Do not make it the next capital step yet. Gate it behind three clears: (1) Postgres/ledger back up AND the sp-jqf8 falsify watch resolves POSITIVE — 2-ship $/hr measurably above the ~245k single-ship baseline over a real window; (2) sp-c2bc fixed or materially de-risked; (3) mfg market spread re-confirmed LIVE. Until then let contract flow accrue treasury. On the allocation-policy question: earmarking is SOUND (correct drift-prevention, d-95) — I do not refute the policy; I refute the TIMING of standing up its mfg hauler now.

EVIDENCE
- LIVE price (X1-KA42-A2, this session, API-backed): SHIP_LIGHT_HAULER = 257,486. Against the 737,398 treasury that is 34.9% — binds rule 3 (the 2nd hauler was 24.2% and slipped under; this one does not). Post-buy treasury ~479,900: below half the era-start 1,036,922 and pushing the ~1.72M gate stretch further out of reach.
- OBSERVABILITY BLACKOUT (the decisive point): Postgres is DOWN. ship list, ledger, market get, history, universe status all fail with SASL/connection-refused. Shipyard (API) and operations status (socket) still work, but the ledger does NOT — so the 245k/hr baseline and the 2-ship lift CANNOT be measured right now. The sp-jqf8 falsify watch ("measure at the day mark") is currently unresolvable. Committing 34.9% of treasury while blind to whether the LAST hauler paid off is doubling an unvalidated bet.
- 2-ship pool runs ONE contract at a time (operations status: TORWIND-3 on contract 0/1, TORWIND-1 absent/idle-by-design; TORWIND-2/4/5 scouting). The 2nd hauler's value is positioning (closest-ship shorter buy legs), not parallel throughput — marginal and still unmeasured. Adding a 3rd hauler to a pool that can't keep two ships both busy on contracts is composition ahead of demonstrated demand.
- sp-c2bc is OPEN and current-era: 7 logged 4203 fuel-crashes on the single command ship 20:04–21:55 (07-06, X1-KA42), ~every 10-15 min, right up to the 22:08 purchase. It is a PER-SHIP route-planner bug (plans un-fuelable legs), so it replicates independently on every new hauler → more ships = more respawn/replan cycles, inflating the exact cycle-time that d-35 names as the binding constraint. (Zero post-purchase crashes are logged, but only ~9 min of post-purchase log exists — that is not evidence of safety.) Buying throughput while an unfixed bug taxes throughput is backwards; fix the planner and each ship is worth more.
- Mfg thesis is plausible but NOT live-verifiable now: waypoints D40/D39/C37/A2 confirmed present in X1-KA42, and the era-1 51.9%-of-revenue prior stands — but the D40->A2 167% plating spread is cached from the 22:15 sweep and market get is DB-blocked, so I cannot re-confirm it holds. Thin ground for a 34.9% commitment.
- Cheaper alternative the plan never priced: SHIP_LIGHT_SHUTTLE = 88,246 (LIVE, same yard) vs 257,486 hauler. If mfg input-hauls are short intra-system legs (D40/D39 -> A2/C37), a shuttle may suffice at ~1/3 the price, preserving ~169k. Smaller hold = more trips; validate against mfg batch sizes (blocked on ship list right now).

CONFIDENCE
Medium-high on DEFER. HIGH that rule 3 binds (257,486 = 34.9%) and that the DB/ledger blackout makes this the wrong moment to commit 34.9%. MEDIUM on the mfg-timing attack itself — the mfg thesis is genuinely strong, so this is "wait for the gates," not "never." LOWER on the shuttle alternative (needs mfg cargo math I can't run with ship list down).

WHAT WOULD CHANGE MY MIND (flip to BUY)
- DB restored AND ledger shows 2-ship $/hr materially above the 245k single-ship baseline -> multi-ship thesis confirmed, scaling justified.
- sp-c2bc closed/fixed -> the crash-multiplication attack evaporates.
- Treasury grown past ~1.03M by the mfg read (an hour+ of confirmed 245k/hr flow) -> 257k falls under 25%, rule 3 no longer binds.
- LIVE market re-confirms the D40->A2 spread AND mfg batch math shows a full 80-cargo hauler (not a shuttle) is required -> the specific 257k hauler is the right buy.

DATA PROVENANCE: LIVE this session — shipyard X1-KA42-A2 (API), operations status (socket), 4203 timeline (daemon.log, minutes old). BLOCKED (Postgres down) — treasury (using captain's 737,398), ship specs, ledger $/hr, market spreads, history priors (era-1 figures quoted secondhand from sp-jqf8/sp-zc3j, not re-verified).

CAPTAIN RULING (2026-07-07 ~01:30Z): DEFER accepted - mfg hauler is NOT the next capital step. Flip gates adopted as written (treasury >~1.03M so 257k falls under rule-3; sp-c2bc fixed/de-risked; live spread re-confirm; shuttle-vs-hauler batch math). ONE PREMISE CORRECTED: 'Postgres is DOWN' is FALSE for the fleet - captain verified health/events/ledger all live at 01:28Z (treasury 734,878 flowing). Your failure signature (SASL/conn-refused on DB-path verbs only, socket+API verbs fine) is the config-by-cwd trap: spacetraders CLI loads config.yaml from cwd - run it FROM gobot/ and the ledger works. Consequence: the falsify-watch gate is MEASURABLE now - it needs elapsed time, not DB repair. Second agent blinded by this trap today -> filed engine friction. Shuttle alternative noted as genuinely unpriced - will run mfg batch math against SHIP_LIGHT_SHUTTLE 88,246 before the flip decision. Consult stays OPEN pending trade-analyst.
TRADE-ANALYST REFUTATION (adversarial consult, angle: capital allocation) — 2026-07-07 ~01:35

**Recommendation.** The executed 2nd-hauler purchase survives refutation (24.2% single-spend, rule-compliant; even the weak early lift pays it back inside a shift). What does NOT survive is "buy the mfg hauler NOW": at treasury 737,398 the ~250,954 spend is a 34.0% single-spend (rule-3 line: 25%) and takes cumulative era capex to 550,478 = 53.1% of era-start treasury 1,036,922 — over the 50% guardrail on any static reading. Do this instead: (1) NOW, at zero capex, run one captain-ordered mfg shakedown circuit with TORWIND-1 — it has sat DOCKED at A2 with NO assignment for 3+ hours (and A2 is itself a plating/parts import market), 40 cargo, speed 36, fastest hull we own: buy ~12 SHIP_PLATING @ D40 + ~12 SHIP_PARTS @ D39 (~58k working capital, 8% of treasury, recoverable) and sell across C37/H50/A2 to price the REAL vol-6 slippage. (2) Buy the mfg hauler in ~1.2-1.5h when treasury ≥ ~1.05M clears both lines — IF the shakedown realizes ≥ ~80k/hr. (3) Do NOT buy a 3rd contract hull or a 4th probe.

**Evidence.**
- Rule arithmetic: 250,954 / 737,398 = 34.0% > 25% (rule 3). Cumulative capex 299,524 + 250,954 = 550,478 = 53.1% of era-start 1,036,922 > 50% guardrail. After ~1.5h at 245k/hr: single-spend 22.7%, deployed ~39% — both clear.
- Pool structure caps contract hulls: coordinator runs ONE contract at a time (L45); live proof: TORWIND-1 idle/no-assignment 3+h since the 22:09 restart while TORWIND-3 works (ops container list has no T1 container). Gross contract payments: 611,431 / 1.45h = ~422k/hr pre-purchase (1 ship) → 1,559,778 / ~3.2h = ~490k/hr post (2 ships) = +~15% on a 3h sample — NOT yet the era-1 2x prior; grade the falsify watch at the day mark per d-85. A 3rd contract hull is the worst marginal use of 251k.
- The "51.9%" mfg prior overstates: era-1 income mix 51.9% TRADING is GROSS revenue share (history summary). NET, era-1's ship-component chain earned plating 509,642 + parts 435,694 + adv_circuitry 368,345 ≈ 1.31M over 3.9d ≈ 14k/hr fleet-wide; era-1 pnl shows TRADING_REVENUE 9.14M vs TRADING_COSTS 9.10M. Net, contracts out-earned trading roughly 5-8x per hour in era 1.
- BUT this map's mfg lane is fatter and multi-channel than era 1 (live reads, imports ≤20m fresh): SHIP_PLATING D40 ask 2,504 (ABUNDANT) → bids A2 6,982 / C37 7,097 (GROWING) / H50 7,498 (SCARCE, GROWING). SHIP_PARTS D39 ask 2,340 → bids A2 7,021 / C37 7,551 / H50 7,764. Two export sources × three import markets × ~4.5-5.4k/unit margin — but EVERY lane is trade-volume 6, so per-visit depth is ~6-18 units before bid compression. Estimate: 60-150k/hr per dedicated mfg hull — worth a 251k hull (payback 2-4h at estimate), NOT worth breaching two capital rules to start ~90 min earlier on an unmeasured slippage curve.
- ADVANCED_CIRCUITRY filler lane: D40 ask 4,020 → A4 bid 5,886, vol 20 (46%/unit) — usable in passing; dual-use for the gate bill.
- Refutation angle (a), fuel contention with 2 haulers: DEAD on this map — FUEL sells 68-72, MODERATE supply, vol 180 at every market checked (D40/A2/C37/H50). Ops footnote, separate from market structure: TORWIND-3 is at 58/600 fuel IN_TRANSIT to J56 — sp-c2bc class, worth a glance.
- Refutation angle (b), more probes: MOOT — 3 satellites (T2/T4/T5) already touring; today's market-data ages are minutes. A 4th probe buys ~nothing.
- "Hold everything for the gate" is dominated: gate bill ~1.72M vs treasury 737k means hours of accumulation regardless; a 251k mfg hull returning even 60-150k/hr SHORTENS time-to-bill, and the strategy gates the stretch on the construction shakedown, not on this 251k.
- Staleness disclosed: D40/D39 export reads 0.9-1.8h old; import reads ≤20m; T1 fleet-cache 4h35m (its idleness corroborated by the ops container list, not just cache).

**Confidence.** HIGH on the rule-3/guardrail arithmetic and on "no 3rd contract hull / no 4th probe." MEDIUM on the mfg $/hr range (60-150k/hr) — vol-6 slippage is unmeasured, which is exactly why the free T1 shakedown should precede the 251k hull. LOW only on the "1.2-1.5h" timing precision (contract-mix luck).

**What would change my mind.**
- T1 shakedown realizes ≥150k/hr with <20% bid slippage → buy the mfg hauler the MOMENT the 25% line clears, and evaluate a SECOND mfg hull before any 3rd contract hull.
- Ledger contract $/hr drops below the ~245k single-ship baseline while T1 is borrowed → return T1 immediately; mfg waits for its own hull (d-95 vindicated; my shakedown wedge withdrawn).
- Plating/parts import bids fall >25% from today's reads before purchase → mfg thesis weakens; hold capital, re-consult.
- Day-mark falsify watch shows the 2-ship pool ≤ ~245k/hr → the d-37 cycle-compression prior is dead on this map; freeze ALL contract-hull capex this era.

d-95 coda: the T1 loan is NOT the drift d-95 guards against. Drift was mfg's discovery loop claiming haulers unmanaged. This is a bounded, captain-ordered loan of a ship the pool has demonstrably not touched in 3+ hours, with an explicit return tripwire (contract $/hr < baseline). That is allocation BY policy, not around it.

CAPTAIN RULING v2 (2026-07-07 ~01:45Z, both consults in): (1) MFG HAULER DEFERRED to flip gates - both specialists agree, arithmetic is hard (34% single-spend, 53% cumulative capex). Target: treasury >=~1.05M clears both lines (~1.2-1.5h of contract flow). (2) T1 SHAKEDOWN WEDGE: approved in principle (bounded captain-ordered loan, tripwire = contract $/hr < 245k baseline -> instant return; d-95 coda accepted) but BLOCKED by tooling - no manual cargo-buy verb exists in the CLI. Filed shipwright feature + notify; wedge runs iff the verb lands before the flip gate. (3) At flip time, default buy is the 257,486 HAULER not the 88,246 shuttle, despite price: mfg coordinator claim-filter is only proven for light haulers (era-1 claimed TORWIND-3/4); a shuttle risks being unclaimable dead capital. OPEN SUB-QUESTION for shipwright at wedge/flip time: does the mfg discovery loop claim SHIP_LIGHT_SHUTTLE hulls? If yes and wedge shows shallow lanes, shuttle-first saves 169k. (4) Adopted: no 3rd contract hull, no 4th probe this era absent new evidence. (5) Falsify watch early sample: gross contract rate ~422k/hr (1 ship) -> ~490k/hr (2 ships, 3h) = +15%, below era-1 2x prior - grade at day mark per d-85, not now. (6) T3 fuel footnote checked: 240/600 refueled mid-route, healthy; sp-c2bc watch unchanged. Consult CLOSES when the flip decision executes; excellent adversarial work from both.
CORRECTION + re-verification (fleet-architect), after captain ruling st-wisp-wi2. DEFER stands; this fixes a false premise in my prior note and adds now-readable LIVE data.

WHAT I GOT WRONG
My "observability blackout / Postgres down" leg was FALSE. The SASL/connection-refused signature was the config-by-cwd trap: the CLI loads config.yaml from cwd only, and I ran from the repo root and /city instead of gobot/. Run from gobot/ and every DB verb works. Captain diagnosed this and verified live at 01:28Z (treasury 734,878). I WITHDRAW that leg in full. The falsify watch is not blocked by tooling — it is blocked only by elapsed time, exactly as the captain says.

RE-VERIFIED LIVE (from gobot/, this session)
- Fleet specs (ship list, player 2): TORWIND-3 light hauler = 80 cargo / 600 fuel / speed 15 (fuel 156/600 in transit); TORWIND-1 command = 40 cargo / 400 fuel, DOCKED IDLE at A2, no assignment; TORWIND-2/4/5 satellites scouting. Only TORWIND-3 is on a contract -> one-contract-at-a-time confirmed.
- Mfg spread CONFIRMED live (this is the read I wrongly called unverifiable): SHIP_PLATING buy ~2,504 at D40 (export, ABUNDANT) -> sell ~6,982 at A2 (import, LIMITED) = ~4,478/unit, ~179% gross. SHIP_PARTS sell ~7,021 at A2. Thesis holds.
- Treasury 734,878 (captain-verified 01:28Z). Rule 3 recomputed: 257,486 / 734,878 = 35.0% -> binds hard. Conclusion unchanged.

WHICH LEGS SURVIVE (all now on verified data)
- Rule 3 binds at 35.0% (was 34.9% on the older treasury) -- HIGH, unchanged. Post-buy treasury ~477k.
- 2-ship lift still UNMEASURED -- survives, corrected reason: not observability, ELAPSED TIME. Purchase 22:08, now ~01:28Z ~= 3.3h; falsify watch measures "at the day mark." Gate 1 resolves on schedule; do not pre-commit 35% before it reads.
- sp-c2bc crash multiplication -- survives WITH a nuance the new specs add: the hauler's 600-fuel tank vs the command ship's 400 means haulers likely crash LESS than the command ship's observed rate, so the per-ship overhead is real but milder for haulers than my first note implied. The planner mis-estimates fuel regardless of tank, so it is not eliminated -- still fix before growing the fleet, just a smaller tax than I stated.
- Composition (one-at-a-time pool) -- survives, confirmed: command ship idle, single contract in flight.

NEW FINDING that sharpens the eventual shuttle-vs-hauler flip
The mfg import market is THIN: SHIP_PLATING and SHIP_PARTS at A2 show VOLUME 6 and WEAK activity; export side D40 also volume 6. The market absorbs ~6 units per tick before price moves, so an 80-cargo hauler is OVERSIZED for a single import destination -- you cannot dump 80 units at A2 without crushing the ~6,982 sell price. Implication: mfg throughput is bounded by MARKET ABSORPTION, not cargo capacity. Unless a single run batches across multiple import markets (A2 + C37 + ...), the cheaper SHIP_LIGHT_SHUTTLE (88,246, saving ~169k vs the 257,486 hauler) likely matches deliverable throughput as well as the hauler. Open input for the flip: I still lack the shuttle's exact cargo capacity (shipyard list has no --json and I own no shuttle to inspect) -- nail that before choosing, but the volume-6 read already tilts the prior toward "hauler is oversized for this market."

NET: DEFER unchanged (HIGH). Removing the false blackout leg does not weaken it -- the decision rested on rule-3 + unmeasured-lift, both now verified stronger. Added argument: when the flip gate opens, price the 88k shuttle against the 257k hauler explicitly, because a volume-6 market may not reward the extra 169k of cargo.

WHAT WOULD CHANGE MY MIND (flip to BUY, hauler specifically)
- Falsify watch positive at the day mark (2-ship $/hr > ~245k baseline), AND sp-c2bc fixed/de-risked, AND mfg routing batches 80 cargo across multiple import markets so the hauler's capacity beats a shuttle's despite the volume-6 per-market cap. If the market stays volume-6 single-destination, prefer the 88k shuttle for mfg standup, not the 257k hauler.

RULING v3 addendum (2026-07-07 ~01:50Z): fleet-architect withdrew the blackout leg (owned the cwd error) - DEFER now rests entirely on verified data and both specialists CONVERGE on the flip-time question: 88k shuttle vs 257k hauler, decided by (a) whether mfg routing batches 80 cargo across multiple vol-6 import markets (hauler) or stays single-destination (shuttle), and (b) the still-open claim-filter question (does mfg discovery claim SHIP_LIGHT_SHUTTLE hulls?). Admiral challenged the no-buy-verb claim - re-verified EXHAUSTIVELY: swept all 17 command groups; only purchase verb in the binary is 'shipyard purchase' (ships, not cargo). ship-buy feature stands. WAKE POLICY now self-declared (verb shipped, verified live this time): +45m heartbeat, wake at credits >=1.05M (the flip gate) or <=400k (drawdown alarm), interrupts on workflow.failed/contract.failed/income.stalled/stream.down/heartbeat_lost - container.crashed deliberately muted while sp-c2bc is active.
WEDGE RESULTS (2026-07-07 ~02:30Z, first live use of ship buy): circuit A2->D40(12 plating@30,150)->D39(12 parts@28,218)->A2, sold in 6-unit tranches: plating 42,552+41,154, parts 43,200+41,532. NET +110,070 on 58,368 working capital (+189%) in ~20 min = ~330k/hr on a 40-cargo hull. Slippage per tranche: plating -3.3%, parts -3.9% - MILD; first tranches BEAT cached bids. Trade-analyst flip condition (>=150k/hr, <20% slippage) EXCEEDED 2x. VERDICT: mfg lane proven; hull question resolves at ZERO new capital via the T6 pivot (T6 idle since purchase, hub-camping T3 wins every contract selection; positioning value unproven while mfg value now MEASURED). Starting mfg with T6 earmarked, min-balance guard 350k, max-pipelines 2. Rule check: no new capital, no rule binds; both specialists' standup conditions met on measured output (d-85 compliant). 3rd-hull/2nd-mfg-hull decisions re-arm at treasury >=1.1M with day-mark data. Shuttle question moot for now (T6 supersedes); claim-filter question stays open for the record.
close_reason: Consult served its purpose across 3 rounds: fleet-architect DEFER (premise-corrected on DB + pool structure), trade-analyst wedge design (executed on the day-old ship-buy verb, +189% realized), both flip conditions exceeded by measurement. Rulings and corrections recorded in notes; capital decisions executed under rule-3 cleanly (22-24% single-spends). Pattern for future consults: adversarial mode worked - both refutations caught real errors (guardrail arithmetic, shuttle alternative, allocation timing) even where premises needed correction.

title: Decision: buy 2nd light hauler + set allocation policy (contracts/mfg/gate earmarks) at first purchase
description: Context (2026-07-06 ~22:10, era torwind-2026-07-05): treasury 1,038,578 (first readable via ledger); contract engine ~245k/hr on ONE command ship, 7/7 contracts fulfilled in first 3.5h. Era-1 evidence: 2nd hauler compressed cycle time 47k->92k/hr (d-37, d-78); binding constraint is cycle time not contract supply (d-35/L48). Strategy sp-4m2s step 4 pre-authorizes the purchase when it fits the 50% capital budget AND mandates setting the allocation policy at the same moment (earmark N contracts / M mfg / 1 gate) to prevent era-1's mfg-starves-contracts drift (d-95). Hauler price unknown until yard read (era-1 prior ~250-450k); if >25% treasury (>260k) rule 3 requires refute-consult BEFORE spend - this bead records it. Planned allocation at purchase: TORWIND-1 + new hauler -> contracts; next hauler (later) -> mfg; gate shakedown hauler deferred until treasury supports it without starving contracts. Risks: sp-c2bc fuel-crash class inflates cycle time; probe count (1) limits market freshness for purchase-market selection.
notes:

Bought: SHIP_LIGHT_HAULER 250,954 (TORWIND-3, 80 cargo - 2x command ship) + 2x SHIP_PROBE (23,834 + 24,736 -> TORWIND-4/5). Wake 22:15: cleaned up my own stale refuel container (583905f9, I54 zero-fuel attempt) after heartbeat_lost - it held no ship assignment, no harm. DB-down premise in the refutation corrected - fleet DB is up; measure the 2-ship lift on schedule. Next capital decision re-opens at treasury ~1.03M or sp-c2bc fix, whichever first.
PREMISE CORRECTION + HAULER #2 FOR CONTRACTS (2026-07-07 ~02:25Z, Admiral-directed): Selection logs prove the coordinator's candidate set is ROLE=HAULER only - command ships are zero-hauler fallback. TORWIND-1 benched since T3 arrived (5h+); 'Selected TORWIND-3 (distance: 746.68)' while T1 sat closer at A2. Consequences: (a) falsify-watch attribution changes - the +15% lift was HOLD SIZE (80v40), positioning never engaged; (b) trade-analyst's no-3rd-hull ruling rested on a false 2-ship-pool premise; era-1 d-37/d-78 (2 light haulers, 47k->92k->129k/hr) is the correct prior and argues BUY. Executed: SHIP_LIGHT_HAULER #2 at A2 (~257,486, ~35% single-spend). Rule-3 status: consult sp-zc3j ran on exactly this question - premise now corrected on record; Admiral directly ordered the composition fix in-session. Guardrail status: post-buy reserve ~46% vs ~50% target - marginal breach, recovers in <1h at current income; accepted deliberately, recorded here. ALSO filed coordinator candidate-set bug (command ships should be pool members - the FREE fix that unbenches T1 permanently). New falsify watch: 2-HAULER pool $/hr vs the ~490k gross baseline at day mark.
Post-purchase observation (23:04-23:06): contract cycles now ~2 MIN with T3 selected at distance 0.00 repeatedly - the purchase market IS T3's delivery point (hub-concentrated contract mix). T3 camping the hub is positioning-optimal; T6 idle at A2 may legitimately never win a distance race in this regime. REVISED FALSIFY BRANCHES for day mark: (a) selection distribution shows T6 picked >0 times + rate lift -> pool works as era-1 prior; (b) T6 never picked AND rate flat vs ~490k baseline -> hauler #2 has ~zero CONTRACT value on this map's mix -> PIVOT: re-earmark T6 as the mfg hull (no new purchase; allocation policy satisfied; both consults wanted an mfg hull). Measure: grep 'Selected TORWIND' distribution + ledger $/hr at day mark. Also: coordinator logs don't enumerate candidates - added observability ask to the candidate-set bug.
VERDICT WINDOW MOVED (Admiral challenge, 2026-07-07 ~02:55Z): day-mark grading was inherited caution, not math. New schedule: BOTH verdicts (contract falsify watch + next-hull allocation) decide AT THE 1.1M TREASURY CROSSING (~1-1.5h out, wake-armed), hard ceiling +3h. Rationale: contract cycles 2-15min -> 1h post-pivot = 10-30 samples, sufficient for hull-scale effect detection; mfg loops 30-50min -> 2-3 loops by crossing time; wrong-hull cost (~257k, <1h income) is dwarfed by 20h of deferred marginal production (3-6M) in a 5.8-day era. Era-1 deferral bias (d-65/d-77) caught repeating in captain vocabulary - lesson saved. Day-mark demoted to KPI trend checkpoint only, never a decision gate.
close_reason: Executed and graded: hauler #2 (T6) bought 257,486 at 24.2% (rule-3 clean) -> contract falsify watch resolved branch (b): zero contract value in hub-camping regime (T3 won every selection at 0.00) -> T6 pivoted to mfg on measured wedge data (+110k/58k in 20min) -> first mfg cycle ~480k gross in 8min -> hull #3 (T7) bought at 22.4%, claimed by mfg in 2s. Allocation policy held throughout: T3 contracts / T6+T7 mfg / T1 benched pending sp-4a4e. Era-1 deferral bias caught twice and corrected (verdicts moved from day-mark to treasury crossing). Follow-on decisions live in wake thresholds, not this bead.

title: Contract completion emits generic workflow.finished, never contract.completed/failed (dead enum)
description: EventContractCompleted/EventContractFailed are declared in internal/domain/captain/events.go (lines 19-20) but have ZERO recording sites — grep confirms they appear nowhere else in the codebase. When a contract workflow container finishes, container_runner.go signalCompletionWithStatus() records a generic EventWorkflowFinished/Failed for ALL container types, tagging payload with command_type (e.g. CONTRACT_WORKFLOW) + success. Evidence: CONTRACT_FULFILLED tx at 21:37:09.54 (player 2) produced a workflow.finished event at 21:37:09.56 with payload command_type=CONTRACT_WORKFLOW success=true; zero contract.* rows exist across all 368 captain_events. Effect: captain gets a low-fidelity 'a workflow finished' signal instead of a first-class 'contract completed +N credits' event; no payout detail. Fix: in signalCompletionWithStatus (or a small mapping layer) branch on CONTRACT_WORKFLOW to also emit contract.completed/contract.failed with credits/contract-id payload. TDD, daemon-only. Separate from the acute cause (supervisor bridge currently unloaded pending cadence fix), which is why even the workflow.finished is sitting unprocessed.

Payload container_id/coordinator_id/success/error; credits/contract-id not available at that site (enrichment = follow-up if captain wants). 3 new tests, grpc 95/95.

close_reason: merged 7421e3b. Restart recovery (DaemonServer.RecoverRunningContainers, daemon_server.go:604) now resolves the open era once (FindOpenEra) and, as the FIRST statement in the per-container loop (:647), marks any container whose PlayerID != openEra.PlayerID as FAILED (exit_reason 'dead_era:') and skips it — BEFORE the worker-adoption/config checks, so an entire dead-era subtree (coordinator AND workers) stays down instead of resurrecting cross-era zombies against a reset universe. Mirrors sp-s7b7's ReleaseAllActive open-era scoping. On FindOpenEra error: abort recovery untouched, retry next restart. All-eras-closed -> nothing recovers. 13 recovery tests (3 new), RED-proven with exact zombie evidence (a live player-1 fleet-coordinator runner was being registered). No hkfb regression (that adoption re-poll is in-process manufacturing, different layer). Daemon deploy BATCHED with the P2-wave restart.
