title: 42703 column-level schema-drift gate: GORM model fields vs migration columns
description: sp-tvcm follow-up (fix agent flag, 2026-07-07): tvcm built the 23514 ENUM CHECK-constraint drift gate (merged 55025f4). The bead's literal headline also named a 42703 COLUMN-level gate — a genuinely larger, different check: reflect GORM struct fields -> column names, compare against columns created/altered by migrations (CREATE TABLE + ALTER ADD COLUMN across all up.sql), fail when a model persists a column no migration defines (undefined_column at write). Same defect CLASS as sp-8ind but the column axis, not the enum axis. Also worth folding: trade_type (mig 019) + pipeline_type (mig 020) are enum-valued columns with NO CHECK constraint — either add constraints (then tvcm's gate covers them) or accept them as unconstrained. Not built by tvcm; needs GORM reflection over the model registry.
notes: RECURRENCE (sp-cu42, 2026-07-10): Lane B's OperationTypeWarehouse passed the gate + all FK-ON tests but DOA'd live on migration 023's valid_operation_type CHECK — the existing schema_enum_drift_test didn't catch the Go→migration direction, and sqlite AutoMigrate carries no CHECKs so tests can't see it. cu42 hardens the storage_operations drift test bidirectionally; THIS bead's scope (the general 42703/23514 column+constraint drift gate across ALL tables) just earned its priority — consider P2.
close_reason: Merged 2768c9c (verified 3 files +419) + migration 036 APPLIED live (idempotent as predicted: min_supply already AutoMigrate-born, NOTICE+skip; both columns verified in information_schema). The 42703 column half now gates: every migration-CREATE'd table's model columns must exist in the effective migration history (12 tables audited: 1 real drift found+fixed, teeth test seeded-drift-proven). Scope ruling accepted: checkable=has-CREATE (AutoMigrate is prod's additive baseline — 'mentioned' would false-positive every base table). Enum/23514 half = the existing cu42-hardened gate, untouched. Test-infra + no-op migration: closing as orchestrator (55aa precedent). Follow-ups: sp-d8dq (trade_type/pipeline_type CHECK decision, agent-filed), orphan-table bead next.

title: Config-driven crash-loop tuning: CaptainConfig fields for window/threshold
description: Follow-up to sp-no9i. detectCrashLoops (gobot/internal/captain/detectors.go) is wired live from in-package defaults (defaultCrashLoopWindow=30m, defaultCrashLoopThreshold=3) because CaptainConfig was out of sp-no9i's scope. Add CrashLoopWindowMinutes + CrashLoopThreshold to config.CaptainConfig (internal/infrastructure/config/captain.go) + defaults.go, and populate dcfg in supervisor.go from them (mirroring StreamDownMinutes/ExpectedStreams) so operators can tune without a rebuild. title: Deploy/env hardening: plist generated from repo template (BD_REAL can't be dropped) + build-stamp startup banners with deploy assertion
description: Admiral-approved 2026-07-07. Two deploy/env hardenings that retire recurring hazard classes: (a) BD_REAL plist fragility — ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.spacetraders.captain.plist must carry BD_REAL=<real bd path> or ALL watchkeeper gc/bd calls exit 127 (the historical whole-fleet outage; memory captain-supervisor-plist-*). Machine-local + hand-maintained = will be dropped on the next regen. FIX: generate the plist from an in-repo template via a make target (make install-launchd or similar) that always includes BD_REAL + correct paths; hand-editing stops being load-bearing. (b) stale-binary class (L42, and the near-miss in the 2026-07-07 survey): a merged fix is invisible if the running binary predates it. FIX: embed a build stamp (git sha + build time) in watchkeeper + daemon startup banners, and make deploy targets assert the freshly-built stamp appears in the live process log after restart. TEST: plist regen from clean checkout carries BD_REAL; make deploy fails loudly if the old binary is still running. RETIRES captain-supervisor-plist-* (fact already merging into the watchkeeper memory via curation) and L42's stale-binary clause on landing.
notes: session died
close_reason: merged 87bac4b. TWO deliverables: (1) COMMITTED launchd templates (deploy/launchd/*.plist.template + render-launchd.sh, paths parameterized) — durable source of truth so a plist regen can NEVER again drop BD_REAL/watchkeeper/ExitTimeOut (the sk68 + t0f6 hand-fixes are now permanent). Agent found the 4 previously-committed plists were ALL STALE (wrong ~/projects path, bin/captain not watchkeeper, no BD_REAL) and replaced them. (2) BUILD-STAMP: -ldflags -X embeds commit+buildtime; watchkeeper/daemon print it at startup, 'spacetraders version'/--version expose it — VERIFIED live (version prints 87bac4b + timestamp). Makefile LDFLAGS applied to all 3 build targets with safe 'unknown'/'dev' defaults; my normal build-daemon/build-cli/build-watchkeeper now stamp automatically. DEPLOY POSTURE: build stamps take effect on each binary's NEXT rebuild (daemon at hs2j restart via 'make assert-live-stamp' bonus verification; watchkeeper next kickstart; CLI done). install-launchd/deploy targets are AVAILABLE but NOT auto-run — re-bootstrapping live units mid-era-mission is disruptive and unnecessary (live units already work); left for a deliberate maintenance window. FLAG on that future apply: install-launchd would ADD ExitTimeOut=45 to the live captain unit (currently has none — harmless drain ceiling, but a conscious change). 5 buildinfo tests TDD RED-proven, gates green. No memory retired (infra).

title: operations status/stop blind to running manufacturing coordinators — silently report nothing while it runs
description: Admiral-approved 2026-07-07. L53 (generalized s83): 'operations status' and 'operations stop --manufacturing' do NOT track a manufacturing operation even when normally launched — status prints 'No active manufacturing operations' while the coordinator RUNS under 'Other Containers', and stop returns 'No matching operations'. Folklore workaround: always observe/halt via 'container logs'/'container stop <parallel_manufacturing-… id>'. FIX: make the operations verbs actually track manufacturing coordinators (match however they are registered), or make them error loudly pointing at container verbs — a verb that silently reports 'nothing running' while the thing runs is an observability lie. RETIRES L53's second half on landing.
notes: session died
close_reason: merged 42dd937, DEPLOYED (build-cli), LIVE-VERIFIED: 'operations status' now shows parallel_manufacturing coordinator + mfg-task worker under Manufacturing->Coordinators/Workers (was 'Other Containers'). ROOT CAUSE: single case-mismatch — daemon persists UPPERCASE domain types (MANUFACTURING_COORDINATOR), CLI matched lowercase substrings, so strings.Contains never hit; also silently broke GAS + 'operations stop --manufacturing'. FIX: classify against container.ContainerType* constants via strings.EqualFold, shared predicates for status+stop (can't disagree again), PARALLEL_MANUFACTURING matched. Construction surfaces here too (runs through the mfg coordinator, no own container). 4 tests, 5 subcases, all RED-proven. Retires lessons.md L53 second-half ('operations --manufacturing don't track, use container stop') + strategy.md d-65 stop-path guard — flag sp-4ek3.

title: Detector tuning: exempt in-transit ships from heartbeat_lost; add crash-LOOP signal distinct from single crashes
description: TIER-3 (watchkeeper detectors), Admiral PRE-APPROVED 2026-07-07. Two detector refinements, same file as sp-1hak (coordinate — land after/with it): (a) L29: heartbeat_lost fires on slow solar scouts whose transit leg exceeds the window — exempt ships whose position is ADVANCING (or IN_TRANSIT) from stale-heartbeat, or use per-role windows; a frozen position is the real signal. (b) L40: a single crash is self-healing (auto-restart+resume) — the actionable signal is a crash-LOOP (restart count climbing with no progress). sp-okwk made container.crashed count true deaths; ADD a distinct crash-loop detection (e.g. container.crashloop interrupt when N true deaths of the same container within window W, or restart_count climbing without progress) so the captain is interrupted for loops, not singles. TEST: transit-legged scout emits nothing; 3 rapid true deaths of one container emit exactly one crashloop interrupt. RETIRES L29 and L40's escalation clause on landing.
notes: session died
close_reason: merged e6682b7, DEPLOYED: watchkeeper rebuilt pid 92240. Two tunings (both TDD RED-proven, sp-1hak cooldown + sp-t0f6 rename respected): (a) stale-heartbeat EXEMPTS in-transit ships — a frozen position is the real death signal, a transit-legged scout no longer false-fires (reuses detectIdleShips' quoted-symbol join); (b) crash-LOOP vs single-crash — new container.crashloop type, detectCrashLoops emits ONE interrupt when >=3 true deaths (per sp-okwk's now-truthful count) of one container in 30m, deduped via HasSince; container.crashed DEMOTED from the DEFAULT interrupt set (single crash defers, self-heals). CRITICAL LIVE-BEHAVIOR NOTE: the captain's DECLARED interrupt override still lists container.crashed and REPLACES the default set (sk68 IsInterrupt) — so its live behavior is UNCHANGED by the default-set demotion; to ADOPT crash-loop-only waking it must re-declare with container.crashloop. Mailing captain. Full gates green. Retires lessons L29 (heartbeat/frozen-position) + L40 escalation clause — flagged for sp-4ek3. Config-tunable window/threshold filed sp-d3xi P3.

Findings preserved: TaskStatusCancelled correctly excluded (never persisted), trade_type/pipeline_type have no DB CHECK at all.

title: Naming collision: supervisor daemon is named 'captain' (same as the agent it wakes) + 'watchkeeper' as a third alias
description: PURE RENAME (no behavior change) to end a naming collision that has real operator cost. The supervisor daemon built from gobot/cmd/captain -> gobot/bin/captain (Go package captainsup) is NOT the captain agent — it is the background process that watches the fleet DB every ~30s and wakes/pokes the captain AI session. So 'captain' names TWO distinct things: (1) the supervisor daemon (gobot/bin/captain, pid observed 20880), (2) the operator AGENT (a gc/Claude session it wakes). Worse, the crew templates AND the surveyor charter call this same daemon 'the watchkeeper' — a THIRD name for the same thing. Three names, two things. EVIDENCE OF COST: this collision made the Admiral lose the thread during a live P1 auto-restart discussion 2026-07-07 ('what the fuck is the watchkeeper'); it also makes logs, greps, and onboarding ambiguous (captain-supervisor.log lines are prefixed 'captain:' for the DAEMON, not the agent). FIX: pick ONE unambiguous name for the supervisor daemon and use it everywhere, reserving 'captain' exclusively for the agent. Recommend 'watchkeeper' (already the conceptual name in the templates) or 'supervisor'. Rename the binary (gobot/bin/*), cmd dir (gobot/cmd/captain), Go package (captainsup), log prefixes, config comments, AND the crew templates + surveyor charter that say 'watchkeeper' — so code and prose converge on one term. CAVEAT: the running daemon logs to captain-supervisor.log with 'captain:' prefixes; the rename must update any dashboards/monitors/log-greps that key on those strings (e.g. captain-dashboard.py, supervisor log tails). TIER-3: touches the watchkeeper AND templates -> human sign-off on scope before it moves. Admiral-requested 2026-07-07.

title: Watchkeeper/gate hardening batch: backoff, double-log, liveness, silent crash-drop, latent edges
description: From a full read-only code review of the captain engine (surveyor-triaged; shipwright verifies each before fixing). Six distinct TIER-3 (watchkeeper/gate) items: (1) Delivery backoff can't back off a dead channel while any interrupt is queued — wake.go:187-192 caps retry at 2xpoll when hasUnmailedInterrupt, so a total gc/bd outage with a standing interrupt retries every ~60s forever; the exponential/15-min ceiling never engages. Fix: full backoff even for interrupts when preflight/SessionAlive shows the channel fully down. (2) Every failed wake logs twice — supervisor.go:224-226 calls noteDeliveryFailure ('WAKE DELIVERY FAILING') AND returns the same err ('tick error'), diluting the grep signal. Fix: return nil once noted. (3) SessionAlive over-eager — gc.go:84 treats any non active/running state as dead -> double-spawn risk on transient startup. (4) nil captainRecorder silently drops crashes — captain_recorder.go:28 returns early when rec==nil with no startup warning; a mis-wired recorder makes container.crashed (and all synthetic events) vanish invisibly (same class as sp-vvnw). (5) Spurious credits.threshold wake on credit-source switch — supervisor.go:159-160 prevCredits baseline; refreshCredits (agentcredits.go:64) can flip ledger<->live between polls, firing a false interrupt wake on the first live read after fallback/restart. (6) Gate hardcodes literal 'main' — worktree.go:110-113 (merge-base --is-ancestor main); any repo whose default branch isn't 'main' has every merge refused as stale (latent). Citations from code review, surveyor spot-checked. TIER-3 — human sign-off.
notes: LIVENESS FALSE-POSITIVE flapping observed 2026-07-09: (1) watchkeeper logged 'STANDING SESSION DOWN captain' while the captain process (claude-fable-5, pid 93157) was ALIVE with tmux up (derrick socket) — nearly triggered an unnecessary manual relaunch. (2) bd flipped sp-s8qq AND sp-snmb to OPEN with false 'session died' notes (x2 each) while those agents were alive + completing. The liveness/orphan-reconciliation detector emits false-DOWN/false-died. RELIABLE liveness = process(ps)/file-mtime/git checks, NOT the watchkeeper 'session down' or bd 'session died' signals. Fix the detector's false-positive (it caused agent-death misdiagnosis + a near-miss captain relaunch this session).

title: Surveyor cadence stamped before mail delivery: one transient error skips a whole review cycle
description: nudgeSurveyorOnCadence (gobot/internal/captain/surveyor_nudge.go:32-37) sets s.lastSurveyorNudge = now (l.33) and persists it BEFORE calling SendMail (l.34); on mail error it just returns (l.35-36) with no log. A single transient mail failure advances the cadence clock as if the survey ran — silently skipping a full meta_review_days cycle (surveyor simply isn't woken until the next interval). Contrast the wake path, which stamps only after success. Surveyor-verified against source 2026-07-07 (my own wake path). FIX: stamp lastSurveyorNudge only after SendMail succeeds; log the failure. TIER-3 (watchkeeper) — human sign-off.

title: Task executor swallows errors on ALL task types - defect-#6 fix covered only the supply call (2 blind failures today)
description: Evidence: (1) defect-#6 era: 4 construction supply failures with zero error detail at any log level - fixed for that call site only (verbatim error logging landed there). (2) 05:35:38 today: COLLECT_SELL fail on T7 (phantom-cargo resume branch, L47 family) - again just 'ERROR: Manufacturing task failed', no underlying error; diagnosis required manual ship refresh + inference. Ask: wrap the task-runner's error path generically - log task type, step, ship, and the underlying error verbatim for EVERY task failure. One wrap at the executor dispatch level covers all current and future task types. Acceptance: any manufacturing/construction task failure logs the root error string.

title: coordinator logs failed workers as completed at INFO; ContractsCompleted counts failures
description: Follow-up from sp-okwk (fix-okwk investigation, 2026-07-07). run_fleet_coordinator.go: all four worker-completion receive sites (:153, :174, :262, :338) log at INFO and never inspect WorkerCompletedEvent.Success/.Error (fields exist, navigation/events.go:10-11) — a crashed worker is logged as a completion; the respawn line (:403 'Starting worker container for %s') doesn't distinguish crash-respawn from new-task. ALSO: those sites do result.ContractsCompleted++ even for failed workers (e.g. :340) — the completed-contracts metric over-counts. Fix: log event.Error at ERROR when !event.Success; label respawn reason; increment ContractsCompleted only on Success. Worker-side crash signature is already fixed by sp-okwk (recordCrash).

title: 3 specialist templates falsely promise 'watchkeeper respawns you' on rollover
description: shipwright/prompt.template.md:72-73, fleet-architect/prompt.template.md:76, and trade-analyst/prompt.template.md:76 each instruct the agent to 'gc handoff' on rollover and promise 'the watchkeeper respawns you.' The watchkeeper does NOT respawn specialists. It respawns ONLY the captain (ensureCaptainAlive -> SpawnSession(s.cfg.CaptainAgent), gobot/internal/captain/respawn.go:15,20) and spawns the surveyor on cadence (surveyor_nudge.go:26-31). For specialists it only reopens orphaned pipeline beads (respawn.go:28 requeueOrphanedPipelineBeads) - it never restarts a session. So a specialist that hands off mid-work is never reawoken; the work stalls silently until a human or a fresh consult re-invokes it. FIX: correct the three TEMPLATES to state real behavior (handoff bead persists; session is re-invoked manually / on next consult). TIER-3 rail (templates) - human sign-off required.

description: friction: (s87) `construction start` resume prints "Task Count: 0" from the in-memory object on EVERY resume (modelToPipeline never loads tasks, manufacturing_pipeline_repository.go:308-361) — the single readout that could distinguish a healthy resume from an empty-shell pipeline is hardwired misleading. title: friction: (s86) No pipeline TASK visibility: `construction status` prints only pipeline status/progress ("EXECUTING, 0.0
description: friction: (s86) No pipeline TASK visibility: `construction status` prints only pipeline status/progress ("EXECUTING, 0.0%") and there is no verb to list a pipeline's tasks with their states (PENDING/READY/ASSIGNED + gating reason). Diagnosing WHY the gate got zero worker-assignments for 5h required a code-read agent over the coordinator source. An `operations tasks <pipeline-id>` (or `construction status --tasks`) readout would make queue starvation observable in one command.

title: friction: (s86) No market-search-by-good verb: answering "where does good X trade in this system" (the Admiral's gas eva
description: friction: (s86) No market-search-by-good verb: answering "where does good X trade in this system" (the Admiral's gas evaluation, and locating an ADVANCED_CIRCUITRY producer) requires `market get` across all 29 cached markets one waypoint at a time. A `market find <GOOD> --system X1-PZ28` (scan the cached market table, print waypoints + prices + supply) would turn a ~30-command sweep into one read.

title: friction: (s85) `construction start` is a SILENT FOOTGUN: it creates the pipeline row (pipeline_type=CONSTRUCTION) + tas
description: friction: (s85) `construction start` is a SILENT FOOTGUN: it creates the pipeline row (pipeline_type=CONSTRUCTION) + tasks and returns "Status: PLANNING", but spawns NO coordinator container and returns NO container id — the pipeline sits INERT at 0.0% forever with nothing executing it. The executor is the MANUFACTURING coordinator, which adopts pipelines ONLY at startup recovery (`FindByStatus(PLANNING,EXECUTING)`, `run_parallel_manufacturing_coordinator.go` recoverState at ~line 200) and NEVER re-polls the table in its main loop. So a construction pipeline created AFTER the coordinator booted is never seen. The undocumented required sequence is: `construction start` FIRST, THEN bounce `operations --manufacturing` so recovery adopts it. Candidate fix: either (a) `construction start` should signal the running coordinator to re-scan (or spawn its own coordinator), or (b) the coordinator loop should periodically re-poll for new PLANNING pipelines, or at minimum (c) `construction start` should WARN "no coordinator running — start operations --manufacturing to execute this pipeline". Nothing about the success output hints the pipeline is inert.
close_reason: friction dup — resolved by sp-hkfb (construction start silent no-adoption footgun fixed). sp-tme0 sweep.

title: friction: (s84) Measuring manufacturing standalone $/h still requires manual ledger archaeology: `--type SELL_CARGO` cle
description: friction: (s84) Measuring manufacturing standalone $/h still requires manual ledger archaeology: `--type SELL_CARGO` cleanly isolates trading REVENUE (L56) but the BUY side (PURCHASE_CARGO) is shared with contract cargo sourcing, so per-stream NET is hand-paired by timestamp — and the ledger prints in UTC-3 while container logs are UTC, forcing a 3h offset correction to align a sell to its task. A `ledger list --category TRADING_REVENUE --by-ship` or a per-container P&L rollup (revenue − costs − fuel, tagged by the worker container that generated it) would make stream-level $/h a one-command read instead of a cross-timezone reconciliation.

title: friction: (s84) `construction status` shows the material BILL (FAB_MATS/ADVANCED_CIRCUITRY/QUANTUM_STABILIZERS remaining
description: friction: (s84) `construction status` shows the material BILL (FAB_MATS/ADVANCED_CIRCUITRY/QUANTUM_STABILIZERS remaining) but NO cost estimate and there is no `construction start --dry-run` — so I cannot pre-price the gate-build material spend (nor its hauler-cycle count) before `construction start` commits haulers and capital. Every other capital commit (shipyard purchase --budget) has a ceiling knob; the gate build has none. Candidate fix: a `construction start --dry-run` that reports estimated material cost + task count, and/or a `--budget` cap, so the biggest single mission spend isn't launched blind.

title: friction: (s81) No visibility into the contract coordinator's hauler-eligibility decision — I could not see WHY it ran o

Had to INFER that TORWIND-3's phantom 44/80 cargo cache benched it, confirm via a failed `ship sell` (server cargo=0), then `ship refresh`. A `coordinator status` / `contract eligible-ships` readout (which ships are in the pool, which are excluded and why: foreign cargo / assignment / distance) would turn a multi-step inference into one command — and this exact phantom-benched-hauler case is a silent $/h leak that emits no event.

title: friction: (s83) `operations status` and `operations stop --manufacturing` do NOT track a NORMALLY-launched manufacturing
description: friction: (s83) `operations status` and `operations stop --manufacturing` do NOT track a NORMALLY-launched manufacturing coordinator (no restart involved) — generalizes the s75 restart-storm item. Right after `operations start --manufacturing` succeeded and the coordinator + worker were RUNNING, `operations status` printed "No active manufacturing operations" and listed the coordinator under "Other Containers", and `operations stop --manufacturing` returned "No matching operations to stop". So the d-65 GUARD (stop the engine if it grabs the contract hauler) is non-functional as written — the only working stop is `container stop <coordinator-id>`. Candidate fix: `operations status`/`operations stop` should classify containers by type from the container DB (name prefix `parallel_manufacturing-` / recorded operation kind), not from in-memory session tracking, so a manufacturing op is stoppable/observable via its own verb whether or not a restart intervened.

title: friction: (s77) The startup AutoMigrate list (`../gobot internal/infrastructure/database/connection.go:86-100`) is a han
description: friction: (s77) The startup AutoMigrate list (`../gobot internal/infrastructure/database/connection.go:86-100`) is a hand-maintained enumeration of only 11 models and silently EXCLUDES the entire manufacturing subsystem (ManufacturingPipeline/Task/FactoryState/Dependency) plus gas/storage models. So every new persisted field on those models is a latent `column ... does not exist (SQLSTATE 42703)` that only fires on the first live persist — this is the THIRD such missing-migration (s73 ships.reserved, s77 manufacturing_pipelines.sequence_number, and the ce10b92 fix only patched the 11 listed models). Candidate: register ALL persistence models in AutoMigrate (or add a CI schema-drift check diffing GORM struct tags against the applied schema) so this class stops recurring one column at a time.
close_reason: friction dup — resolved by sp-tvcm (AutoMigrate/schema-drift gate); column-level follow-up tracked separately in sp-s0mw. sp-tme0 sweep.

title: friction: (s77) RESOLUTION of the s76 item above — the "untracked report is invisible to the pipeline" fear was a FALSE
description: friction: (s77) RESOLUTION of the s76 item above — the "untracked report is invisible to the pipeline" fear was a FALSE ALARM. The d-83 report merged (`status: merged`, fix commit 3e5dc4f on main) THIS session despite still showing `??` untracked in the local working tree. So the fix pipeline DOES reach a report that the local `git status` calls untracked (it works from its own worktree/source, not the Captain's uncommitted local copy). The s76 candidate fixes are therefore lower priority than I flagged — untracked-locally ≠ stranded. Keep the "surface uncommitted status:new report" idea only as a nice-to-have observability aid, not a blocker fix.

title: friction: (s76) A FILED fix report can sit UNTRACKED in git and never reach the fix pipeline. The d-83 report (mission's
description: friction: (s76) A FILED fix report can sit UNTRACKED in git and never reach the fix pipeline. The d-83 report (mission's sole blocker) is `status:new` but shows `??` in `git status` — the fix pipeline builds from git worktrees (committed files only), so an uncommitted report is invisible to it. All of s75's `state/*` output is likewise uncommitted, so it may just await the normal state-committer, BUT the Captain has NO git access (CLI-only actuator) and cannot commit it, and the situation-report gives no signal that a `status:new` report is uncommitted. Candidate fixes: (a) report-filing auto-stages/commits the report file; (b) the situation-report surfaces "uncommitted status:new report exists"; (c) confirm the captain-state committer stages `reports/` and not just `state/`. Without one of these a filed blocker can silently cost sessions.
close_reason: resolved (false alarm) — the concern (filed fix report sits untracked, never reaches pipeline) was investigated and found false per sp-1fc3. sp-tme0 sweep.

title: friction: (s75) `operations stop --manufacturing` FAILED to stop a restart-looping manufacturing coordinator ("no matchi
description: friction: (s75) `operations stop --manufacturing` FAILED to stop a restart-looping manufacturing coordinator ("no matching operations") because it lost its operation tracking across the daemon restarts; only `container stop <container-id>` worked (it persists STATUS=STOPPED to the DB, which recovery honors). Candidate fix: `operations stop` should reconcile target operations from the container DB (the same source recovery uses), not from in-memory/session tracking that a restart wipes — otherwise the one command meant to halt a runaway operation is exactly the one that fails during a restart storm.
close_reason: friction dup — resolved by sp-dpsq (operations status/stop made mfg-aware). era-1 friction migration, verified landed 2026-07-08 (sp-tme0 sweep).

title: friction: (s75) The contract coordinator's select-closest bought PRECIOUS_STONES FRESH on empty TORWIND-4 (distance 714)
description: friction: (s75) The contract coordinator's select-closest bought PRECIOUS_STONES FRESH on empty TORWIND-4 (distance 714) while TORWIND-3 sat idle already holding 65 units of exactly that good (the contract's required good) — selection is position-only and ignores existing matching cargo, so it re-buys goods a hauler already carries. Candidate fix: bias ship selection toward a hauler already carrying the required contract good (or at least skip the re-buy leg when a held hauler can fulfill), cutting a full buy-leg's travel + cargo spend.

title: friction: (s75) THE FIX-PIPELINE GATE (build+tests) IS BLIND TO A COORDINATOR THAT CRASHES THE WHOLE DAEMON ON LAUNCH —
description: friction: (s75) THE FIX-PIPELINE GATE (build+tests) IS BLIND TO A COORDINATOR THAT CRASHES THE WHOLE DAEMON ON LAUNCH — same class as the s73 missing-migration blind spot. `operations start --manufacturing` panics the daemon in a restart loop because the parallel-manufacturing handler's `eventSubscriber`/`eventPublisher` are never wired in main.go (they're nil), and the command runs on a naked goroutine with no recover() (L53, report d-83). Tests pass; a live launch takes down the process. CANDIDATE FIXES: (a) a gate smoke-test that actually launches EACH coordinator type against a throwaway daemon and asserts it survives ≥1 iteration past state-recovery; (b) a top-level `recover()` in ContainerRunner.execute() so no single coordinator panic can ever kill the whole daemon (containment — turns a process-death into a container FAILED).

title: friction: (s73) THE FIX-PIPELINE GATE CANNOT CATCH A MISSING-MIGRATION REGRESSION. The reservation feature (d-79) merged
description: friction: (s73) THE FIX-PIPELINE GATE CANNOT CATCH A MISSING-MIGRATION REGRESSION. The reservation feature (d-79) merged with green tests yet took the WHOLE contract earner down in production: it added GORM struct tags (Reserved/ReservationReason on ShipModel) but no SQL migration, and the test DB AutoMigrates tags while production requires hand-applied migrations/*.sql that were never authored (SQLSTATE 42703 on every ship Save; L52). The build+test gate is structurally blind to this class. CANDIDATE FIX (high value — this outage cost hours of zero income): add a schema-drift check to the gate that compares GORM model tags against the applied `migrations/` DDL (or runs the production migration path in CI) and FAILS a schema-touching feature that lacks its migration. Second-order gap: the Captain has no way to apply a DB migration (CLI-only actuator, no psql) — a migration fix requires an out-of-band operator step even after code merges, so schema fixes have a longer restore-latency than pure-code fixes; a `spacetraders admin migrate` verb (or auto-run migrations on daemon boot) would let the daemon self-repair schema drift on restart.
close_reason: friction dup — resolved by sp-tvcm (AutoMigrate/schema-drift gate); column-level follow-up tracked separately in sp-s0mw. sp-tme0 sweep.

title: friction: (s71) `goods produce <good>` has NO `--dry-run`, and `operations start --manufacturing --dry-run` echoes only
description: friction: (s71) `goods produce <good>` has NO `--dry-run`, and `operations start --manufacturing --dry-run` echoes only the coordinator SETTINGS (prefer-fabricate / min-price / workers / pipelines) — neither previews the acquisition plan (which ship it would claim, the buy-vs-fabricate tree for a specific good, or estimated cost). So the Admiral-ordered "study the fabrication economics before committing" is only partially answerable read-only; the real per-good plan requires a LIVE run + `container logs`, which for a hauler-hungry engine is not a safe dry study on a one-hauler fleet. Candidate fix: a `--dry-run`/`--plan` on `goods produce` (and a richer manufacturing dry-run) that prints the resolved dependency tree, buy-vs-fabricate decisions, ship count needed, and estimated credits — so the economics can be evaluated before actuation.

description: friction: (s67) The SAME missing ship-exclusion/reservation flag applies to `construction start` — it too auto-claims idle haulers with no `--ships`/`--exclude-ships`. Now that the discovery verb shipped and I've read the jump-gate bill (X1-PZ28-I67: FAB_MATS 1600 + ADVANCED_CIRCUITRY 400, fabricate-only), BOTH mission threads (gate-build + manufacturing) converge on the same need: a hauler pinned to the campaign without racing the contract earner (L46c). This elevates the reservation flag from a manufacturing nicety to the single binding constraint on BOTH mission horizons — a fleet-level ship-reservation (or `--exclude-ships`) shared by manufacturing + construction is now the top tooling ask. GOOD (resolved, note for meta-review): the waypoint/system-discovery verb ask is DONE — `waypoint list/get` shipped and works end-to-end; the jump-gate is now locatable and its construction status readable.

title: friction: (s58) `operations start --manufacturing` (and `goods produce`) exposes NO ship-exclusion/reservation flag — it
description: friction: (s58) `operations start --manufacturing` (and `goods produce`) exposes NO ship-exclusion/reservation flag — it auto-discovers idle haulers, unlike gas extraction which takes explicit `--siphons`/`--storage`. With a single productive hauler that is also the contract earner, this makes a clean *parallel* manufacturing experiment impossible: the engine will race the contract coordinator (L46c) for the same ship, and I can't fence it off. Candidate fix: a `--ships`/`--exclude-ships` flag (or a fleet-level ship-reservation) for manufacturing so a stream can be pinned to a dedicated hauler without stealing the contract earner. This is the actual gate on validating manufacturing $/h without corrupting the contract loop.

title: Contract split-trips: split one delivery's multi-trip quantity across N hulls (concurrency model change)
description: sp-snmb design (contract-floor 2026-07-09) — 4 verified blockers: (1) coordinator single-worker-per-PLAYER gate (run_fleet_coordinator.go ~184) forbids 2 workers on the same contract; (2) no reservation/claim primitive — concurrent workers double-purchase toward the same units-needed (delivery_executor.go:150); (3) contract persistence has NO concurrency control (no row-versioning/SELECT FOR UPDATE) -> lost updates; (4) qpmi terminal-delivery ambiguity under concurrent workers (which delivery zeroed remaining?). Decompose: (1) design a reservation/claim primitive (atomic UPDATE...WHERE remaining>=N); (2) make delivery-progress persistence concurrency-safe; (3) relax coordinator gate per-player->per-contract (keep restart-dup-prevention per-contract); (4) resolve qpmi terminal ambiguity. Seq: 1+2 before 3; 4 after 3.
notes: CAPTAIN DEFER (st-wisp-93mg): analyst data refutes the premise — all 380+ observed contracts fit a single 80-hold; split-trips has no live demand this era. Revisit only if contract sizes grow past one hold.

title: shipyard purchase --waypoint false-fails on empty cache instead of refreshing
description: Admiral-approved 2026-07-07. L49: 'shipyard purchase' with a pinned --waypoint FALSE-FAILS against an empty shipyard cache (reads stale/empty cache and reports failure instead of refreshing); the workaround folklore is 're-buy WITHOUT --waypoint so auto-discover visits and reads fresh'. FIX: on empty/stale cache for an explicitly pinned waypoint, refresh (visit/read) before failing — an explicit waypoint should be MORE reliable than auto-discovery, not less. RETIRES L49's re-buy clause on landing.
notes: session died
close_reason: merged 05df3e3. Real defect (agent correctly ignored my brief's case/trait speculation, diagnosed from bead): 'shipyard purchase --waypoint' false-failed on a FRESH shipyard. SpaceTraders /shipyard omits the priced 'ships' array until one of your ships is present at the waypoint (returns only 'shipTypes'); the batch handler read listings UP FRONT for budget math BEFORE the purchase loop navigates there -> FindListingByType miss -> hard abort 'ship type not available'. FIX: getShipPriceFromShipyard returns listingReady bool, discriminated via always-present shipTypes — HasShipType+empty-priced = empty cache -> DEFER (purchasableCount=Quantity, keep pinned waypoint so the loop visits+reads fresh, bounded by per-purchase credit guard); type absent from shipTypes = genuine error, fail fast. Symmetric with auto-discover, which dodged this by skipping the pre-read (the 're-buy WITHOUT --waypoint' folklore, L49). 3 TDD tests incl. over-defer guard, RED-proven, gates green. Daemon deploy BATCHED with sp-hs2j restart. Retires L49 re-buy clause — flag sp-4ek3.

notes: session died
close_reason: merged cbad21e. Removed the dead batch-contract --iterations flag end-to-end (Go layer: CLI flag/validation/echo, client param + req/resp fields, ignored daemon param + misleading TODO, gRPC pass-through). Evidence it was dead: container_ops_contract.go delegated to ContractWorkflow which hardcodes iterations:=1, so any N ran exactly ONE contract (matches L43). Help now points at 'contract start' for continuous operation (which already does it via the coordinator). Proto fields left in place deliberately (P4: regen/reservation disproportionate; proto3 default 0 was already ignored — zero wire/behavior change). scout-markets --iterations untouched (separate, genuinely wired). TDD RED-proven (flag-absent test), gates green. Behavior-identical removal -> daemon deploy BATCHED with sp-hs2j's restart; CLI picks up next build-cli. Retires L43 first clause — flag sp-4ek3.

title: Generalize event burst-grouping at emission (L23) — one incident, one event, not one per retry
description: Admiral-approved 2026-07-07. L23: the event feed emits MULTIPLE rows per failure (one retry burst) and consumers must hand-group by container_id+ts before treating rows as incidents. sp-okwk fixed this for container.crashed (one event per true death). GENERALIZE: burst-group at EMISSION for the remaining multi-row event types (workflow.failed retry bursts, transient API-error bursts) — group by container_id + time window, emit one event carrying a count. Keep raw rows in logs; the EVENT stream is the captain's attention budget. RETIRES L23 on landing.
notes: session died
close_reason: merged c5a655f. NOT a no9i dup (verified): no9i.detectCrashLoops is CONSUME-side, counts container.crashed rows into a crashloop interrupt; kb61 is EMISSION-side, suppresses repeated workflow.failed for the same (type,ship) within 15m via the shared HasSince cooldown (BurstGroupingRecorder decorator wired at the daemon root over captainEventRepo, burst set {workflow.failed}). container.crashed DELIBERATELY EXCLUDED (grouping it would starve detectCrashLoops — locked by TestBurstGroupingLeavesContainerCrashedUngrouped). Closes a real session-burn hole: every death emits BOTH container.crashed AND interrupt-class workflow.failed; no9i demoted crashed but left workflow.failed, so a crash loop still burned a session/death via workflow.failed — now one incident = one wake. FAIL-OPEN on cooldown-check error (one-shot emission must never silently drop). Keyed on ship not container_id (respawn assigns new container_id/reuses ship — ship-keying collapses the respawn storm container_id would miss). 5 TDD tests RED-proven, gates green. Daemon deploy BATCHED with sp-hs2j restart. Retires L23 (multi-row-per-failure grouping) — flag sp-4ek3/surveyor. Follow-ups noted (not filed to avoid parallel-bd conflict): config-tunable window (mirrors sp-d3xi), optional in-payload count (needs update-or-insert store method), api.error burst when that event type lands.

Admiral lifted the Tier-3 freeze 2026-07-07 and delegated to the shipwright. 'human' label removed — human sign-off is GIVEN; bead is now active shipwright work. Fix still edits templates/watchkeeper, but the safety-rail approval the engine cannot self-grant has been granted by the Admiral.

title: CLI: manual cargo purchase verb missing - 'ship buy' (or market buy) blocks captain-ordered trade circuits
description: Need: buy cargo with a docked ship outside the workflow engines. Blocked TODAY (2026-07-07, sp-zc3j): trade-analyst's zero-capex mfg shakedown (TORWIND-1 circuit D40 plating + D39 parts -> A2/C37/H50 imports, ~58k working capital) cannot execute - ship has sell/dock/navigate/refuel but NO buy; market has only read verbs. Same gap will block manual gate-material purchases (FAB_MATS/ADV_CIRCUITRY direct buys) during the construction shakedown. Acceptance: 'spacetraders ship buy --ship <sym> --good <G> --units <n>' buys from the market at the ship's docked waypoint; validates cargo capacity + market availability; records PURCHASE_CARGO ledger transaction with balance; errors clearly when not docked / good not traded / insufficient credits. Mirror of existing ship sell.
notes:
Deprioritized P2->P4 by captain (2026-07-07 ~02:00Z) after Admiral challenge on need. Honest re-read: the wedge this unblocks decays in value once the flip gate opens (~1.5h); slippage is partially derivable from market history; manual trade circuits scale poorly with a wake-based captain; and the manual-fallback use case (gate materials) only matters at construction-shakedown time. KEEP the bead - the verb is legitimately useful and tiny - but it must not compete for shipwright bandwidth with sp-c2bc (live ~6-crash/hr tax on the earner, replicates per hull) or sp-900v (treasury observability). Shipwright order: sp-c2bc > sp-900v > this.
shipwright: claimed for parallel build (Admiral order: no sequencing). Worktree ../captain-worktrees/sp-71bj, branch shipwright/sp-71bj, dedicated ultracode workflow.
session died
gate JSON: {"GatePassed":true,"Stale":false,"Merged":true}. Branch commit 1227ea8 -> squash 7dfd940. 3 files, +464: cli/ship.go verb (mirror of sell, reuses existing PurchaseCargo application command), cli/ship_buy_test.go, purchase_cargo_test.go (+264 lines on the reused command). Agent-committed issues.jsonl contamination stripped pre-gate (vector noted on sp-ezar). Feature cap: 1 of 2 today.
